## L2 — TCD (34 records)

### ✅ [TCD] BDF and DID Assignments for THC (HID-SPI / HID-I2C)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458198 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Verify and ensure the
>      Bus/Device/Function (BDF) assignment are done correctly for both THC0 and
>      THC1 ports.
>
>   - Verify that Vendor ID and
>      Device ID are configured correctly for both THC0 and THC1 ports operating
>      in HID-I2C/HIDSPI modes.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> Both
> THC0 and THC1 ports/instances have their own respective dedicated BDF and
> Vendor/Device ID values in each and every products/projects.
>
> These
> hardcoded BDF and Vendor/Device ID values for both THC instances can be
> verified in both UEFI Shell boot, as well as in WinOS boot via Windows' Device
> Manager. 
>
> Vendor ID will be a
> constant value of 8086 (which represent Intel Vendor ID).
>
> E.g. Using NVL-PCH-S
> / NVP-S as example for this instance: 
>
> According to Chap06_NVP-S_RegMaps, the following table
> listed the PCI global device IDs and BDF for NVL-PCH-S / NVP-S :
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for
> more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP boots to UEFI shell with
>      all hardware and BIOS configuration requirements met.
>
>   - Print out complete
>      list of PCI device, look for "Digitizer
>      Pen" device name from the print-out PCI device list.
>
>   - Check the BDF,
>      Vendor ID and Device ID for each THC0/THC1 port with HID-I2C/SPI
>      interface.
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirements: 
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to UEFI Shell or Ubuntu/Windows OS.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Both UEFI Shell and Windows' Device Manager able to
>      reflect the valid Vendor/Device ID values and mapped to respective BDF for
>      both THC0 and THC1 ports, as required according to HAS.
>
>   - In PythonSV, register
>      namednodes verification able to reflect the correct values as well. 
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above, i.e. incorrect/invalid BDF, Vendor or
>      Device ID. 
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI or WinOS
>      with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and/or THC1
>      ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panels
>       (e.g. BOM52 SPI/I2C, or BOM36/37)
>
>   - PythonSV (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>      connected on host system.
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Verify system correctly
>      enumerates THC device and reports the expected BDF, Vendor ID and Device
>      ID via PythonSV/EFI shell.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - THC device should expose
>      valid and correct PCI Vendor ID and Device ID during system enumeration.
>
>   - THC device is recognized by
>      the correct driver based on these IDs.

### ✅ [TCD] Cold Reset PM Cycling

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458213 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Ensure the SUT/RVP able to
>      perform and complete "N" iterations of Cold-Reset PM
>      cycling (via solar tool) successfully, with THC0 and/or THC1 ports
>      enabled as I2C/SPI-interface. 
>
>   - Verify that the touch
>      functionality of the touch panel still working normally after each CR
>      cycle completed. 
>
>   - Alternatively, both PIO and
>      DMA operations can be exercise to ensure that both THC port and the
>      connected TIC/touch device functionality remains operational and performs
>      as expected.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> Cold-Reset (CR) is a
> prevalent PM regression/cycling test in OSbV, CR testing involves resetting
> both THC0 and THC1, similar to a G3/Cold boot or Warm Reset exit.
>
> This test is
> conducted manually using Solar SW tool or automated via NGA framework—the
> specifics of which are delineated at the Test Case (TC) level.
>
> In the context of
> System-level Validation, this test will implicitly encompasses the verification
> of Fuses/Soft-straps loading, BIOS, SW Driver, THC Controller + TIC device
> initialization/enumeration sequences, and HID-SPI/I2C interface with PIO and
> DMA operations, covering all critical controller and system startup sequences.
>
>
>
> (Note: Details for CR/Cold-Boot flow are as shown
> below,  this information taken from IP-HAS  "G3 Exit
> Flow/THC HW Power-Up".)
>
>
>
> At the TC level,
> multiple distinct test configuration combinations are defined due to the
> SoC/PCH supporting two Touch Host Controller (THC) instances,
>
> each with different
> interfaces and protocols as per the THC-IP specifications:
>
>
>   - For single-port test mode:
>      Either THC0 or THC1 will be enabled as HID-SPI or HID-I2C.
>
>   - For dual-port test mode: Both
>      THC0 and THC1 will be enabled concurrently, include all the possible
>      combination of HID-SPI and/or HID-I2C.
>
> Here are a summary
> TC list of different test combinations/configurations for CR regression: 
>
>
>
>
> 15014447583
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455179
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014296898
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC0 (HID-I2C) Enabled only
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455180
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455151
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455150
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC0 (HID-SPI) Enabled only
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014447584
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled only
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455149
>
>    | 
>
>
> Cold Reset Cycles
>   with THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled only
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Boot RVP to Ubuntu OS, ensure HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
>
>   - Check the THC touch panel
>      status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no
>      yellow bang.
>
>   - Launch Solar tool from
>      terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
>
>   - In Solar, set up cold-reset
>      test with yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and
>      specify 'N' cycles (e.g. 100).
>
>   - Start cold-reset PM cycling
>      test; optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
>
>   - Wait for the test to complete
>      and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP
> schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Verify and
>      ensure there are no errors returned/encountered when targeted 'N' number
>      of Cold-Reset (CR) PM cycling count is achieved successfully on RVP. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0
>      still working normally after each CR cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable to
>      pass/complete the targeted CR cycling count with error code
>      returned, 
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices, 
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after CR. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>
>   - Other
>      IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level
>      components utilized in this test that may cause the CR cycle failed to
>      complete the targeted "N" Iterations of CR cycle.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - NVL RVP booted to UbuntuOS
>      with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and THC1
>      ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE touch panel support
>      HID-SPI/-I2C (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A or BOM37A) 
>
>   - SW Driver support
>      HID-SPI/I2C  (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C)
>
>   - Solar Tool
>
>   - (Optional) Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Monitor THC0/THC1 touch
>      controller status after each cold-reset cycle, including driver
>      functionality and error status (yellow-bang checks)
>
>   - Validate the touch
>      functionality and HID-SPI/I2C operations using both PIO and DMA tests
>      post-reset.
>
>   - Include negative scenarios
>      (e.g., force error conditions, run extended and rapid cycles) for stress
>      coverage.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Ensure the system is
>      repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each
>      cold-reset cycle.
>
>   - Solar tools and test scripts
>      must log all failures, errors, and device status; All logs must be
>      traceable.
>
>   - Device and driver error
>      states must be clearly reported in test results.

### ❌ [TCD] Global Interrupt Enable/Disable (1-->0-->1) Flow

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458202 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective: 
>
>
>   - This test is
>      to verify and ensure that the enable/disable global interrupt
>      mechanism able to function normally as expected.
>
>   - Expectation
>      is that global interrupt mechanism/feature will be fully integrated and
>      utilized by QuickSPI/QuickI2C SW Driver. 
>
>
>
>
>   - This test is
>      also designed to have implicit coverage for the LNL PCR below: 
>
>
>
>
>
> HSD Link
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> PCR Title
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Comments
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 22013202729
>
>    | 
>
>
> Improve THC
>   support for burst mode (burst of incoming reports)
>
>    | 
>
>
> On
>   LNL+, THC HW will support global interrupt disable/enable bit used for SW
>   interrupt re-evaluation and handling during burst mode (i.e. multiple input
>   reports before SW can response). 
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
> Overview: 
>
> From LNL and forward, THC-IP will
> have a global interrupt mechanism supported and it is controlled
> via GBL_INT_EN disable/enable bit. 
>
> The main goal for having this
> mechanism/feature is to simplify driver's interrupt ISR which avoid the requiring the SW driver to fully
> understand the HW interrupt generation RTL to get the handling right.
>
>
>   - This
>      mechanism will come-in very handy when SW driver is dealing with burst
>      mode of multiple incoming/pending RX data frames/reports before driver
>      able to response.
>
>   - Following HW
>      change can eliminate SW workaround in interrupt handling (which is hard to
>      do complete code coverage).
>
> Previously, on TGL, there is one sighting on frame
> missed was due to SW interrupt re-evaluation race condition. (HSD#: 14011440628)
>
> THC Port Interrupt Enable Register  (THC_M_PRT_INT_EN)
>
>
>
>
> Bits
>
>    | 
>
>
> Access Type
>
>    | 
>
>
> Default
>
>    | 
>
>
> Description
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 31
>
>    | 
>
>
> RW
>
>    | 
>
>
> 1'b1
>
>    | 
>
>
> Enable
>   THC Interrupt (GBL_INT_EN) 
>
>
> b0:
>   Disable THC Interrupt globally.
>
>
> b1:
>   Enable THC Interrupt globally.
>
>
> When GBL_INT_EN
>   bit is 1 (enabled), THC will send any interrupt (MSI or legacy line
>   interrupt) to SW.
>
>
> When GBL_INT_EN
>   bit is 0 (disabled),  THC does not send any MSI/Line interrupt.
>
>
> [Note:
>   When changing from 0->1, THC shall check all pending interrupt sources
>   (NDDI_STS, EOF_INT_STS, error interrupts etc.)
>
>
> and
>   generate a new SW interrupt if there is any pending interrupt source.]
>
>    | 
>
>
> [More details will be added/covered in TC-level.]
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for more
> information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS/WinOS
>      with all the HW/SW requirements met. 
>
>   - On SUT/RVP, enable and setup the driver
>      ETL tracing to capture the input reports/packets on THC0/THC1 port.
>
>   - On Host, use ITH tool to perform ODLA
>      or VISA2PIN tracing to capture the two internal-IP VISA signals.
>
>   - Perform test and verify the following
>      scenarios:
>
> Scenario A: When GBL_INT_EN bit is 1
> (Enabled by default)
>
> (a) In PythonSV, enable global interrupt feature on THC0
> port and read back this register to verify the configured value.
>
> (b) Verify and validate the expected outcome:
>
>
>   - When touch event occurredTHC HW will send any MSI/Legacy
>      line interrupt to SW when touch event occurred.
>
>   - Observed internal signal toggling/assertion on "ilb_parb_cavail"
>
> Scenario B: When GBL_INT_EN bit
> is 0 (Disabled)
>
> (a) In PythonSV, disable global interrupt feature on
> THC0 port and read back this register to verify the configured value.
>
> (b) Verify and validate the expected
> outcome: 
>
>
>   - When touch event occurred (i.e. THC received input
>      report from TiC), THC HW will not send any MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW
>      when touch event occurred.
>
>
>   - No
>      signal toggling/assertion observed on "ilb_parb_cavail" 
>
> Scenario C: When GBL_INT_EN bit
> transition from 0->1
>
> (a) Attempt test scenario B first. And, then
> follow-up by test scenario A. 
>
> (b) Verify and validate the expected
> outcome: 
>
>
>   - THC HW shall check all pending interrupt sources prior
>      to GBL_INT_EN bit enablement.
>
>   - After global interrupt enabled, THC HW
>      will generate a new MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW if there is any
>      pending interrupt source. 
>
>
>   - Repeat same steps #2 to #4 for the other test scenarios
>      above. 
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - Acute logic analyzer to
>      monitor the HID-SPI/I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - Optional: Custom rework
>      required to wire-out all the THC-SPI/I2C IO pins for Acute LA signals
>      tracing. (Note: Make sure to use single-core wires and keep the
>      length as short as possible to avoid/minimize any signal integrity
>      impact.)
>
>   - VISA-to-pin setup + Acute LA
>      to analyze the HID-SPI/-I2C traffic together with the THC-IP VISA signals.
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Enable/disable
>      global interrupt feature on THC0 port able to work and function as
>      expected.
>
>   - QuickSPI/QuickI2C
>      SW Driver is used to implicit cover the enable/disable global
>      interrupt flow.
>
>
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above. 
>
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both
>      THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR
>      touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A/37A, BOM52)
>
>   - SW Driver -
>      QuickSPI (for HIDSPI), QuickI2C (for HIDI2C)
>
>   - PythonSV on
>      host system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>   - (optional)
>      Intel TraceHub Tool (ODLA/visa-to-pin) 
>
>   - Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope for SPI/I2C traffic analysis
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Confirms
>      consistent global interrupt behaviour across all THC ports supported by
>      platform.
>
>   - Validate
>      that the interrupt flow, from HW to OS/driver, is functioning:
>
>
>   - THC0/THC1
>       ETL traces capture input reports/packets.
>
>   - ODLA or
>       VISA2PIN traces reflect internal IP signalling behaviour.
>
>
>
>
> Dfx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Ability to
>      read back GBL_INT_EN bit/interrupt status bit in PythonSV.
>
>   - PythonSV
>      must allow set/clear global interrupt enable, clear pending interrupts,
>      and optional software inject/force-pending for validation.

---

### ✅ [TCD] HIDI2C POR Touch Device Enabling+Functional on THC0/THC1 (OSBV) using MSI Interrupt

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458178 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Verify and
>      ensure that both POR touch panels (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37, BOM52, etc)
>      are enabled and working with production SW driver installed in Ubuntu OS,
>      using current project's platform for both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
> Overview:
>
> In Post-Si Environment, POR touch
> panels from 3PE will be used for end-to-end validation and covered most of the
> THC's IP/domain related test cases on RVP with production SW driver
> installed. 
>
> Therefore, it is important to
> ensure the touch panels are enabled and able to function during the early phase
> of the PO. 
>
> [Test
> details will be added in TC-level.]
>
>
>
> References:
> Refer to THC HAS
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Enable THC0/THC1 port in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully boot OS with SW/HW requirements met.
>
>   - Verify NO
>      yellow-bank occur and perform different touch gestures to verify touch
>      functionality of device.
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - OS with feature rework to
>      enable I2C/SPI interface on THC port
>
>   - Touch panel with touch cable
>      connected on RVP via THC port
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Touch
>      functionalities of touch panel are fully operational, all touch
>      gestures/operations perform as anticipated 
>
>   - NO
>      errors/issues and yellow-bank warning occurred on THC port's I2C/SPI
>      device/driver end during validation process   
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC port - QuickI2C/SPI device/driver.
>
>   - Touch panel not
>      functional (gesture fail).
>
>   - HID-I2C/SPI
>      communication errors (PIO/DMA failures).
>
>   - SUT hang,
>      unresponsive, or hit into errors triggered by the touch events.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0
>      and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR
>      touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37, BOM52, etc)
>
>   - SW Driver -
>      QuickSPI (for HIDSPI), QuickI2C (for HIDI2C)
>
>   - PythonSV
>      (via USB2DBC or XDP) conn
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Functional
>      coverage via gesture validation
>
>
>
> DFx requirements:
>
>
>   - Utilize the TraceView tool to
>      capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of
>      HID-I2C/SPI data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.

### ✅ [TCD] HIDI2C POR Touch Device Enabling+Functional on THC0/THC1 (Pre-OS) using BIOS on-screen VKB support + Legacy Interrupt (Non-MSI) Mode

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458175 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Verify and
>      ensure touch functionalities are enabled and working in Pre-Ubuntu OS
>      environment (i.e. UEFI shell, BIOS Menu) with virtual-keyboard (VKB)
>      feature enabled in BIOS, using touch panels on current project's platform
>      for both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>
> Overview:
> Pre-OS touch operations are supported and used by CCG
> teams, and this feature will be visible to the end-user as well. In order to
> enable Pre-OS touch operations, BIOS will need to include an UEFI driver
> that mimics the THC Host Driver component which used in the Ubuntu OS
> environment.
> (Note: This UEFI driver to support
> basic touch operation may be just be a simplify/lite version of Ubuntu OS
> driver)
> Soft keyboard (or, a.k.a Virtual Keyboard - VKB)
> support is built on top of BIOS touch support. VKB will become visible on touch
> eDP display, once it is enabled in the BIOS setting and took effect after
> system rebooted.
> (i.e. Boot Maintenance Manager Menu -> Boot
> Configuration Menu -> Enable Virtual Keyboard)
> [Test details will
> be added at TC-level.]
>
> References:
> Refer to THC HAS
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP successfully boot to BIOS Setup Menu and enable
>      THC0/THC1 port in BIOS settings.
>
>   - After SUT
>      rebooted to BIOS Menu, virtual keyboard should appear.
>
>   - Verify NO
>      error/issue occur and perform touch gestures with VKB to verify touch
>      functionality of device.
>
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP with feature rework to
>      enable I2C/SPI interface on THC port
>
>   - Touch panel with touch cable
>      connected on RVP via THC port
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Touch functionalities of
>      touch panel are fully operational with the on-screen displayed virtual
>      keyboard
>
>   - All keys on the virtual
>      keyboard respond correctly as expected to user's touch gestures
>
>   - NO errors/issues occurred on
>      THC port's I2C/SPI device/driver end during validation process
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Virtual
>      keyboard failed to display on-screen
>
>   - Touch panel
>      not functional (gesture fail) and not working with VKB available on-screen
>
>   - HID-I2C/SPI
>      communication errors (PIO/DMA failures)
>
>   - SUT hang,
>      unresponsive, or hit into errors triggered by the touch events
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0
>      and/or THC1 ports
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports
>
>   - 3PE POR
>      touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52, etc)
>
>   - SW Driver -
>      QuickSPI (for HIDSPI), QuickI2C (for HIDI2C)
>
>   - PythonSV
>      (via USB2DBC or XDP) connected on host system
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Functional
>      coverage via gesture validation
>
>
> DFx requirements:
>
>
>   - Utilize the Logic Analyzer
>      tool to capture the transaction, enabling detailed analysis of
>      HID-I2C/SPI data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies

---

### ❌ [TCD] Hyper-V and VT-d Flow with THC Enabled (IOMMU remapping MSI Interrupt)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458203 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Enabling the
>      Hyper-V(VSM) and VT-d might cause the THC not functioning. This test is
>      required to ensure THC and touch functionality is still working after
>      enabling these features.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> In previous project prior to ADL/MTL, THC sends out
> MSI(s) with incorrect BDF (e.g. Bus=1, Device=5, Function 0) instead of Bus=0.
>
> Device and Function should come from the following IP
> top level parameter straps THC_PCI_DEV_NUM and THC_PCI_FUNC_NUM. 
>
> Due to this when platform enables Hyper V
> virtualization feature, IOMMU remapping discards the MSI as IOMMU Bus virtualization feature unable
> to handle the MSI interrupts due to wrong BDF number which is very critical
> feature. 
>
> Thus, the MSI never makes it to CPU/Host SW (i.e. THC
> driver) and touch functionality doesn't work and yellow-bang encountered on THC
> drivers.   
>
>
>
> Issue Reported on LKF:  https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article/#/1607357444 
>
> RTL Bug Fix on ADL onwards:   https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article/#/14010397508 
>
>
>
> Bug disposition for different
> projects:
>
> [Legends: Fix (Fix), NF (No Fix.), NC (No
> Clone), NA (Not Applicable), Op (Opportunistic)]
>
>
>
>
> _internal
>
>    | 
>
>
> MTL
>
>    | 
>
>
> MCC
>
>    | 
>
>
> ADP-S
>
>    | 
>
>
> TGPH
>
>    | 
>
>
> TGPLP
>
>    | 
>
>
> TGPLP-R
>
>    | 
>
>
> LKF1B
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Fix
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix
>
>    | 
>
>
> Op
>
>    | 
>
>
> Op
>
>    | 
>
>
> Op
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> #
>
>    | 
>
>
> SIP Bug Board questionnaire
>
>    | 
>
>
> Answers
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 1
>
>    | 
>
>
> Source
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 2
>
>    | 
>
>
> Summary
>
>    | 
>
>
> THC sends out MSIs
>   with incorrect BDF (Bus=1, Device=5, Function 0) instead of Bus=0, Device and
>   Function should come from the following IP top level parameter straps  
>   THC_PCI_DEV_NUM and THC_PCI_FUNC_NUM. 
>
>
> Due to this when
>   platform enables Hyper V virtualization feature, IOMMU remapping discards the
>   MSI, which carries incorrect BDF info. The MSI never makes it to the THC
>   driver, and and touch functionality doesn't work.   
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 3
>
>    | 
>
>
> Impact of not fixing
>
>    | 
>
>
> For the IOMMU Bus
>   virtualization feature unable to handle the MSI interrupts due to wrong Bus
>   number which is very critical feature.
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 4
>
>    | 
>
>
> SIP Recommendation
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 5
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix type? (PRQ gating/important/opportunistic)
>
>    | 
>
>
> Important
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
> * Why has post si/FPGA never found?
>
>    | 
>
>
> Found by Post
>   Silicon team
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
> * What is likelihood/MTBF end customer would
>   trigger this?
>
>    | 
>
>
> High with bus
>   virtualization feature enabled.
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
> * Are these real corner case scenarios?
>
>    | 
>
>
> No
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
> * For recent projects (Done in FM), What was the
>   miss (Val/RTL reviews, HAS change etc..)?
>
>    | 
>
>
> Missing checks for
>   the requestor ID field for upstream transactions
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 6
>
>    | 
>
>
> Workaround options
>
>    | 
>
>
> Line interrupt
>   workaround is available but it has another RTL issue.
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 7
>
>    | 
>
>
> ECOability (Impact on gates, Severity)
>
>    | 
>
>
> Yes
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 8
>
>    | 
>
>
> Errata
>
>    | 
>
>
> N-A
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 9
>
>    | 
>
>
> Why found now?
>
>    | 
>
>
> Bus virtualization
>   feature is enabled recently
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 10
>
>    | 
>
>
> RTL Fix description
>
>    | 
>
>
> Fix the ReqID
>   programming in the outgoing MSI transaction with Bus device and function
>   number
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 11
>
>    | 
>
>
> Validation plan description
>
>    | 
>
>
> Add new checks for
>   all outgoing transaction reqID and cmpltrID and compare against Bus Device
>   and Function number of the THC.
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 12
>
>    | 
>
>
> RTL post-mortem required?
>
>    | 
>
>
> Yes
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 13
>
>    | 
>
>
> Validation post-mortem required?
>
>    | 
>
>
> Yes
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> For more information, refer to the THC HAS.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - SUT/RVP boot into BIOS menu with
>      all the HW requirements met.
>
>   - Go to BIOS menu to enabled VT-d.
>
>   - For WinOS, go to Windows Feature to enabled Hyper-V.
>
>   - For Ubuntu OS, Hyper-V is not supported.
>
>   - Check the touch functionality of the touch panel
>      after enabling VT-d and Hyper-V with multiple touch gestures.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu/WinOS
>      with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0 port.   
>
>   - 3PE
>      POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on
>      RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Touch functionalities of the
>      touch panel are fully operational and validated after VT-d and Hyper-V are
>      enabled.
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both
>      THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-I2C/-SPI on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - FTH trace
>      via Intel TraceHub tool to capture MSI upstream cycle from THC-IP
>
>   - 3PE POR
>      touch panel  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A)
>
>   - SW Driver -
>      QuickI2C (for HIDI2C), QuickSPI (for HIDSPI)
>
>   - PythonSV
>      connected to host system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>   - Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope for SPI/I2C traffic analysis 
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Multiple
>      touch gestures (e.g., tap, swipe, multi-touch) are performed and virtual
>      keyboard (VKB) is enabled to ensure comprehensive validation of touch
>      input capabilities.
>
>   - Includes
>      validation that no errors, warnings, or loss of touch functionality occur
>      after enabling VT-d and Hyper-V.
>
>   - System logs,
>      driver status, and event traces are collected during the test to confirm
>      that all relevant code paths and hardware states are exercised.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Ensure that
>      platform provides access to relevant debug hooks for the touch panel
>      interface (e.g., register read/write access).
>
>   - Confirm the
>      availability of validation registers that can be used to observe the state
>      of VT-d and Hyper-V enablement.
>
>   - If possible,
>      enable VISA tracing or equivalent hardware event tracing to capture touch
>      panel activity during test.

---

### ✅ [TCD] Interrupt Line Quiescing Mode Enabled/Disabled with Back-to-Back Device Interrupt

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458204 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective: 
>
>
>   - This test is to verify and
>      ensure that the device interrupt quiescing mechanism/feature in THC-IP
>      able to function as expected according to the details in overview description
>      in below.
>
>
>
> Overview: 
>
> THC-IP
> does provide device interrupt quiescing capability which can be controlled by
> SW setting the THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN register bit to temporarily ignore the
> touch device interrupt.
>
> When
> this bit is set, THC shall complete servicing:
>
>
>   - Current touch micro-frame if THC is receiving ongoing
>      touch data, or
>
>   - Current non-touch data device interrupt and send the
>      interrupt/MSI to SW.
>
> And,
> then ignore any new TIC's interrupt (including touch and non-touch interrupt),
> until THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN is cleared by SW later.
>
> When
> SW writes 1 to this bit, THC shall keep the bit read as 0 before it completes
> the current micro-frame/interrupt and then set it to 1.
>
> THC
> shall not drop any pending interrupt asserted during the quiesce flow. SW
> shall poll this bit to be 1 before it can clear it again.
>
> After
> THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN is cleared by SW, THC shall start service device
> interrupt as usual.
>
> (Note: This bit does not impact the activities on
> TXDMA and PIO operations.)
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer
> to THC HAS for more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI shell/Ubuntu OS
>      with all the HW/SW requirements met and configure all the BIOS settings
>      accordingly.
>
>   - In PythonSV, confirm quiesce registers (THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN
>      and THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS) are cleared. If not, set them
>      to 0.
>
>   - Enable quiescing mode by setting THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN
>      to 1 and verify both THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN and THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS
>      are 1.
>
>   - Perform PIO or TxDMA operations to verify normal function
>      during quiesce.
>
>   - Trigger a touch device interrupt and verify no RxDMA frames
>      are observed (interrupt is ignored).
>
>   - Disable the quiescing mode by setting THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN
>      to 0 and verify THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS is cleared.
>
>   - Verify RxDMA and touch functionality resume normal
>      operation.
>
>   - Repeat steps #2 to #7 for both THC-I2C and THC-SPI
>      interfaces.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI shell/Ubuntu OS/WinOS with feature
>      rework to enable SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
>
>   - PythonSV connected to host
>      system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>
>   - Acute logic analyzer to
>      monitor the HID-SPI/HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Touch device interrupt quiescing mechanism function as
>      expected on THC0 port.
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above. 
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu/WinOS
>      with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface for both THC0 and/or THC1
>      ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-I2C/-SPI on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A)
>
>   - SW Driver - QuickI2C (for
>      HIDI2C) , QuickSPI (for HIDSPI)
>
>   - PythonSV connected to host
>      system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>   - (optional) Intel TraceHub
>      Tool with VISA-to-pin setup 
>
>   - Logic Analyze/Oscilloscope
>      for SPI/I2C signal analysis 
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - System logs, driver status,
>      and event traces are collected during test to ensure that the touch event
>      matched the expected behaviours as follow:
>
>
>   - Touch device interrupt is
>       ignored when quiescing mode is enabled.
>
>   - Touch functionality return
>       to normal operation after quiescing mode is disabled.
>
>
>
>   - Both THC-I2C and THC-SPI
>      interfaces are validated, ensuring the quiescing mechanism is robust
>      across different hardware configurations.
>
>   - All relevant quiesce control
>      and status registers are validated via PythonSV, including the behaviour
>      of the quiesce enabled/HW status registers during quiesce. 
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Ensure
>      that platform provides access to relevant debug hooks for the touch panel
>      interface (e.g., register read/write access).
>
>   - Enable
>      LA capture to analyse touch panel activity during test.

---

### ✅ [TCD] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) (with WoT Disabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458216 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Ensure SUT/RVP able to
>      perform one or more complete S0ix PM cycling successfully with THC0 and/or
>      THC1 ports enabled + POR BOM Touch Panel connected on RVP (with WoT
>      function Disabled).
>
>   - Verify that the touch
>      functionality of the touch panel still working normally after each S0ix
>      entry/exit cycle completed. 
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> S0ix (e.g.
> s0i2.0/2.1/2.2) is one of the very common PM Cycling feature test in OSbV
> which is supported by the IP, and this test is carry out on a Post-Si
> System/RVP. (either by manual approach or using automation). 
>
> During the entry and
> exit of S0ix state, THC IP/Domain will need to undergo different power states
> transition (i.e. D0 -> D3 or D3 -> D0) and this will also involve
> the IP-to-PMC handshakes and the IP related functional blocks' behaviour/status
> changes. (Refer HAS for more info)
>
> S0ix
> Requirements and Entry/Exit Flow stated in IP HAS: https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html#s0i3 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> For
> PTL program, the POR S0ix state are as shown in the table below:  (Link
> -  https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_doc/PTL/HAS/Power/S0ix/S0ix.html#state-por)
>
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for
> more information.
>
> https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html  
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Manually enter sleep state (postcode=01C5), dump
>      residency counter script in PythonSV for sanity check (S0ix substate
>      counter will increase over time).
>
>   - Boot RVP to
>      Ubuntu OS, ensure
>      HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
>
>   - Check the THC touch panel
>      status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no
>      yellow bang.
>
>   - Launch Solar tool from
>      terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
>
>   - In Solar, set up S0ix test
>      with yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and specify
>      'N' cycles (e.g. 100).
>
>   - Start S0ix PM cycling test;
>      optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
>
>   - Wait for the test to complete
>      and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Using Solar
>      tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can
>      be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s)
>      returned/encountered. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0
>      still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable
>      to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code
>      returned, 
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices, 
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>
>   - Other
>      IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level
>      components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE touch panel support
>      HIDSPI  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A)
>
>   - SW Driver support HIDSPI
>      (i.e. QuickSPI)
>
>   - Solar Tool
>
>   - (Optional) Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Functional check of touch
>      panel device after each S0ix cycle, confirming no device/yellow-bang
>      errors.
>
>   - Validation of the system
>      ability to enter and maintain S0ix state and return to S0 state (active).
>
>   - Proper reporting and tracking
>      of S0ix substate residency using residency counters (verifying increment
>      over cycles).
>
>   - Includes negative scenarios
>      (e.g., force error conditions, run extended and rapid cycles) for stress
>      coverage.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Ensure the system
>      is repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each S0ix
>      cycle.
>
>   - Solar tools and
>      test scripts must log all failures, errors and device status, allowing
>      systematic tracking.
>
>   - Verify the
>      residency counters (PythonSV script) for S0ix states, ensuring power
>      management telemetry is exposed and correct.

---

### ✅ [TCD] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) (with WoT Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458217 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Ensure
>      SUT/RVP able to perform one or more complete S0ix PM cycling successfully
>      with THC0 and/or THC1 ports enabled + POR BOM Touch Panel connected on RVP
>      (with WoT function Enabled).
>
>   - Verify that
>      the touch functionality of the touch panel still working normally after
>      each S0ix entry/exit cycle completed. 
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> S0ix (e.g. s0i2.0/2.1/2.2) is one of the very
> common PM Cycling feature test in OSbV which is supported by the IP, and this
> test is carry out on a Post-Si System/RVP. (either by manual approach or using
> automation). 
>
> During the entry and exit of S0ix state, THC IP/Domain
> will need to undergo different power states transition (i.e. D0 -> D3 or D3
> -> D0) and this will also involved the IP-to-PMC handshakes and the IP
> related functional blocks' behavior/status changes. (Refer HAS for more info)
>
> S0ix Requirements and Entry/Exit Flow
> stated in IP HAS:   https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html#s0i3 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> For PTL program, the POR S0ix state are as
> shown in the table below:  (Link -  https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_doc/PTL/HAS/Power/S0ix/S0ix.html#state-por)
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for more information
>
> https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html  
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Manually enter sleep state (postcode=01C5), dump
>      residency counter script in PythonSV for sanity check (S0ix substate
>      counter will increase over time).
>
>   - Boot RVP to
>      Ubuntu OS, ensure
>      HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
>
>   - Check the THC touch panel
>      status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no
>      yellow bang.
>
>   - Launch Solar tool from
>      terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
>
>   - In Solar, set up S0ix test
>      with yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and specify
>      'N' cycles (e.g. 100).
>
>   - Start S0ix PM cycling test;
>      optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
>
>   - Wait for the test to complete
>      and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Using Solar
>      tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can
>      be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s)
>      returned/encountered. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C, QuickSPI).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C/SPI touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable
>      to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code
>      returned.
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC - QuickI2C/SPI device and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices.
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>
>   - Other
>      IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level
>      components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle.
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and
>      THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE touch
>      panel support HIDSPI  (i.e. BOM36A)
>
>   - SW Driver
>      support HIDSPI (i.e. QuickSPI)
>
>   - Solar Tool
>
>   - (Optional)
>      Logic Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Validate PCI device with THC
>      touch panel driver check (quickspi/i2c) and yellow-bang check.
>
>   - Solar logs with cycle counts
>      supports reproducibility, error analytics, and root cause investigation.
>
>   - Validation of the system
>      ability to enter and maintain S0ix state and return to S0 state (active).
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Solar tool must allow log
>      failures, errors, and device status, allowing systematic tracking.
>
>   - Residency of S0ix
>      substates checked via PythonSV provides critical power telemetry.
>
>   - Ensure the system
>      is repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each S0ix
>      cycle.

>

>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.

>      panel support HIDSPI/I2C  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A)

>      support HIDSPI/I2C (i.e. QuickSPI, QuickI2C)

>      substates checked via PythonSV provides critical power telemetry.
>
>   - Ensure the system
>      is repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each S0ix
>      cycle.

---

### ✅ [TCD] S0i2.1 / S0i2.2 PM Cycling (MS with Display OFF while THC in D3) (with WoT Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458219 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Ensure the SUT/RVP able to
>      perform one or more complete S0ix PM cycling successfully (via solar
>      tool), with THC0 and/or THC1 ports enabled (with WoT Enabled). 
>
>   - Verify that the touch
>      functionality of the touch panel still working normally after each S0ix
>      entry/exit cycle completed. 
>
>   - Alternatively, both PIO and
>      DMA operations can be exercise to ensure that both THC port and the
>      connected TIC/touch device functionality remains operational and performs
>      as expected.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> S0ix (e.g.
> s0i2.0/2.1/2.2) is one of the very common PM Cycling feature test in OSbV
> which is supported by the IP, and this test is carry out on a Post-Si
> System/RVP. (either by manual approach or using automation). 
>
> During the entry and
> exit of S0ix state, THC IP/Domain will need to undergo different power states
> transition (i.e. D0 -> D3 or D3 -> D0) and this will also involved
> the IP-to-PMC handshakes and the IP related functional blocks' behavior/status
> changes. (Refer HAS for more info)
>
> (Note: D0i3 is not supported in PTL.)
>
>
>
> S0ix IP Requirement and Communication for
> SoC/PCH:
>
>
>   - SoC initiates the SoC S0ix
>      flow, if the THC is already in D3 and power gated.
>
>   - This is the IP deepest
>      functional power state, and no device wake from THC-IP is supported
>      (during S0ix).
>
>
>
> Entry Sequence to SoC S0ix Power State from
> THC Controller D3 Power State:
>
> [Note: SIP transitions from D0 to D3 are described in
> sections above.]
>
>
>   - Driver: Driver prepares for D3 Entry by quiescing the
>      controller which will result in TSI mode, Link Clocks being un-requested
>      due to all ports placed into ULPS state.
>
>   - Run
>      the DMA pause flow (in section SW Pause/Stop DMA Flow)
>
>   - Disable
>      the Device interrupt.
>
>   - All
>      PIO cycles are completed and stopped.
>
>   - SIP:
>      PLL and Trunk clock gating
>
>   - Driver:
>      THC Driver is ready for D3 entry which will result in THC driver
>      transitioning the Power State of the controller from D0 to D3 through
>      writing to PCI PMCSR Power state register. 
>
>
>   - THC_pmc_d3 will be set at this time to indicate the
>       controller is in D3 state. 
>
>   - SoC
>       may use this as an indication for Controller internal power state.
>
>   - THC
>       Driver inherent behavior would be to make sure THC HW is IDLE, before
>       setting the D3 bit .
>
>
>
>   - THC
>      driver: Driver will clear the START bit and poll until the THC is
>      stopped. Add the boundary case when SW hit the D3 and interrupt is
>      running. Expectation is SW disable the device using DEVRST, then set D3.
>
>
>
> Exit Sequence from SoC S0ix Power State:
>
>
>   - S0ix Exit trigger conditions
>      can be SW initiated.
>
>   - SoC : SoC will trigger the
>      IP-inaccessible Exit Flow.
>
>   - PCI Driver will transition
>      the controller from D3 to D0 in the case where the IP was in D3. 
>
>   - THC driver will initialize
>      the controller and place it into the running state.
>
>   - SIP : The Link clocks will be
>      requested when activity on the links is required. (Note: There will be
>      corner case when THC is Power-Gated, before driver restore the
>      controller.)
>
> Reference - IP HAS
> Link:  https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html  
>
>
>
>
>
> For PTL program, the
> POR S0ix substates supported are S0i2.0, S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 (as shown in the
> table below). 
>
> (FYI. Link:   https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_doc/PTL/HAS/Power/S0ix/S0ix.html#state-por)
>
>
>
>
>

>
>
>
>   - "Custom" indicates the definition of the
>      substate is unique to the product and is not shared with legacy
>      generations ADP and prior.
>
>   - Note the intent is that every product defines its
>      substates according to that product's unique needs. Thus
>      "custom" states are the expectation and not the exception.
>
>
>
> At the TC level,
> multiple distinct test configuration combinations are defined due to the
> SoC/PCH supporting two Touch Host Controller (THC) instances,
>
> each with different
> interfaces and protocols as per the THC-IP specifications:
>
>
>   - For single-port test mode:
>      Either THC0 or THC1 will be enabled as HID-SPI or HID-I2C.
>
>   - For dual-port test mode: Both
>      THC0 and THC1 will be enabled concurrently, include all the possible
>      combination of HID-SPI and/or HID-I2C.
>
> Here are a summary
> TC list of different test combinations/configurations for S0ix PM
> regression: 
>
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014800291
>
>    | 
>
>
>   S0ix PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled (with WoT Enabled)
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014800292
>
>    | 
>
>
>   S0ix PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled (with WoT Enabled)
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014800293
>

>

>
>    | 
>
>
>

> 15014800295
>

>

>
>
>

>
>
>
>
> Reference:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for
> more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Manually enter sleep state (postcode=01C5), dump
>      residency counter script in PythonSV for sanity check (S0ix substate
>      counter will increase over time).
>
>   - Boot RVP to
>      Ubuntu OS, ensure
>      HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
>
>   - Check the THC touch panel
>      status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no
>      yellow bang.
>
>   - Launch Solar tool from
>      terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
>
>   - In Solar, set up S0ix test
>      with yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and specify
>      'N' cycles (e.g. 100).
>
>   - Start S0ix PM cycling test;
>      optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
>
>   - Wait for the test to complete
>      and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirement:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP
> schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Using Solar
>      tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can
>      be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s)
>      returned/encountered. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C, QuickSPI).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C/SPI touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable
>      to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code
>      returned, 
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC - QuickI2C/SPI device and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices, 
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>
>   - Other
>      IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level
>      components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu S with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE touch panel support
>      HID-SPI/-I2C (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A or BOM37A) 
>
>   - SW Driver support
>      HID-SPI/-I2C  (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C)
>
>   - Solar Tool
>
>   - (Optional) Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Validate PCI device with THC
>      touch panel driver check (quickspi/i2c) and yellow-bang check.
>
>   - Solar logs with cycle counts
>      supports reproducibility, error analytics, and root cause investigation.
>
>   - Validation of the system
>      ability to enter and maintain S0ix state and return to S0 state (active).
>
>
>
> DFx Requirement:
>
>
>   - Solar tool must allow log
>      failures, errors, and device status, allowing systematic tracking.
>
>   - Residency of S0ix
>      substates checked via PythonSV provides critical power telemetry.
>
>   - Ensure the system
>      is repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each S0ix
>      cycle.

---

### ✅ [TCD] S4 PM Cycling

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458221 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Ensure the SUT/RVP able to
>      perform one or more complete S4 PM cycling successfully (via solar tool),
>      with THC0 and/or THC1 ports enabled. 
>
>   - Verify that the touch
>      functionality of the touch panel still working normally after each S4
>      cycle completed. 
>
>   - Alternatively, both PIO and
>      DMA operations can be exercise to ensure that both THC port and the
>      connected TIC/touch device functionality remains operational and performs
>      as expected.
>
> Overview:
>
> S4 (or, commonly
> known as system Hibernate mode/state) is a prevalent PM regression/cycling test
> in OSbV.
>
> When SUT enters S4
> power state, it saves the contents of the RAM and system appears to be power
> off. Power consumption is reduced to the lowest level in S4 mode.
>
> The contents of the
> RAM, including all running applications and open documents, are written to a
> file on the hard disk. This file is known as the hibernation file.
>
> To resume from S4,
> the hibernation file saved on the disk is read back into RAM, and the system
> returns to the previous state as it was in just before entering S4.
>
> This process is
> typically slower than resuming from S3 because the data has to be read from the
> hard disk, but it is faster than performing a full system boot from G3/S5
> state.
>
>
>
> S4 power
> sequence/flow is supported and exercise by the THC-IP;
>
> As the IP will
> need to undergo different power states transition (i.e. Entry: D0 -> D3 or
> Exit: D3 -> D0) which initialized by SW Driver in WinOS.
>
> From the perspective
> of THC-IP, the Sx Exit sequence mirrors that of D3 Exit (i.e. D3 -> D0
> transition).
>
> This test is
> conducted using Solar SW tool on a RVP, either by using manual testing approach
> or an automated method via NGA framework—the specifics of which are delineated
> at the Test Case (TC) level.
>
> In the context of
> System-level Validation, this S4 test will implicitly encompasses the
> verification of BIOS/ SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC
> device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>
> It also includes the
> verification of HID-SPI or HID-I²C interfacing with PIO and DMA operations on
> dedicated THC port(s) under test in each and every S4 cycle.
>
> Additionally, this
> S4 test will have implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the
> IP-HAS, as detailed below.
>
>
>
> S3/4/5 and Deep
> S3/4/5 Flow:
>
> IP Requirement/Communication for SoC:
>
>
>   - SoC initiates a platform reset warn flow either through
>      IOSF SB or through sideband wire.
>
>   - If
>      the THC is already power gated, the Reset Warn flow will wake up the IP's
>      power gated domain to respond to the message.
>
>   - This
>      is the IP's deepest functional power state and no device wake is
>      supported.
>
>   - The
>      Deep Sx flows are the same as normal Sx flows from THC perspective.
>
>   - In
>      Sx flow, PMC does not do save and restore to THC. SW / BIOS will
>      reinitialize THC as in boot flow.
>
>
>
> Sequence for Entry into S3/S4/S5 Power State
> [From D3-Power Gated State]
>
>
>   - THC Driver prepares for D3 entry.
>
>   - SoC:
>      Platform reset warn flow is initiated by SoC through IOSF SB.   
>
>        -SoC: IOSF SB Message: ResetWARN as a request from SoC
>
>   - SIP:
>      The wake is due to a pending Reset Prep Message to indicate Reset Warn
>      request results in requesting for PGCB clock to enable the power up
>      sequence.
>
>   - SIP:
>      THC will trigger the IP-accessible entry
>
>   - SIP:
>      THC will complete the handshake either through IOSF SB or through sideband
>      wire.                 -SIP:
>      IOSF SB Message: RSACK as a Reset Warn request acknowledgement from SIP
>
>   - SIP:
>      Since the controller is still in D3 state, D3 Power Gating flow is
>      triggered as soon as the IDLE conditions are met again (usually as soon as
>      the Reset Warn Ack is returned).
>
>   - SIP:
>      THC will trigger the IP-accessible PG entry.
>
>   - SoC:
>      SBI reset and Primary resets are asserted.
>
>   - SoC:
>      PGCB Reset remains de-asserted.
>
>   - SIP:
>      Once the power off sequence is complete the PGCB will be un-requested.
>
>
>
> Sequence for Exit from S3/S4/S5 Power State
> [From D3]  (Note:
> The exit from Sx State is exactly as the flow described in section 7.4.4.3.)
>
>
>   - D0i3 Exit trigger conditions can only be SW initiated.
>
>   - SIP:
>      THC will trigger the IP-accessible PG exit flow
>
>   - SIP:
>      Once THC Controller PGD domain is powered up,                                                                
>                    -ANY MMIO cycle shall NOT be UR'ed
>      even if the IP state is still in D0i3. 
>
>     -ANY MMIO
> cycle shall be UR'ed if the IP state is still in D3.
>
>
>   - Driver: THC/PCI driver will transition the controller
>      from D0i3/D3 to D0.
>
>   - Driver:
>      THC driver will initialize the controller and place it into the initial
>      state.
>
>   - SIP:
>      The Link clocks will be requested when activity on the links is
>      required.                 
>
>        -Link clock requests will assert once the THC driver initiates a
>      Bus cycle.
>
>
>
> At the TC level,
> multiple distinct test configuration combinations are defined due to the
> SoC/PCH supporting two Touch Host Controller (THC) instances,
>
> each with different
> interfaces and protocols as per the THC-IP specifications:
>
>
>   - For single-port test mode:
>      Either THC0 or THC1 will be enabled as HID-SPI or HID-I2C.
>
>   - For dual-port test mode: Both
>      THC0 and THC1 will be enabled concurrently, include all the possible
>      combination of HID-SPI and/or HID-I2C.
>
> Here are a summary
> TC list of different test combinations/configurations for S4 PM
> regression: 
>
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455589
>
>    | 
>
>
>  S4 PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-SPI) Enabled only
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455590
>
>    | 
>
>
>  S4 PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455591
>
>    | 
>
>
>  S4 PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15014455592

>

>  S4 PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled
>

> 15014455595
>

>  S4 PM
>   Cycling with THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled only
>

>
>
> 15014455596
>
>    | 
>
>
>  S4 PM
>   Cycling with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for
> more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Boot RVP to Ubuntu OS, ensure HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
>
>   - Check the THC touch panel
>      status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no
>      yellow bang.
>
>   - Launch Solar tool from
>      terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
>
>   - In Solar, set up S4 test with
>      yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and specify 'N'
>      cycles (e.g. 100).
>
>   - Start S4 PM cycling test;
>      optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
>
>   - Wait for the test to complete
>      and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
>
>
> HW Requirement:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP
> schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Using Solar
>      tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S4 PM cycling count can be
>      achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C, QuickSPI).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C/SPI touch device connected on
>      THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S4 cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable to
>      pass/complete the targeted S4 cycling count with error code
>      returned, 
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC - QuickI2C/SPI device and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices, 
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S4. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>
>   - Other
>      IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level
>      components utilized in this test that may cause the S4 cycle failed to
>      complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S4 cycle.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE touch panel support
>      HID-SPI/-I2C (i.e. BOM36A or BOM37A)
>
>   - SW Driver support
>      HID-SPI/-I2C  (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C)
>
>   - Solar Tool
>
>   - (Optional) Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Automated and repeated entry
>      into and exit from S4 state, stressing full shutdown and restore cycles.
>
>   - Verifies the THC touch panel
>      PCI/ACPI enumeration and correct driver load after each S4 cycle, NO
>      yellow bang/device error.
>
>   - All outcomes are logged for
>      analysis, aiding root cause detection of S4-related bugs.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Solar tool must allow log
>      failures, errors, and device status, allowing systematic tracking.
>
>   - Ensure the system is
>      repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each S4
>      cycle.
>
>   - BIOS and platform
>      configuration are pre-validated to reduce variables and help localization
>      of failures.
