### ✅ [TCD] S5 PM Cycling

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458222 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> ﻿Objective:
> 	• Ensure the SUT/RVP able to perform one or more complete S5 PM cycling successfully (via solar tool), with THC0 and/or THC1 ports enabled. 
> 	• Verify that the touch functionality of the touch panel still working normally after each S5 cycle completed. 
> 	• Alternatively, both PIO and DMA operations can be exercise to ensure that both THC port and the connected TIC/touch device functionality remains operational and performs as expected.
>
> Overview:
> S5 (equivalent to system Shutdown state) is a prevalent PM regression/cycling test in OSbV. In the S5 state, the SUT has been completely shut down.
> This means that no sessions are active and no memory is being maintained. Additionally, no context is saved, and the system will require a complete boot sequence to return to a working state (S0). The main advantage of the S0 state is the conservation of energy while the system is not in use.
>
> Here are some characteristics of the S5 power state:
> 	• The system appears to be turned off.
> 	• The power supply (PSU) typically provides power only to the motherboard to detect the power button press to wake up or return to S0 state.
> 	• No hardware contexts are maintained.
> 	• The system consumes the minimum amount of power necessary to respond to a wake event.
> 	• Full system initialization is required after entering S0 from S5 state, which involves POST (Power-On Self Test) and booting the operating system from scratch.
> S5 power sequence/flow is supported and exercise by the THC-IP;
> As the IP will need to undergo different power states transition (i.e. Entry: D0 -> D3 or Exit: D3 -> D0) which initialized by SW Driver in WinOS.
> From the perspective of THC-IP, the Sx Exit sequence mirrors that of D3 Exit (i.e. D3 -> D0 transition).
> This test is conducted using Solar SW tool on a RVP, either by using manual testing approach or an automated method via NGA framework—the specifics of which are delineated at the Test Case (TC) level.
> In the context of System-level Validation, this S5 test will implicitly encompasses the verification of BIOS/ SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> It also includes the verification of HID-SPI or HID-I²C interfacing with PIO and DMA operations on dedicated THC port(s) under test in each and every S5 cycle.
> Additionally, this S5 test will have implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, as detailed below.
>
> S3/4/5 and Deep S3/4/5 Flow:
> IP Requirement/Communication for SoC:
> 	1. SoC initiates a platform reset warn flow either through IOSF SB or through sideband wire.
> 	2. If the THC is already power gated, the Reset Warn flow will wake up the IP's power gated domain to respond to the message.
> 	3. This is the IP's deepest functional power state and no device wake is supported.
> 	4. The Deep Sx flows are the same as normal Sx flows from THC perspective.
> 	5. In Sx flow, PMC does not do save and restore to THC. SW / BIOS will reinitialize THC as in boot flow.
>
> Sequence for Entry into S3/S4/S5 Power State [From D3-Power Gated State]
> 	1. THC Driver prepares for D3 entry.
> 	2. SoC: Platform reset warn flow is initiated by SoC through IOSF SB.                                                       -SoC: IOSF SB Message: ResetWARN as a request from SoC
> 	3. SIP: The wake is due to a pending Reset Prep Message to indicate Reset Warn request results in requesting for PGCB clock to enable the power up sequence.
> 	4. SIP: THC will trigger the IP-accessible entry
> 	5. SIP: THC will complete the handshake either through IOSF SB or through sideband wire.                 -SIP: IOSF SB Message: RSACK as a Reset Warn request acknowledgement from SIP
> 	6. SIP: Since the controller is still in D3 state, D3 Power Gating flow is triggered as soon as the IDLE conditions are met again (usually as soon as the Reset Warn Ack is returned).
> 	7. SIP: THC will trigger the IP-accessible PG entry.
> 	8. SoC: SBI reset and Primary resets are asserted.
> 	9. SoC: PGCB Reset remains de-asserted.
> 	10. SIP: Once the power off sequence is complete the PGCB will be un-requested.
>
> Sequence for Exit from S3/S4/S5 Power State [From D3]  (Note: The exit from Sx State is exactly as the flow described in section 7.4.4.3.)
> 	1. D0i3 Exit trigger conditions can only be SW initiated.
> 	2. SIP: THC will trigger the IP-accessible PG exit flow
> 	3. SIP: Once THC Controller PGD domain is powered up,                                                                              -ANY MMIO cycle shall NOT be UR'ed even if the IP state is still in D0i3.
> 							                                                                                                                                 -ANY MMIO cycle shall be UR'ed if the IP state is still in D3.
> 	4. Driver: THC/PCI driver will transition the controller from D0i3/D3 to D0.
> 	5. Driver: THC driver will initialize the controller and place it into the initial state.
> 	6. SIP: The Link clocks will be requested when activity on the links is required.                                       -Link clock requests will assert once the THC driver initiates a Bus cycle.
>
> At the TC level, multiple distinct test configuration combinations are defined due to the SoC/PCH supporting two Touch Host Controller (THC) instances,
> each with different interfaces and protocols as per the THC-IP specifications:
> 	• For single-port test mode: Either THC0 or THC1 will be enabled as HID-SPI or HID-I2C.
> 	• For dual-port test mode: Both THC0 and THC1 will be enabled concurrently, include all the possible combination of HID-SPI and/or HID-I2C.
> Here are a summary TC list of different test combinations/configurations for S5 regression: 
>
> References:
> Refer to THC HAS for more information.
>
> Test Flow:
> 	1. Boot RVP to Ubuntu OS, ensure HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
> 	2. Check the THC touch panel status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no yellow bang.
> 	3. Launch Solar tool from terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
> 	4. In Solar, set up S5 test with yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and specify 'N' cycles (e.g. 100).
> 	5. Start S5 PM cycling test; optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
> 	6. Wait for the test to complete and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
> HW Requirement:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C), which can be verified in Windows' Device Manager.
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> 	(Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and THC1 ports.
> 	• BIOS enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports.
> 	• 3PE touch panel support HID-SPI/-I2C (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A or BOM37A)
> 	• SW Driver support HID-SPI/-I2C (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C)
> 	• Solar Tool
> 	• (Optional) Logic Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
> Coverage:
> 	• Automated and repeated entry into and exit from S5 state, stressing full shutdown and restore cycles.
> 	• Verifies the THC touch panel PCI/ACPI enumeration and correct driver load after each S5 cycle, NO yellow bang/device error.
> 	• All outcomes are logged for analysis, aiding root cause detection of S5-related bugs.
>
> DFx Requirements:
> 	• Solar tool must allow log failures, errors, and device status, allowing systematic tracking.
> 	• Ensure the system is repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each S5 cycle.
>         • BIOS and platform configuration are pre-validated to reduce variables and help localization of failures.

---

### ✅ [TCD] SW GPIO Interrupt driven from THC MMIO register (SWGPIO_INT) to Dedicated vGPIO pin

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458205 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective: 
>
>
>   - This test is
>      to verify and ensure that the THC's SW GPIO interrupt register bit able to
>      assert/de-assert the vGPIO signal level (make sure it can be reflected at
>      GPIO-IP side). 
>
> Overview: 
>
> Starting on MTL and forward, a
> virtual GPIO based SW interrupt is also added as an output signal from THC-IP,
> and it is connected to a input vGPIO on GPIO-IP at SoC level.
>
> This feature request is to satisfy the existing
> Windows HID-class SW requirement using internal mechanism without physical
> pins. 
>
> (More details can be found in the
> following MTL PCR# 22010402304 , 22010402930) 
>
>
>
> From THC HW perspective, the
> vGPIO output signal/pin is toggle based on a MMIO RW
> register bit (i.e. SWGPIO_INT) which can be controlled by SW driver.
>
> In summary, it is up to SW driver
> decides when and how this bit is used. SW/driver is responsible to select which interrupt mode
> should be used for THC.
>
> If this vGPIO based interrupt is
> selected, SW shall disable THC's MSI/legacy interrupt and enable vGPIO
> interrupt.
>
> (Note  The
> enable of the vGPIO interrupt is not in THC, It shall be on vGPIO side like the
> normal GPIO enable.)
>
> THC GPIO Interrupt
>
>
>
>
> Bit
>
>    | 
>
>
> Attribute
>
>    | 
>
>
> Default
>
>    | 
>
>
> Description
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 4
>
>    | 
>
>
> RW
>
>    | 
>
>
> 1'b0
>
>    | 
>
>
> SW
>   GPIO Interrupt (SWGPIO_INT)
>
>
> 1:
>   Assert SW GPIO interrupt through vGPIO.
>
>
> 0:
>   De-assert SW GPIO interrupt through vGPIO.
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> For
> more information, please refer to the THC HAS
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Boot
>      system to UEFI shell with all HW requirements and BIOS configurations met.
>
>   - In PythonSV, verify that all
>      relevant GPIO configurations for touch controller interface are set
>      correctly by BIOS.
>
>   - Perform halt on the processor
>      cores.
>
>   - Configure the system to
>      enable SCI interrupt on the designated THC touch pad.
>
>   - Locate and reach the base
>      address for THC port to confirm its accessibility.
>
>   - Identify the control register
>      and field for software-triggered GPIO interrupt, then trigger the SCI
>      interrupt via software.
>
>   - Observe and confirm the
>      change in GPIO state indicating the interrupt has been asserted.
>
>   - Verify the SCI/GPE interrupt
>      is generated and propagated from GPIO to PMC.
>
>   - Remove/de-assert the SCI
>      interrupt by updating the relevant configuration.
>
>   - Confirm that all status
>      indicators reflect the interrupt has been cleared.
>
>   - Resume to normal system
>      operation by un-halting the processor cores.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable
>      SPI interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes. 
>
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - THC's SW-GPIO interrupt
>      register bit can effectively assert and de-assert the vGPIO signal level,
>      ensuring it is reflected on GPIO via register verification on
>      vgpio_thc2. 
>
>   - [Stretch-Goal]: The
>      assertion of THC's SW-GPIO interrupt able to trigger and generate Tier-1
>      SCI/GPE interrupt via vgpio_thc2 to PMC.
>
>   - On contrary, Tier-1
>      SCI/GPE interrupt can be cleared/de-asserted once THC's SW-GPIO
>      interrupt is de-asserted and the interrupt removed on
>      vgpio_thc2. 
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.  
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI/WinOS.
>
>   - PythonSV connected to host
>      system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Coverage includes
>      verification of system's behaviour before, during, and after interrupt
>      events, confirming correct state transitions and recovery.
>
>   - Functional coverage is
>      achieved by observing system responses to both software-triggered and
>      hardware-triggered interrupt events.
>
>   - Confirm the status indicators
>      are correctly cleared after interrupt removal and system operation returns
>      to normal.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Register read/write access is
>      required for all relevant GPIO, touch controller, and PMC configuration
>      and status registers to enable, trigger, and verify interrupt events.
>
>   - LA or equivalent hardware
>      debug tool should be used to monitor GPIO signal transitions and confirm
>      physical interrupt assertion and de-assertion.
>
>   - Platform should support
>      halting and resuming processor cores to facilitate controlled test
>      execution and state observation.
>
>   - Error status logging should
>      be enabled to capture any anomalies or failures during interrupt handling
>      and system recovery.

---

### ✅ [TCD] THC D0i2 Entry/Exit Flow (D0-->D0i2-->D0) and check PG/CGs (Allow SUT/RVP to enter S0i2.0)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458190 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test is to ensure and
>      verify that THC0 and/or THC1 able to enter and exit D0i2 power state,
>      working in conjunction with POR BOM Touch Panel connected on RVP.
>
>   - Verify that the touch
>      functionality of the touch panel still working normally after exited each
>      D0i2 power state. 
>
>   - (Optional/Additional
>      Verification): S0i2.0 residency increases as long as THC remained in D0i2
>      state. As THC in D0i2 does allow system to enter low-power/sleep/CS/MS
>      power state (i.e. S0i2.0 substate, specifically). 
>
>           [Important Note: S0i2.0 can be potentially blocked by other
>      IP/Domains due to other requirements not being met as it is system-level
>      power state, not IP specific.]
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> THC IP support D0i2
> power state which is initiated by the HW controller itself , and this feature
> is called the Hardware Autonomous power gating.
>
> In order for SOC/PCH
> to enter S0i2.0 state, there is a prerequisite requirement that THC has to
> enter D0i2 power state first.  
>
> [Test details will be added in TC-level.]
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for
> more information
>
>
>
> Clock Gating
>
> The
> THC controller does provide internal clock gating functionalities with
> controlled register which can enable/disable CG, and a clock req/ack interface
> following the Chassis Clock Specification for the following clocks:
>
>
>   - IOSF
>      Primary Clock
>
>   - IOSF
>      Sideband Clock
>
>   - PGCB
>      Clock
>
>   - SPI
>      non-SSC IO Clock
>
>   - SSC SPI IO clock (spread
>      spectrum clock from SoC)
>
>   - SPI DFT Clock
>
> (Note: Clock gating efficiency must be greater than
> 98%, to ensure that once enabled, THC will clock-gating most of the time
> including touch event. All input clocks have clock_req/clock_ack)
>
> For
> more information/details related to THC's D0i2, may refer to IP HAS document: https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html  
>
> PMC
>
> From PMC perspective
> of S0ix requirements for THC-IP, PMC must change the LPM Dashboard to match the
> S0ix Mapping Table (as shown below).
>
> No other changes by
> PMC are required. 
>
>
>
> Entry & Exit Flow of THC D0i2
>
> Below shows the VISA
> signals (available in ITH) to verify THC IP Controller entered/exit D0i2 +
> PG state
>
>
>
> THC D0i2 + IP
> Auto-PG ('ENTRY' Flow):
>
> https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_uarch_doc/MTL/MAS/THC_D0_S0ix.html#s0ix-entry
>
>
>
> THC D0i2 + IP
> Auto-PG (EXIT Flow): 
>
>
>
>
>
> THC IP Controller Behavior in Various Power
> States (according to IP HAS)
>
> This section
> summarizes the various power management capabilities of the THC. 
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Enable
>      THC0/THC1 port in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully boot OS with SW/HW requirements met.
>
>   - Leave touch
>      panel to be idle to enter D0i2 for a few seconds.
>
>   - Touch the
>      panel/Initiate touch activity to trigger D0i2 exit.
>
>   - Verify that
>      all signal state transition as expected.
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP with feature rework to
>      enable I2C/SPI interface on THC port
>
>   - Touch panel with touch cable
>      connected on RVP via THC port
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - For D0i2
>      entry, both PG_REQ_b and PG_ACK_b signals will be asserted to LOW state;
>      whereas, both FET_EN_b and FET_EN_ACK_b will de-asserted to HIGH state.
>
>   - For D0i2
>      Exit, both PG_REQ_b and PG_ACK_b signals will be de-asserted to HIGH
>      state; whereas, both FET_EN_b and FET_EN_ACK_b will asserted to LOW state.
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   -  RVP booted to Ubuntu OS
>      with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and/or THC1
>      ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e.
>      BOM36A/BOM37A/BOM52)
>
>   - SW Driver - QuickSPI (for
>      HIDSPI), QuickI2C (for HIDI2C)
>
>   - PythonSV + Intel TraceHub
>      Tool on host system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>   - (Optional) Logic
>      Analyzer/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Coverage
>      includes monitoring entry and exit of D0i2 events using VISA signal
>      tracing in ITH tool
>
>   - Transitions from low to high (L→H) or
>      high to low (H→L) are verified during idle-triggered entry and
>      touch-triggered exit.
>
>   - Modify the D0i2 entry timer
>      via PythonSV to include the robustness across different configurations
>
>
>
> DFx requirements:
>
>
>   - Enable Platform Debug Consent
>      in BIOS settings (Note: Only for MTL-S/MTP-S VISA tracing on ITH tool)
>
>   - Use ITH tool for VISA signal
>      monitoring
>
>   - (Optional) Adjust
>      D0i2_entry_timer via PythonSV for better capture

---

### ✅ [TCD] THC D3 Entry/Exit Flow (D0-->D3-->D0) and check PG/CGs  (Allow SUT/RVP S0i2.1/S0i2.2)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458192 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test is to ensure and
>      verify that THC0 and/or THC1 able to enter and exit D3 power state.
>
>   - Verify that the touch
>      functionality of the touch panel still working normally after exited each
>      D3 power state. (Alternatively,  can perform DMA / PIO read and write
>      operation.)
>
>   - Expectation is that the SW
>      driver will have the capability to perform D3 entry/exit on both THC ports
>      when system entering/exiting S0ix.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> THC IP support D3
> power state which is SW initiated via PCI PMCSR Power state
> register (i.e. THC_CFG_PMD_PMCSRBSE_PMCSR.PWRST).
>
> In order for SOC/PCH
> to enter deeper low-power sleep state (i.e. S0i2.1/S0i2.2 substate), there
> is a prerequisite requirement from THC-IP perspective that it has to enter D3
> power state first.  
>
> [Test details will be added in TC-level.]
>
>
>
> References:
>
> For more
> information/details related to THC's D3, may refer to IP HAS document.
>
> -
> Link:  https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/thc/THC_IP/4.x/IP%20Specs/HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS/SIP_THC_4x_HAS.html  
>
>
>
> Clock Gating
>
> The THC controller does provide internal clock gating
> functionalities with controlled register which can enable/disable CG, and a
> clock req/ack interface following the Chassis Clock Specification for the
> following clocks:
>
>
>   - IOSF Primary Clock
>
>   - IOSF Sideband Clock
>
>   - PGCB Clock
>
>   - SPI non-SSC IO Clock
>
>   - SSC SPI IO clock (spread
>      spectrum clock from SoC)
>
>   - SPI DFT Clock
>
> (Note: Clock gating efficiency is required to be
> greater than 98% which means that THC will clock-gating most of the time once
> its' enabled even during touch event. All input clocks
> have clock_req/clock_ack)
>
>
>
>
>
> PMC
>
> From PMC perspective
> of S0ix requirements for THC-IP, PMC must change the LPM Dashboard to match the
> S0ix Mapping Table (as shown below).
>
> No other changes by
> PMC are required. 
>
>
>
> THC D0i2 + IP Auto-PG ('ENTRY' Flow)
>
> Refer to https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_uarch_doc/MTL/MAS/THC_D0_S0ix.html#s0ix-entry  
>
> Note: Ignore the Chassis 2.2 RES_OWN_REQ/ACK[9:0] as
> they are Disabled by
> default (POR setting) 
>
>
>
> THC D0i2 + IP Auto-PG (EXIT Flow)
>
>
>
>
>
> THC IP Controller Behavior in Various Power
> States (according to IP HAS)
>
> This
> section summarizes the various power management capabilities of the THC. 
>
>
>   - 1.3ms gap between the frames.
>
>   - The exit of the ARMed/Doze states are not controlled by
>      SW. SW should not be in the exit latency when exiting from these states.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Enable
>      THC0/THC1 port in BIOS settings.
>
>
>   - Use SoCWatch tool to capture the D3 residency on THC enabled
>      port(s).
>
>   - On RVP/SUT,
>      press the PWRBTN for system to enter CS state.
>
>   - Perform read
>      to THC and PMC register bits in PythonSV to check if THC has entered D3
>      power state.
>
>   - Leave the
>      system to be idle for at least 3 minutes.
>
>   - Press PWRBTN
>      again to wake system from CS state.
>
>   - Read the THC
>      and PMC register bits again, it should indicates that THC has entered D0
>      power state.
>
>   - Stop the
>      SoCWatch capture and analyze log, expect to get high D3 residency.
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP with feature rework to
>      enable I2C/SPI interface on THC port
>
>   - Touch panel with touch cable
>      connected on RVP via THC port
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Able to
>      perform D3 entry and exit on THC0 port as expected.
>
>   - When perform
>      SoCWatch capture for at least 3 minutes (or more), a high D3 residency of
>      at least >70% on THC0 port is expected.
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to WinOS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and/or THC1
>      ports.
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings
>      for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel
>       (i.e. BOM36A/BOM37A/BOM52)
>
>   - SW Driver - QuickSPI (for
>      HIDSPI), QuickI2C (for HIDI2C)
>
>   - PythonSV + Intel TraceHub
>      Tool on host system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>
>   - (Optional) Logic
>      Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Capture THC
>      D3 residency on THC enabled port(s) using S0CWatch tool.
>
>   - Verify D3
>      entry/exit transitions via postcode when PWRBTN is pressed for system to
>      enter or wake from CS state.
>
>   - Validate
>      register bit values using PythonSV, confirming THC has enter/exit D3 power
>      state.
>
>   - Additional
>      verification: 
>
>
>   - Ensure
>       S0i2.0 + S0i2.1 + S0i2.2 (main) residencies increases when SUT
>       remains in sleep state (specifically S0i2.1/S0i2.2 substates).
>
>
> [Important Note: S0i2.1/S0i2.2 can be potentially
> blocked by other IP/Domains due to other requirements not being
> met as it is system-level power state, not IP specific.]
>
>
>
> DFx requirements:
>
>
>   - Validate debug visibility for
>      THC D3 entry/exit via: 
>
>
>   - Postcode logging during
>       PWRBTN-triggered CS state transitions.
>
>   - SoCWatch counters for
>       residency tracking.
>
>
>
>   - Confirm PythonSV provides
>      access to relevant power state registers for THC.

---

### ✅ [TCD] THC Function Disable Flow (BWG + HAS) and check PG/CGs

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458194 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Verify and ensure IP
>      requirements/conditions are met accordingly as expected during the THC IP
>      function disabled flow.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> Function
> Disable is one of the power states feature supported by the IP which
> involves certain IP requirements have to be met during function disable flow.
> This test will be carry out on a Post-Si System/RVP.
>
> BIOS initiates the
> function disable flow for both THC0 and THC1 ports, it is used when user needs
> to disable THC port(s) on the platform.
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to IP HAS,
> "Function Disable" Section/Chapter: 
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP boots to
>      UEFI shell with all hardware and BIOS configuration requirements met.
>
>   - Check/read
>      the relevant THC registers via PythonSV.
>
>   - Verify the
>      THC function disabled.
>
>   - Use Intel
>      Trace-Hub to check VISA signal states for THC0/THC1 for additional
>      verification.
>
>   - Perform host
>      reset to exit function disabled state.
>
>   - Repeat steps
>      #2 to #5 two more times for consistency.
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell or Windows OS.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - (Optional) Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the THC-SPI/I2C GPIO signal states for debug/analysis
>      purposes.
>
>   - (Optional) Intel Trace Hub
>      tool for VISA signal analysis.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - BIOS correctly configured all
>      the relevant registers involved in function disable flow.
>
>   - [Additional/Stretch-Goal]
>      VISA signals states reflected correctly as expected. 
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu or
>      UEFI Shell.
>
>   - In BIOS settings, disable
>      THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected to host system.
>
>   - Logic Analyzer for I2C/SPI
>      signals analysis.
>
>   - (Optional) Intel Trace Hub
>      tool for VISA signal analysis
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Verify all
>      required register configurations for THC function disable.
>
>   - Validate THC
>      responds with UR for all IOSF primary non-posted cycles and drops all IOSF
>      primary posted cycles.
>
>   - Check VISA
>      signal state for THC0/THC1 during disable sequence by using Intel
>      Trace-Hub.
>
>
>
> DFx requirements:
>
>
>   - Registers for THC disable
>      sequence must be accessible via PythonSV.
>
>   - Trace-Hub VISA signals
>      available for THC state monitoring.

### ✅ [TCD] THC Fuse/Softstrap Checkout (New Strap in NVL -- PCI_PI Class code/Programming Interface for BIOS)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458200 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test is to ensure and
>      verify all the THC related fuses and softstraps values/settings are
>      configured accordingly as expected. 
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
> THC IP has its' very
> own fuses and softstraps settings that can be initialized during early-boot
> stage of the system/platform.
>
> It is crucial to
> have all these settings configured correctly, as it may impact the overall IP
> features/functionalities in normal operation. (e.g. Power/Clock Gating,
> etc.)
>
>
>
> For example, here
> are a list of register available in below for both fuses and softstraps values
> checking/verification for THC IP:
>
> (Refer to TC level for more details on all the other
> remaining fuses/softstraps related to THC.)
>
>
>
> [PTL] CRIF/RDL .xml file Link: thc_top_ptl.xml 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> For more THC
> information, please refer to THC HAS.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Execute bootscript in
>      PythonSV to bring up the RVP post bootstall state/breakpoint.
>
>   - Bootscript is expected to
>      halt/hook to the specified breakpoint and find the relevant namednodes
>      (THC and Fuses) in PythonSV.
>
>   - Compare the output results
>      with HAS, all relevant fuse/softstraps must have the same configuration as
>      stated in HAS.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP in early-boot state
>      whereby all Fuses' and THC0's register spaces are accessible.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - All the Fuses/Softstraps configuration
>      for THC0 perfectly matches the expectations outlined in the HAS and
>      Harness requirements. 
>
>   - No missing Fuse/Softstrap register for
>      THC0 in PythonSV.
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu or
>      UEFI Shell.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected to host system.
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Verify all THC softstrap bits
>      defined in HAS.
>
>   - Ensure each configurable
>      field is validated against its corresponding expected value.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - THC softstrap/fuse register
>      must be accessible and readable via PythonSV.

---

| Field | Value |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test is
>      to ensure and verify that the all the GPIOs settings (used for THC-I2C
>      interface) are configured accordingly as required and expected by
>      BIOS. 
>
> Overview:
>
> THC IP uses GPIO pins for I2C interface with the TIC /
> Touch Device externally on RVP. 
>
> Therefore, the GPIOs need to be configured accordingly
> to specification (i.e. BWG, 'Chap18 GPIO Automation' spreadsheet, 'RVP Platform
> Mapping' spreadsheet). 
>
> Details will be
> added at TC-level.
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP boot to post-BIOS initialized
>      state (i.e. BIOS Setup Menu, UEFI Shell)
>
>   - Verify that
>      all respective GPIO register bits are configured accordingly by BIOS for
>      every THC0-I2C's GPIO pins in PythonSV
>
>
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with
>      feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0 port
>
>   - Touch panel with touch cable
>      connected on RVP via THC port
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - All THC0 I2C's GPIO register
>      bits are correctly configured by BIOS as expected
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Post-BIOS state [After BIOS initialization completed, e.g. at UEFI
>      shell or BIOS Menu]
>
>   - In
>      BIOS settings, THC0/THC1 port enabled with HID-I2C setup. 
>
>   - PythonSV
>      (via USB2DBC or XDP) connected on host system
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Functional
>      coverage via gesture validation
>
>
>
> DFx requirements:
>
>
>   - Ability to read THC
>      configuration registers via PythonSV

### ✅ [TCD] THC RLTR (Memory) Programming to PMC via QoS_DMD [PM Telemetry#11, 12]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458196 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test is
>      to verify that the Infinite, Active and Low power LTR messages are
>      sent to the PMC when there's a change in LTR value.
>
> Overview:
> The test will be run in Windows for Post Si. 
> Verify that the Infinite, Active and Low power LTR messages are sent to the PMC when there's a change in LTR value. 
>
> References:
> Refer to THC HAS for
> more information.
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP boots to UEFI shell with all hardware and BIOS configuration
>      requirements met.
>
>   - Perform Active/LP/Infinite LTR tests separately.
>
> 1. Active LTR
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.thc_ltr_en=1
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.active_ltr_en=1
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_val=100
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_scale=3
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.active_ltr_req=1
>
> 2. LP LTR
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.thc_ltr_en=1
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.active_ltr_en=1
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_val=100
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_scale=3
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.active_ltr_req=1
>
> 3. Infinite LTR 
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.thc_ltr_en
> = 1
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_en
> = 0 
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_req
> = 0 
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_req
> = 0 
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_en
> = 1  
>
> OR
>
> ## When the all ports' RXDMA's
> start bits are cleared
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_1.start
> = 0
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_2.start
> = 0
>
> 4. Read
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.show()
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.show()
>
> Hardware Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell or Windows OS.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - LTR messages
>      sent to PMC whenever LTR value changes. 
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell.
>
>   - In BIOS settings, enable
>      THC0/THC1 ports.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected to host system.
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Observe the
>      PMC receive LTR message from THC when conditions are met
>
>   - Confirm LTR
>      values are reflected on PMC register when messages are sent 
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Registers for THC and PMC
>      must be accessible via PythonSV.

---

### ✅ [TCD] THC Register Checkout (PCI Config, MMIO, Private Spaces) via PythonSV

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458201 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test
>      aims to validate and confirm the register read/write accessibility on both
>      THC0/THC1 port's register space and ensure all default values defined for
>      every bit of the register comply with the CRIF .xml file as outlined for
>      the platform.
>
> Overview:
>
> According to IP HAS, THC IP does provides the register
> programmed access for SW/BIOS, and DMA capability for OS and SW driver to
> configure(write) the touch IC and receive(read) the touch data in both HID and
> raw modes.
>
> Each project or product is accompanied by
> a distinct CRIF/RDL XML file, which meticulously catalogues all available
> registers supported by the THC-IP. [e.g. For PTL project CRIF/RDL .xml file
> Link: thc_top_ptl.xml  ]
>
> CRIF/RDL facilitates a structured and precise
> description of these registers, including their attributes such as address,
> size, default values, access permissions, and bit-field organization —are
> clearly defined and easily accessible.
>
>
>
> RDL = Register Description Language
>
> CRIF = Control Register Interchange Format 
>
>
>
> List of register space available for THC IP, which
> applicable for both THC0 and THC1 ports: 
>
>
>   - CFG - PCI Config Space Register Map
>
>   - MEM - MMIO Space Common Register Map 
>
>   - PORT.MEM - MMIO Space Port Register Map
>
>   - PVT - IOSF Sideband Private Space Register Map
>
> [Important Note: All 4 register
> spaces can be accessed via IOSF-Sideband/TAP-to-Sideband('tap2sb') in PythonSV
> tool as well.]
>
>
>
> Conducting the read/write register accessibility test
> via IOSF-Sideband/TAP2SB access method can implicitly encompass coverage for
> PCR in below
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> HSD Link
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> PCR Title
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Action/Coverage for Post-Si
>   FV
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Comments
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> 15010721030
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> [PTL-SoC]
>   IP to support 16-bit port ID endpoint
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Able
>   to cover
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Existing
>   register checkout test in PythonSV able to cover this PCR.
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to the THC HAS
> for more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Bring up RVP to post SOC/PCD
>      bootstall state/breakpoint, THC-IP register spaces should become
>      accessible.
>
>   - Bootscript is expected to
>      halt/hook to specified boot breakpoint.
>
>   - Configure all 4 THC0/1
>      register spaces access methos to 'TAP2SB' to validate the register
>      accessibility flow.
>
>   - Import 'thc_registers' script
>      and read all the THC0/1 registers.
>
>   - Write to the THC0/1 register
>      bits.
>
>   - Check and review if there are
>      any failures/errors return by script.
>
>   - Switch to IOSF-Primary access
>      method for all THC0/1 register spaces and repeat steps #5 to #7.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to UEFI shell or WinOS.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Able to perform register read/write
>      access on all THC0/THC1 registers in PythonSV, via both IOSF-SB and
>      IOSF-Primary access methods
>
>   - No missing register in PythonSV vs
>      CRIF.
>
>   - Verify THC0/THC1 register default
>      values in PythonSV against CRIF, excluding error/status registers.
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing
>      criteria above.
>
>   - Identified one or more missing
>      THC0/THC1 register in PythonSV. 
>
>   - Failed to access one or more THC0/THC1
>      register or field bit via PythonSV.
>
>   - Unexpected default value of any
>      THC0/THC1 register identified in PythonSV.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP in
>      early-boot state whereby all THC's register spaces are accessible [e.g. at
>      Pre-BIOS state or even earlier via bootscript() method to boot to a
>      certain breakpoint.]
>
>   - PythonSV
>      with USB2DBC connected to host system.
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Accessibility of the entire
>      THC register space.
>
>   - All documented fields checked
>      against expected values, including mode/SKU/board-variant dependent
>      fields.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - THC register spaces must be
>      accessible and readable via PythonSV.

### ❌ [TCD] Timestamping on Touch Input Report from RxDMA (when THC entering D0i2/D3)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458224 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
> This test is to verify and validate the following features/functionalities: - 
>
>   - Enable timestamp and driver can read timestamp from thc_m_prt_timestamp_2 and expose timestamp through GET_FEATURE telemetry channel,
>   - The timestamp read from register is monotonically increased, checked by GET_FEATURE telemetry channel,  
>   - Check the timestamp to the header of incoming frame read by RxDMA2,
>   - Check timestamp for every display sync event.
>   - This test is designed to have coverage for the LNL PCR below:  
> HSD Link
>  | 
> PCR Title
>  | 
> Comments
>  | 
> 22013202668
>  | 
> THC to timestamp incoming frame and provide timestamp register to SW
>
>  | 
> Added new SW-accessible registers to THC-IP for this timestamp feature.
>  | 
>
> Overview:
>
> Smooth Scrolling with touch is one of the key touch experience indictor.  For SW algorithm to smooth out jittery in touch positional data, timestamps are needed.
> The timestamp requirement to THC IP are:
> 1. (SW configurable) Prepend timestamp for each incoming frame.
> 2. SW (interrupt handler) readable of the current timestamp.  
>
> Additional Information:
>
>   - The PCR is related to the display frame sync signal PCR, but is independent as it can be unit tested without display frame sync.
>   - BUGECO: Override THC parameter THC_MICROSEC_CNT_DEF with the number of PGCB_CLK ticks in 1us window. (Default 7'h19 set for 2.56MHz PGCB_CLK frequency.)
>
> According to THC IP HAS:
> To support touch smoothing to improve the user scroll experience, THC supports timestamping on all input reports from RXDMA only.
>
> THC maintains an internal continuous running timer during D0 state. 
> (The unit of the timestamp is in 10 microsecond. When the timer overflows, it wraps around to restart from 0 again.)
>
> When THC receives a new touch frame (not micro-frame), it sends the timestamp as a QWord (64 bit) to the current RXDMA frame buffer as the first Qword, before sending the rest of the data frame.
> To support touch smoothing, the timestamp values shall be the time when the beginning of the frame is received by THC-IP.
> (i.e. interrupt cause register in HIDSPI, or the beginning of the HIDI2C input report when I2C controller sends the start bit )
> It shall not be the time when THC sends the frame to the system memory.
>
>   - If THC_TS_D0I2_MODE = 1 (low priority), the timer is reset to 0 when entering D0i2. 
> After every D0i2 exit, the timer starts incrementing when the first input frame is received, i.e. the timestamp for the first frame out of D0i2 is always 0.
>
>   - If THC_TS_D0I2_MODE = 0, the timer is paused instead of reset to 0 when entering D0i2. 
> After every D0i2 exit, the timer starts incrementing immediately out of D0i2 without waiting for input frame.
> Important Note: 
>
>   - The timer is always reset to 0 when entering D3. 
>   - THC has option to use either an external microsecond tick reference from SoC(PMC), or an internal core clock counter to generate the 10 microsecond pulse based on the bit THC_TIMESTAMP_SRC.
>   - Timestamping is not supported on SWDMA, TXDMA or PIO.
> 	THC Timestamp Register (THC_M_PRT_TIMESTAMP_1/2)
> Bits | Access Type | Default | Description | 
> 31 | RW | 0 | 
> Timestamp Enable (THC_TIMESTAMP_EN)
> This field enables/disables the THC timestamp capability. This bit can only be changed when the corresponding RXDMA is not running.
> 0: disable
> 1: enable: When THC receives a new touch frame (not microframe), it sends the time stamp as a QWord (64 bit) to the current RXDMA frame buffer as the 1st Qword before sending the rest of the data frame.
> (Note: When the THC_TIMESTAMP_EN is 0, the timestamp will be reset to 0.)	
>  | 
> 27:0 | RO/V | 0 | 
> Current Timestamp (THC_TIMESTAMP)
> This field reflects the current THC timestamp (1-based) that is used by THC HW to send with the input report. The unit of the timestamp is in 10 microsecond. | 
> THC Power Management Control Register (THC_SB_PM_CTRL)
>
> Bits | Access Type | Default | Description | 
> 30:24 | RW/V | 7'h19
>
>  | 
> Ten Microsecond Counter Value (THC_MICROSEC_CNT)
>
> BIOS programs this register based on the SoC PGCB source clock frequency for THC generate the 10 microsecond tick internally.
> For example, if the PGCB clock frequency is 2.5MHz, each clock cycle is 400ns, BIOS shall program this field to 25(7'h19) to generate the 10μs tick.
> This timer is not running in D0i2.
> This bit can be locked when the THC_BIOS_LOCK_EN bit is 1.
>  | 
> 18 | RW | 1'b0 | 
> Timestamp Timer Mode (THC_TS_D0I2_MODE)
> This field sets the behavior of the timestamp timer behavior in D0i2.
> If THC_TS_D0I2_MODE=1, the timer is reset to 0 when entering D0i2. After every D0i2 exit, the timer starts incrementing when the first input frame is received,
> i.e. the timestamp for the first frame out of D0i2 is always 0.
> If THC_TS_D0I2_MODE=0, the timer is paused instead of reset to 0 when entering D0i2. THC HW shall keep the timer in state retention in D0i2.
> After every D0i2 exit, the timer starts incrementing immediately out of D0i2 without waiting for input frame.
>  | 
> 17 | RW | 1'b0 | 
> Timestamp source (THC_TIMESTAMP_SRC)
> This field selects the THC timestamp's reference.
> 0: use external PMC microsecond tick as reference
> 1: use an internal counter on core clocks
> (Note: This register is used to select PMC 1us or internal counter for all the timers (in time based coalescing and frame sync event). 10us inaccuracy is ok for the timers.)
>  | 
>
> [Test details will be added in TC-level.]
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies: 
>
>   - RVP booted to WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>   - BIOS enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A/37A)
>   - SW Driver - QuickSPI (for HIDSPI), QuickI2C (for HIDI2C)
>   - PythonSV + Intel TraceHub Tool on host system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>   - (Optional) Logic Analyze/Oscilloscope

---

### ❌ [TCD] Touch Panel Display Sync (DSYNC) Signal to THC via TCON GPIO

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458225 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>   - Verify and ensure TCON Display SYNC GPIO function working as expected on THC0/THC1 when touching the BOM36A/37A display screen.
>   - And, this test is designed to have coverage for the PTL PCR/RCR below:
> HSD Link
>  | 
> PCR Title
>  | 
> Comments
>  | 
> 22013186639 
>  | 
> Pass display frame sync signal to either instance of THC IPs (SoC)
>
>  | 
> Display sync signal is routed back from TCON through eDP connector onto the RVP, which passes it to LNL GPIO.
> The expectation is the same signal on LNL RVP can be routed to SoC (since there is no MCF LCH).
> THC:  Accept internal display sync signals (from DE, and GPIO for unit testing)
>
> THC IP level changes that are directly related to the display sync signal are:
>
>   - Add display sync as a configurable SWI source
>   - Add display sync as a cause to flush coalescing state.
>   - Add delay start of coalescing based on display sync to avoid injecting delay
>   - Support GPIO signal as alternate source of display sync (for integration and stress testing)
>   - Related THC IP change (but not related to display sync signal): timestamp capability
>  | 
> 16020850026 
>
>  | Provide configuration option for assigning Display Sync pin to THC IP  (THC0 / THC1)
>  | BIOS only programs respective GPIO pin to native mode and IO Standby related settings as per platform GPIO automation sheet.
> E.g. In PTL-P, BIOS programs DSYNC-GPIO pin pmode as native function 3, 
> IO Standby State as GpioV2IosStateLatchLastValue and IO Standby termination as GpioV2IosTermSame.
>  | 
>
>
> Overview: 
> To further improved the accuracy of the display-touch synchronization, an external TCON GPIO sync is added as input signal to THC on RVP.   
> Basically, the display sync signal is routed back from TCON through eDP connector onto the RVP, which passes it to PTL-SoC/PCD via a physical GPIO pin.
>
> High-level Use Case/Scenario: 
>
>   - HW display frame sync signal will enable display and touch cadence alignment which is necessary for achieving smooth scrolling gestures on touchscreen.
> Below is the high-level block diagram showing how TCON Display Sync signal ingress into the PTL platform/system.
>
> THC-IP Internal Functional Block for TCON: 
>
> Important Note:
>
>   - The Sync signal source is selected by SW based on user or policy setting before enabling the coalescing using DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC register.
>   - SW Shall make sure the corresponding DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC is enabled before enabling the SYNC_EVT interrupt. 
>
> SYNC Event Timestamping
> The SYNC_EVT is also timestamped using the same timestamp timer in section Timestamping on Input Report (LNL+).
> The latest SYNC_EVT timestamp are provided to SW to capture the SYNC event timings for smoothing. 
> On every SYNC_EVT assertion, THC HW shall write the current time stamp to the CUR_SYNC_EVT_TS register.
> THC SYNC Event Timestamp Register (THC_M_PRT_SYNC_TIMESTAMP_1/2)
> Bits | Access Type | Default | Description | 
> 27:0 | RO/V | 0 | 
> Current SYNC Event Timestamp (CUR_SYNC_EVT_TS_TIMESTAMP)
> This field reflects the timestamp of the last SYNC Event. The unit of the timestamp is in 10 microsecond. | 
>
> THC Display Sync Register (THC_M_PRT_DISP_SYNC)
>
> Bits | AccessType | Default | Description | 
> 31 | RW | 0 | 
>
> SYNC based Coalescing Enable (C_ENABLE)
> SW sets the C_ENABLE to 1 to start the coalescing based on the sync signal. 
>
> (Note: This bit can be locked when the THC_DRV_LOCK_EN bit is 1 if this mode is not used.)
>  | 
> 30 | RW/L | 0 | 
>
> Emulated Display Sync Enable (DISP_SYNC_EMU_TIMER_EN)  -> [Not Relevant]
> SW sets the DISP_SYNC_EMU_TIMER_EN to 1 to start the display sync emulation timer. 
>
> (Note: This bit can be locked when the THC_DRV_LOCK_EN bit is 1 if this mode is not used.) 
>  | 
> 29:16 | RW/L | 13'h0828 | 
> Emulated Display Sync Period (DISP_SYNC_EMU_TIMER_PER) -> [Not Relevant]
> Specify the period of the emulated display frame sync. The minimum period is 2ms, maximum period is 100ms.
> The unit of the timer is 10us. SW sets the DISP_SYNC_EMU_TIMER_EN to 1 to start the timer. 
> When the timer expires, THC shall set the DISP_SYNC_EVT_INT_STS to 1 if DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC =01. 
> (Note: This bit can be locked when the THC_DRV_LOCK_EN bit is 1.) 
>  | 
> 15:14 | RW/L | 2'b00 | 
> THC Display Sync Event Source (DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC)
> SW selects the source of the Display Sync Event.
> 00 - None. The display sync event is disabled.
> 01 - SW emulated sync event.
> 10 - GPIO SYNC event.
> 11 - Display Engine provided IOSF SB Virtual Wire Message.
> SW shall select the source only after the source signal/interface are enabled and stabilized.
> (Note: This bit can be locked when the THC_DRV_LOCK_EN bit is 1.)  | 
> 13:0
>  | RW
>  | 0
>  | 
>
> Display Sync Delay (DISP_SYNC_DELAY)
> 0: No delay
> Others: delay in 10 microsecond for the display sync signal. This applicable for all three display sync mode. 
>
> Note: 
>
>   - If it is programmed with a value X, it is expected that the effective delay could be in the range X to X-3. ie (X-3)*10us delay difference can be expected in worst case. 
>   - SW will always write X+1 delay value expecting this variation and reducing variation to 20us resolution.
>   - This field needs to be programmed such that the effective delay value should be less than the sync event period.
>
>  | 
>
> TCON SYNC GPIO
> If DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC =10, a TCON GPIO input is received as a display frame sync signal on every display frame. 
> DISP_SYNC_GPIO_MODE is used to program THC to detect the GPIO event on different modes, including rising edge, falling edge or both edge.
> When the corresponding edge occurs, THC shall set the DISP_SYNC_EVT_INT_STS to 1 if DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC =10.
> Extra: 
> THC supports programmable delay after the sync source before used for interrupt or coalescing.
> The interrupt and coalescing engine have option to use delayed or un-delayed version of the sync signal which are selected using the DISP_SYNC_INT_DELAY_EN and DISP_SYNC_COAL_DELAY_EN respectively.
> The delay amount is programmable in DISP_SYNC_DELAY in the unit of 10 microsecond.
> (Note: The Coalescing can only be used when RXDMA is enabled. It is not used for SW DMA mode. The programmable delay should not be programmed to a value larger than the display sync period. The accuracy of the delay is less than 20 us.)
>
> THC Display Sync Register (THC_M_PRT_DISP_SYNC_2)
>
> Bits | AccessType | Default | Description | 
> 3 | RW | 0 | 
>
> Display Sync Coalescing Delay Enable (DISP_SYNC_COAL_DELAY_EN)
> SW selects the delayed version of Sync Event for the coalescing
> 0 : Un-delayed Sync event for the coalescing
> 1 : Delayed version of Sync event for the coalescing
>
> The delay amount is programmable in DISP_SYNC_DELAY in the unit of 10 microsecond. 
>  | 
> 2 | RW | 0 | 
>
> Display Sync Interrupt Delay Enable (DISP_SYNC_INT_DELAY_EN)  
> SW selects the delayed version of Sync Event for interrupt
> 0 : Un-delayed Sync event for sync event Interrupt
> 1 : Delayed version of Sync event for the sync event Interrupt.
> Note: 
>
>   - The delay amount is programmable in DISP_SYNC_DELAY in the unit of 10 microsecond.
>   - When THC_M_PRT_DISP_SYNC.C_ENABLE is set to 1,
> DISP_SYNC_INT_DELAY_EN is a don't care, and in this case sync event interrupt status setting delay will be controlled only by the DISP_SYNC_COAL_DELAY_EN and delay value programmed in DISP_SYNC_DELAY.
>  | 
> 1:0
>  | RW | 0 | 
> GPIO SYNC Signal Mode (DISP_SYNC_GPIO_MODE)
> SW sets the DISP_SYNC_GPIO_MODE based on the GPIO polarity.
> 00: SYNC assertion on GPIO rising edge.
> 01: SYNC assertion on GPIO falling edge.
> 10: SYNC assertion on GPIO both edge.
> 00: reserved
>
>  | 
>
> THC Interrupt Enable Register (THC_M_PRT_INT_EN)
> Bits | AccessType | Default | Description | PowerWell | 
> 14 | RW | 1'b0 | 
> THC Display Sync Event Interrupt Enable (DISP_SYNC_EVT_INT_EN)
> 1 - Enable the display sync event reporting interrupt.
> 0 - Disable the display sync event reporting interrupt.
> Note: The Interrupt enable is also qualified by the MSIE or Line Interrupt Enable AND GBL_INT_EN. When all these enables are set and when DISP_SYNC_EVT_INT_EN is set, an interrupt will be sent to SW. |  | 
>
> THC Interrupt Status Register (THC_M_PRT_INT_STATUS)
> Bits | AccessType | Default | Description | PowerWell | 
> 14 | RW/1C/V | 1'b0 | 
> THC Display Sync Event Interrupt Status (DISP_SYNC_EVT_INT_STS)
> Interrupt Status when the Display Sync Event occurs.
> The display sync event is selected in DISP_SYNC_EVT_SRC register.
> Open: SW is required to clear this bit before THC sends another SYNC MSI.
> If the DISP_SYNC_EVT_INT_EN bit is also 1, then an SW interrupt is generated. SW clears the bit by writing 1 to the bit.
> 1 - A display sync event occurs.
> 0 - no display sync event occurs. | 
> PGD
> State retention | 
>
> HW/SW Driver/BIOS Dependencies:
>   - RVP with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface for THC0 and THC1 port.
>   - BIOS enabling for HID-SPI/-I2C on THC0 and THC1 port.
>   - 3PE touch panel support HIDSPI / HIDI2C (i.e. BOM36A/37A)
>   - SW Driver support HIDSPI (i.e. QuickSPI), HIDI2C (i.e. QuickI2C)
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected to host system.
>   - Logic Analyzer for i2C/SPI signals analysis.

---

### ✅ [TCD] Wake-on-Touch (WoT) via Dedicated vGPIO pin

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458206 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - This test is
>      to ensure and verify that the vGPIO settings (used for Wake-On-Touch
>      function) are configured accordingly as required and expected by
>      BIOS. 
>
>   - Validate and
>      verify the WoT function (via vGPIO) is working as expected in Windows OS,
>      by single/double tap on the touch device after system entered CS
>      state. 
>
> Overview:
>
> Virtual GPIO (vGPIO) is used as a legacy GPIO (GPI)
> interrupt signal for Wake-On-Touch functionality (a.k.a, WoT or
> Tap-to-Wake). 
>
> Therefore, the GPIOs need to be configured accordingly
> to specification, i.e. SwAS, 'Chap18 GPIO Automation' spreadsheet. 
>
> [Details will be
> added in TC-level.]
>
>
>
> According to QuickSPI SwAS v1.0:
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to THC HAS for more information.
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - Boot the system to Ubuntu OS with all required hardware, software
>      and BIOS configurations.
>
>   - Verify that
>      WoT-related registers and kernel modules are properly configured and
>      loaded.
>
>   - Ensure WoT
>      is enabled for the touch controller device.
>
>   - Suspend the
>      system to enter low power (S0ix) state.
>
>   - Wake the
>      system using a touch input and verify successful wake-up.
>
>   - Repeat
>      suspend and wake cycles multiple times to check for consistency.
>
>   - Optionally,
>      monitor system signals and logs to confirm expected behaviour during sleep
>      and wake transitions. 
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0/THC1 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR
>      touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via
>      the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>
>   - Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI/I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
>   - PythonSC
>      with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>   - Optional: Custom rework
>      required to wire-out all the THC-SPI/I2C IO pins for Acute LA signals
>      tracing. (Note: Make sure to use single-core wires and keep the
>      length as short as possible to avoid/minimize any signal integrity
>      impact.)
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Able to achieve N targeted
>      number of cycles of WoT successfully.
>
>   - No errors and Yellow-Bang
>      observed on THC0/THC1 port. 
>
>   - SleepStudy indicates system
>      wake caused by Wake-on-Touch (WoT) only.
>
>   - THC0/THC1-SPI/I2C signal
>      lines are clean and no activity after the S0iX entry (i.e. No periodic
>      wakes/interrupts occurred).
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Windows OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for
>      both THC0 and THC1 ports.
>
>   - In BIOS
>      settings, THC0 / THC1 port enabled with HID-SPI or HID-I2C setup + WoT
>      enabled.
>
>   - 3PE touch
>      panel support HIDSPI (i.e. BOM36A / BOM37A).
>
>   - SW Driver
>      with WoT support enabled for both HID-SPI and HID-I2C (i.e. QuickSPI,
>      QuickI2C).
>
>   - GPIO SW
>      Driver with proper mapping for the vGPIO.
>
>   - PythonSV on
>      host system (via USB2DBC or XDP).
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Ensures
>      correct kernel version, THC kernel module loading, and PCI device
>      enumeration.
>
>   - Validates
>      system entry into S0ix and correct residency counter behaviour.
>
>   - Verifies
>      that touch input reliably wakes the system from sleep, with correct
>      postcode transitions and residency counter updates.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - PythonSV
>      tool ability to read and configure Vgpio_THC0/THC1 registers and PCI
>      device wakeup settings.
>
>   - Access to
>      kernel logs, module status, and PCI device information.
>
>   - RVP/SUT must
>      support WoT, S0ix, and all relevant kernel modules.

---

### ✅ [TCD] Warm Reset PM Cycling

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458223 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
> 	• Ensure the SUT/RVP able to perform and complete "N" iterations of Warm-Reset PM cycling (via solar tool) successfully, with THC0 and/or THC1 ports enabled as I2C/SPI-interface. 
> 	• Verify that the touch functionality of the touch panel still working normally after each WR cycle completed.
> 	• Alternatively, both PIO and DMA operations can be exercise to ensure that both THC port and the connected TIC/touch device functionality remains operational and performs as expected.
>
> Overview:
> Warm-Reset (WR, or equivalent to OS Restart) is a prevalent PM regression/cycling test in OSbV, supported and exercise by the THC-IP, as it entails resetting both THC0 and THC1 controllers during the WR cycle.
> From the perspective of THC-IP, the WR exit sequence mirrors that of a G3-Exit or Cold-Boot. (Same for Cold-Reset as well)
> This test is conducted using Solar SW tool on a RVP, either by using manual testing approach or an automated method via NGA framework—the specifics of which are delineated at the Test Case (TC) level.
> In the context of System-level Validation, this WR test will implicitly encompasses the verification of Fuses/Soft-straps loading, BIOS, SW Driver and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> It also includes the verification of HID-SPI or HID-I²C interfacing with PIO and DMA operations on dedicated THC port(s) under test in each and every WR cycle.
> Additionally, this WR test will implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, as detailed below.
>
> (Note: Details for WR/Cold-Boot flow are as shown below, this information taken from IP-HAS "G3 Exit Flow/THC HW Power-Up".)
>
> At the TC level, multiple distinct test configuration combinations are defined due to the SoC/PCH supporting two Touch Host Controller (THC) instances,
> each with different interfaces and protocols as per the THC-IP specifications:
> 	• For single-port test mode: Either THC0 or THC1 will be enabled as HID-SPI or HID-I2C.
> 	• For dual-port test mode: Both THC0 and THC1 will be enabled concurrently, include all the possible combination of HID-SPI and/or HID-I2C.
> Here are a summary TC list of different test combinations/configurations for WR regression: 
>
> References:
> Refer to THC HAS for more information.
>
> Test Flow:
> 	1. Boot RVP to Ubuntu OS, ensure HW/SW and BIOS are correctly configured.
> 	2. Check the THC touch panel status using "lspci"; expect intel_quickspi/i2c driver with no yellow bang.
> 	3. Launch Solar tool from terminal: "sudo ./SolarPanel.sh".
> 	4. In Solar, set up Warm Reset test with yellow bang check, exit on failure, bypass windows login, and specify 'N' cycles (e.g. 100).
> 	5. Start Warm Rest PM cycling test; optionally, load a save Solar config XML for a faster setup.
> 	6. Wait for the test to complete and review the pass/fail result in the solar tool logs.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of Warm-Reset (WR) PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0 still working normally after each WR cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> 	(Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted WR cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after WR. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the WR cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of WR cycle.
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and THC1 ports
> 	• BIOS enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on both THC0 and THC1 ports
> 	• 3PE touch panel support HID-SPI/-I2C (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A or BOM37A)
> 	• SW Driver support HID-SPI/-I2C  (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C)
> 	• Solar Tool
> 	• (Optional) Logic Analyze/Oscilloscope
>
> Coverage:
> 	• Monitor THC0/THC1 touch controller status after each warm-reset cycle, including driver functionality and error status (yellow-bang checks)
> 	• Validate the touch functionality and HID-SPI/I2C operations using both PIO and DMA tests post-reset.
> 	• Include negative scenarios (e.g., force error conditions, run extended and rapid cycles) for stress coverage.
>
> DFx Requirements:
> 	• Ensure the system is repeatably bootable and touch controller is accessible after each warm-reset cycle.
> 	• Solar tools and test scripts must log all failures, errors, and device status; All logs must be traceable.
>         • Device and driver error states must be clearly reported in test results.

---

### ✅ [TCD] [ERT] THC Power/Energy Reporting Activity Counter on PMC [PM Telemetry#3]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458208 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case_definition |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L2 (TCD) |

**Description:**

> Objective:
>
>
>   - Verify that the PMC correctly
>      increments the FG_PR_CNTR_PGD register counters for THC0/THC1 as expected,
>      when THC0/THC1 is active state (not idle, or D0i2) due to ongoing touch
>      input interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - FG_PR_CNTR_PGD register
>      counters for THC0 and/or THC1 will stop incrementing and/or reset back to
>      0 (controlled by PMC FW), once THC0/THC1 entered idle state (or D0i2) with
>      NO ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>
>
> Overview:
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> References:
>
> Refer to
> THC HAS for more information.
>
> https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_doc/NVL/PCD-H/Feature%20HAS/NVL_PCD_Energy_Reporting_HAS.html#dynamic-energy-activity-indicators 
>
>
>
> Test Flow:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met.
>
>   - Ensure the system enable and
>      support S0ix _PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC.
>
>   - Read and record down the
>      initial values of THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counters on PMC registers,
>      and check the PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers.
>
>
>   - Configure
>      the D0i2_entry_timer register to a higher value 0x8 or 0x9 for longer
>      timeout, so that THC controller will only enter D0i2 after the timer
>      expired/timeout 
>
>   - Force THC0/THC1 into active
>      state by touching the eDP display screen of the touch panel as it will
>      generate touch input interrupt + traffic to THC.
>
>   - Continuously read and note
>      the FG_PR_CNTR registers values while THC enter and remain in D0 (active)
>      state. Expectation: THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR blocker counter values increment
>      with touch activity and system traffic.
>
>   - Stop all touch
>      activity, allowing the system to idle, permitting THC0/THC1 to enter D0i2
>      (idle). Expectation: THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR blocker counter will stop
>      incrementing and/or reset back to 0.
>
>   - Repeat steps #3 to #7 to
>      ensure consistent and repeatable behaviour. 
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0/THC1 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - While
>      THC0/THC1 is in active state, PMC increments FG_PR_CNTR register counters
>      for THC0/THC1 as expected.
>
>   - When
>      THC0/THC1 enter idle state (D0i2), FG_PR_CNTR register counters for
>      THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing and/or reset to 0.
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> HW/SW/Driver/BIOS/Other Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both
>      THC0 and/or THC1 ports with touch devices connected.
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - PythonSV
>      (via USB2DBC or XDP) connected on host system.
>
>
>
> Coverage:
>
>
>   - Includes validation that
>      counter increments correlate with touch activity and remain static during
>      idle.
>
>   - The test covers THC0/THC1
>      transitions between D0-active and D0i2 idle states, verifying that
>      FG_PR_CNTR counters increment during active state and stop/reset in idle
>      state.
>
>   - All relevant PMC and THC
>      registers are monitored before, during, and after touch activity and idle
>      periods.
>
>
>
> DFx Requirements:
>
>
>   - Use of PythonSV for
>      automation and register polling.
>
>   - Ability to read/write PMC and
>      THC registers (including FG_PR_CNTR and D0i2_entry_timer).
>
>   - Hardware support for
>      generating touch events and observing system response.
>
>   - System must be configured
>      with validated power management and PMC telemetry features enabled.
