### ✅ [TC] Cold Reset Cycles with THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458310 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically Cold Resets (CR), while running test traffic through a single port THC1 that configured as HID-I2C interface.
>                (Note: 'N' iterations of CR Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
>
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of Fuses/Soft-straps loading, BIOS, SW Driver and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>   - Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - Additionally, the THC controller will undergo a reset during each Cold-Reset cycle. This CR sequence will implicitly encompass the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>   - Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>   - In Solar tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of Cold-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
>   - Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>   - Specify Start Delay Time (Optional)
>   - Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state (e.g. 180 seconds)
>   - Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds) 
>   - Select the "Cold-Reset" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "Cold-Reset|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right, which implied it is confirmed being set.
>   - Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test. 
>
> (Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously .)
>
>   - Wait for the CR cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>   - NGA Testline for CR Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17 
>   - If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL/NVL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL/NVL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-I2C kernel driver.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of Cold-Reset (CR) PM cycling count can be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bang occurred on THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
>   - Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC1 still working normally after each CR cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted CR cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after CR. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>   - Other IP/domains, Ubuntu OS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may cause the CR cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of CR cycle.
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC controller/driver after any cold-reset cycle.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-reset (can operate via HID-I2C interface, PIO/DMA)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted CR cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after CR. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>   - Other IP/domains, Ubuntu OS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may cause the CR cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of CR cycle.
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC controller/driver after any cold-reset cycle.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-reset (can operate via HID-SPI interface, PIO/DMA)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ❌ [TC] Hyper-V and VT-d Flow with THC0 Enabled (IOMMU remapping MSI Interrupt)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458279 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>
>   - The main purpose of this test is to ensure the touch
>      panel is still functioning, even with both VT-d and Hyper-V mode enabled.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - SUT/RVP
>      boot into BIOS menu with all the HW requirements met.
>
>   - Enable the
>      VT-d in BIOS menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> System Agent (SA)
>      Configuration -> VT-d setup menu -> VT-d.
>
>   - Window OS
>
>           Enable the
>      Hyper-V in WinOS, go to Windows Feature -> Check/Tick 'Hyper-V' check
>      box.
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>           Hyper-V is not supported in Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Check the touch panel is still functioning after enabled
>      VT-d and Hyper-V by trying multiple touch gesture/enabling VKB and touch
>      in pre-OS environment.
>
> SW/HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/I2C Driver
>      on Ubuntu OS/Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C driver for THC0 port)
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu/WinOS
>      with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - Optional:
>      Custom rework required to wire-out all the THC-SPI/I2C IO pins for Acute
>      LA signals tracing.
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.  
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Touch functionalities of the touch panel are fully
>      operational and validated after VT-d and Hyper-V are enabled.
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>   - For further verification, the touch panel should also be working in
>      Hyper-V Virtual Machine (VM).
>
>
>   - In WinOS, go to Hyper-V
>       Manager -> Quick Create -> Create new image for the VM -> Wait
>       for VM to boot to WinOS.
>
>
>
>   - Confirm that
>      all expected touch responses (e.g., tap, swipe, multi-touch) are correctly
>      registered by the system.
>
>   - Verify that enabling VT-d and Hyper-V
>      does not cause any loss of touch capability or driver malfunction.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ❌ [TC] Hyper-V and VT-d Flow with THC1 Enabled (IOMMU remapping MSI Interrupt)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458282 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The main purpose of this test is to ensure the touch panel is still functioning, even with both VT-d and Hyper-V mode enabled.
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - N/A
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - SUT/RVP boot into BIOS menu with all the HW requirements met.
>   - Enable the VT-d in BIOS menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> System Agent (SA) Configuration -> VT-d setup menu -> VT-d.
>   - Window OS
> Enable the Hyper-V in WinOS, go to Windows Feature -> Check/Tick 'Hyper-V' check box.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
> Hyper-V is not supported in Ubuntu OS.
>   - Check the touch panel is still functioning after enabled VT-d and Hyper-V by trying multiple touch gesture/enabling VKB and touch in pre-OS environment.
> SW/HW Requirements:
>
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/I2C Driver on Ubuntu OS/Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C driver for THC1 port)
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC1 port.   
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - Optional: Custom rework required to wire-out all the THC-SPI/I2C IO pins for Acute LA signals tracing.
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.  
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Touch functionalities of the touch panel are fully operational and validated after VT-d and Hyper-V are enabled.
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - For further verification, the touch panel should also be working in Hyper-V Virtual Machine (VM).
>   - In WinOS, go to Hyper-V Manager -> Quick Create -> Create new image for the VM -> Wait for VM to boot to WinOS.
>
>   - Confirm that all expected touch responses (e.g., tap, swipe, multi-touch) are correctly registered by the system.
>   - Verify that enabling VT-d and Hyper-V does not cause any loss of touch capability or driver malfunction.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] S0i2.0 PM Cycling  (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) with THC0 (HID-I2C) Enabled only (with WoT Disabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458318 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes
>      'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test
>      traffic through a single port THC0 that configured as HID-I2C
>      interface with WoT disabled.
>
>
>  (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary
> across different project milestones.)  
>
>
>   - This test is designed to
>      implicitly encompass the verification of SoC/PCH S0ix entry/exit flow,
>      THC-IP's D3 + PG entry/exit flow, and THC Controller + TIC device
>      re-initialization sequences by SW Driver.
>
>   - It will also assess and
>      exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port
>      to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC
>      device.
>
>   - Solar SW tool will be
>      utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>
>   - This S0ix sequence
>      will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS,
>      i.e. S0ix Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD -->
>      'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings ->
>      Enable Low Power S0 Idle Capability
>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configurations -> OS IDLE Mode, S0ix
>      Auto Demotion -> Enabled
>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration
>      -> LPM S0i2.0 / S0i2.1 / S0i2.2 -> Enabled
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - Pre-Test or Sanity Check
>
>
>   - Manually enter
>       sleep state by clicking on Power -> Sleep in Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter
>       sleep/S0ix state, i.e., check for postcode = 01C5.
>
>   - Dump residency counter script in PythonSV to
>       check and verify that the residency for each S0ix substate are
>       progressively increasing over time.
>
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>      the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch
>      panel with the following commands
>
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
>
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel
> info and which bus device function
>
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go
>      into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>
>   - Enter the following command to open the
>      Solar GUI
>
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>
>   - In Solar tool, validator/user may use the
>      following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of
>      Cold-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows
>      Login enabled.
>
>
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang check
>       in Advanced Verification PRE/POST check tab.
>
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs
>       tab.
>
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in
>       Knobs tab.
>
>
>
>
>   - Specify 'N' total
>       number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>
>   - Specify Start Delay Time (e.g. 120 seconds)
>
>   - Specify total sleep time before system wake
>       back up (e.g. 120 seconds)
>
>   - Specify total Active time in Ubuntu OS before
>       starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 60 seconds)
>
>   - Select the "S0ix"
>       button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer"
>       will be shown in the big blue box in the right ->
>       which implied it is confirmed being set.
>
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met
>       after completed each S0ix PM cycle (e.g. 50%)
>
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar will
>       verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix Residency
>       Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix PM cycled.
>
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency (e.g.
>       S0C or PCH)
>
>   - Finally, click on the green plat button to start the PM
>       Regression/Cycling test.
>
>
> (Note:
> Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM
> regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file
> with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling
>      test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the
>      "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file
>      as well.
>
> [Alternate
> Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released
> NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>
>   - NGA Testline for
>      S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>
>   - If using the existing
>      testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant
>      framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA
>      support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>      booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>      HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
>         (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according
>      to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled
>      via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>
>   - Requirement:
>      THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>
>   - Requirement: For
>      SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.
>
> New S0ix Mapping
> for THC
>
>
>
>
> Requirement
>
>    | 
>
>
> S0i2.0
>
>    | 
>
>
> S0i2.1
>
>    | 
>
>
> S0i2.2
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> THC
>   Power Gated
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> THC
>   in D3
>
>    | 
>
>
> Not
>   Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Using Solar
>      tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can
>      be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s)
>      returned/encountered. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0
>      still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable
>      to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code
>      returned, 
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices, 
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>
>   - Other
>      IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level
>      components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle.
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - System shall successfully
>      enter and exit low power S0ix state, with clear verification by
>      incrementing residency counters over time.
>
>   - Verify no yellow-bang or
>      driver errors occur on THC device in Ubuntu, with the quicki2c/spi kernel
>      driver loaded and active.
>
>   - Ensure the touch device
>      maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter

>      panel with the following commands

>

>

>      use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles
>      of S0ix with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure

>
>

>       Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
>
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.

>       back up. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>
>   - Specify total Active Time in WinOS before
>       starting next PM cycle. (e.g. 60 seconds)
>
>   - Select the "S0ix"
>       button. It will shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer"
>       will be shown in the big blue box in the right ->
>       which implied it is confirmed being set. 
>
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met
>       after completed each S0ix PM cycled. (e.g. 50%)
>
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar
>       will verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix
>       Residency Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix
>       PM cycled.
>
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency.
>       (e.g. SOC or PCH)
>
>   - Finally, click on the green play button to
>       start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
>
>
> (
> Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM
> regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file
> with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling test to
>      complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test
>      Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate
> Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released
> NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>
>   - NGA Testline for
>      S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>
>   - If using the existing
>      testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant
>      framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA
>      support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>      booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>      HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with
>      feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according
>      to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled
>      via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>
>   - Requirement:
>      THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>
>   - Requirement: For
>      SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.
>
> New S0ix Mapping
> for THC
>
>
>
>
> Requirement
>
>    | 
>
>
> S0i2.0
>
>    | 
>
>
> S0i2.1
>
>    | 
>
>
> S0i2.2
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> THC
>   Power Gated
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> THC
>   in D3
>
>    | 
>
>
> Not
>   Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Required
>
>    | 
>
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Using Solar
>      tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can
>      be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on both THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
>
>   - Extra: Ensure
>      that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch devices connected
>      on THC0 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively,
>      this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA
>      operations/traffic tests.
>
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled
> especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if
> touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>   - Solar tool unable to
>      pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code
>      returned, 
>
>
>   - Yellow-bang
>      occurred on any THC port, and/or caused by any other
>      drivers/devices, 
>
>   - Touch
>      functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e.
>      PIO and DMA operation)
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - System can
>      reliably enter S0ix state and show residency counters increment over time.
>
>   - Verify
>      driver stack and enumeration are correct for all critical peripherals (THC
>      touch panel), with no yellow-bang errors through OS and kernel logs.
>
>   - Ensure the touch device
>      maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled (with WoT Disabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458319 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test traffic through both THC0 and THC1 ports that configured as HID-I2C interface with WoT disabled.
>                (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)  
>
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of SoC/PCH S0ix entry/exit flow, THC-IP's D3 + PG entry/exit flow, and THC Controller + TIC device re-initialization sequences by SW Driver.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 and THC1 ports to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>   - Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This S0ix sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. S0ix Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Enable Low Power S0 Idle Capability
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configurations -> OS IDLE Mode, S0ix Auto Demotion -> Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> LPM S0i2.0 / S0i2.1 / S0i2.2 -> Enabled
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - Pre-Test or Sanity Check
>   - Manually enter sleep state by clicking on Power -> Sleep in Ubuntu OS.
>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter sleep/S0ix state, i.e., check for postcode = 01C5.
>   - Dump residency counter script in PythonSV to check and verify that the residency for each S0ix substate are progressively increasing over time.
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>   - Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>   - In Solar tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of Cold-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST check tab.
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
>
>   - Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>   - Specify Start Delay Time (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total sleep time before system wake back up (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total Active time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 60 seconds)
>   - Select the "S0ix" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met after completed each S0ix PM cycle (e.g. 50%)
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar will verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix Residency Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix PM cycled.
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency (e.g. S0C or PCH)
>   - Finally, click on the green plat button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
>
> (Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>   - NGA Testline for S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>   - If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 and THC1 ports.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 and THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>         (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>   - Requirement: THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>   - Requirement: For SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.
> New S0ix Mapping for THC
> Requirement
>  | 
> S0i2.0
>  | 
> S0i2.1
>  | 
> S0i2.2
>  | 
> THC Power Gated
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> THC in D3
>  | 
> Not Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
>   - Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>   - Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle.
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - System shall successfully enter and exit low power S0ix state, with clear verification by incrementing residency counters over time.
>   - Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC device in Ubuntu, with the quicki2c/spi kernel driver loaded and active.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled (with WoT Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458335 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test traffic through THC0 port and THC1 port, which both are configured as HID-I2C interface with WoT enabled, concurrently.
>         (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)    
>
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of SoC/PCH S0ix entry/exit flow, THC-IP's D3 + PG entry/exit flow, and THC Controller + TIC device re-initialization sequences by SW Driver.
>   - It will also assess and exercise HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on both THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently to ensure robust touch operations between the host controller and TIC device.   
>   - Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This S0ix sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. S0ix Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration :
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> Wake on Touch Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Enable Low Power S0 Idle Capability
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configurations -> OS IDLE Mode, S0ix Auto Demotion -> Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> LPM S0i2.0 / S0i2.1 / S0i2.2 -> Enabled
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - Pre-Test or Sanity Check
>   - Manually enter sleep state by clicking on Power -> Sleep in Ubuntu OS.
>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter sleep/S0ix state, i.e., check for postcode = 01C5.
>   - Dump residency counter script in PythonSV to check and verify that the residency for each S0ix substate are progressively increasing over time.
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>   - Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>   - In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S0ix with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
>   - Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>   - Specify Start Delay Time. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>
>   - Specify total sleep time before system wake back up. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total Active Time in WinOS before starting next PM cycle. (e.g. 60 seconds)
>   - Select the "S0ix" button. It will shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set. 
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met after completed each S0ix PM cycled. (e.g. 50%)
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar will verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix Residency Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix PM cycled.
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency. (e.g. SOC or PCH)
>   - Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
>
> ( Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>   - NGA Testline for S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>   - If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
> SW Requirements:

>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.

> HW Requirements:

>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.

>

> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled (with WoT Disabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458320 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test traffic through port THC0 that configured as HID-I2C interface and port THC1 configured as HID-SPI interface with WoT disabled.
>                (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)  
>
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of SoC/PCH S0ix entry/exit flow, THC-IP's D3 + PG entry/exit flow, and THC Controller + TIC device re-initialization sequences by SW Driver.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C/SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0/THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>   - Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This S0ix sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. S0ix Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Enable Low Power S0 Idle Capability
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configurations -> OS IDLE Mode, S0ix Auto Demotion -> Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> LPM S0i2.0 / S0i2.1 / S0i2.2 -> Enabled
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - Pre-Test or Sanity Check
>   - Manually enter sleep state by clicking on Power -> Sleep in Ubuntu OS.
>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter sleep/S0ix state, i.e., check for postcode = 01C5.
>   - Dump residency counter script in PythonSV to check and verify that the residency for each S0ix substate are progressively increasing over time.
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c/spi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c/spi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>   - Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>   - In Solar tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of Cold-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST check tab.
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
>
>   - Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>   - Specify Start Delay Time (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total sleep time before system wake back up (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total Active time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 60 seconds)
>   - Select the "S0ix" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met after completed each S0ix PM cycle (e.g. 50%)
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar will verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix Residency Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix PM cycled.
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency (e.g. S0C or PCH)
>   - Finally, click on the green plat button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
>
> (Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>   - NGA Testline for S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>   - If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port and SPI interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>         (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>   - Requirement: THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>   - Requirement: For SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.
> New S0ix Mapping for THC
> Requirement
>  | 
> S0i2.0
>  | 
> S0i2.1
>  | 
> S0i2.2
>  | 
> THC Power Gated
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> THC in D3
>  | 
> Not Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C, QuickSPI).
>   - Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C/SPI touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C/SPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>   - Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle.
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - System shall successfully enter and exit low power S0ix state, with clear verification by incrementing residency counters over time.
>   - Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC device in Ubuntu, with the quicki2c/spi kernel driver loaded and active.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled (with WoT Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458337 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test traffic through THC0 port which configured as HID-I2C interface and THC1 port, configured as HID-SPI interface, both with WoT enabled.
>         (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)    
>
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of SoC/PCH S0ix entry/exit flow, THC-IP's D3 + PG entry/exit flow, and THC Controller + TIC device re-initialization sequences by SW Driver.
>   - It will also assess and exercise HID-I2C/SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on THC0/THC1 ports to ensure robust touch operations between the host controller and TIC device.   
>   - Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This S0ix sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. S0ix Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration :
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> Wake on Touch Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Enable Low Power S0 Idle Capability
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configurations -> OS IDLE Mode, S0ix Auto Demotion -> Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> LPM S0i2.0 / S0i2.1 / S0i2.2 -> Enabled
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - Pre-Test or Sanity Check
>   - Manually enter sleep state by clicking on Power -> Sleep in Ubuntu OS.
>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter sleep/S0ix state, i.e., check for postcode = 01C5.
>   - Dump residency counter script in PythonSV to check and verify that the residency for each S0ix substate are progressively increasing over time.
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c/spi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c/spi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>   - Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>   - In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S0ix with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
>   - Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>   - Specify Start Delay Time. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>
>   - Specify total sleep time before system wake back up. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total Active Time in WinOS before starting next PM cycle. (e.g. 60 seconds)
>   - Select the "S0ix" button. It will shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set. 
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met after completed each S0ix PM cycled. (e.g. 50%)
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar will verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix Residency Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix PM cycled.
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency. (e.g. SOC or PCH)
>   - Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
>
> ( Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>   - NGA Testline for S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>   - If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>   - Requirement: THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>   - Requirement: For SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.
> New S0ix Mapping for THC
> Requirement
>  | 
> S0i2.0
>  | 
> S0i2.1
>  | 
> S0i2.2

> THC Power Gated
>  |

> THC in D3
>  | 
> Not Required

> Required
>  |

>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on both THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C. QuickSPI).
>   - Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C/SPI touch devices connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on any THC port, and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e. PIO and DMA operation)
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - System can reliably enter S0ix state and show residency counters increment over time.
>   - Verify driver stack and enumeration are correct for all critical peripherals (THC touch panel), with no yellow-bang errors through OS and kernel logs.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458321 |

| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test traffic through a single port THC0 that configured as HID-SPI interface with WoT disabled.
>                (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)

>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.

>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>   - Requirement: THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>   - Requirement: For SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.

> Requirement

> S0i2.1
>  | 
> S0i2.2
>  | 
> THC Power Gated
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> THC in D3
>  | 
> Not Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can be achieved successfully on RVP with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickSPI).
>   - Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch device connected on THC0 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
>   - Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may cause the S0ix cycle.
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - System shall successfully enter and exit low power S0ix state, with clear verification by incrementing residency counters over time.
>   - Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC device in Ubuntu, with the quicki2c/spi kernel driver loaded and active.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] S0i2.0 PM Cycling (MS with Display ON while THC in D0i2) with THC0 (HID-SPI) Enabled only (with WoT Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458338 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S0ix, while running test traffic through THC0 port which configured as HID-SPI interface with WoT enabled.
>         (Note: 'N' iterations of S0ix Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)    
>
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of SoC/PCH S0ix entry/exit flow, THC-IP's D3 + PG entry/exit flow, and THC Controller + TIC device re-initialization sequences by SW Driver.
>   - It will also assess and exercise HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on THC0 port to ensure robust touch operations between the host controller and TIC device.   
>   - Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This S0ix sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. S0ix Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration :
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> Wake on Touch Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Enable Low Power S0 Idle Capability
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO  Configurations -> OS IDLE Mode, S0ix Auto Demotion -> Enabled
>   - Go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> LPM S0i2.0 / S0i2.1 / S0i2.2  -> Enabled
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - Pre-Test or Sanity Check
>   - Manually enter sleep state by clicking on Power -> Sleep in Ubuntu OS.
>   - Wait awhile for the system to completely enter sleep/S0ix state, i.e., check for postcode = 01C5.
>   - Dump residency counter script in PythonSV to check and verify that the residency for each S0ix substate are progressively increasing over time.
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
>   - Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
>
>   - In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
>   - Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
>
>   - In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S0ix with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
>   - Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
>   - Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
>   - Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
>   - Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
>   - Specify Start Delay Time. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>
>   - Specify total sleep time before system wake back up. (e.g. 120 seconds)
>   - Specify total Active Time in WinOS before starting next PM cycle. (e.g. 60 seconds)
>   - Select the "S0ix" button. It will shown as green box once clicked, and the "S0ix|sec:120|sec:60|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set. 
>   - Specify S0ix Residency Threshold percentage to be met after completed each S0ix PM cycled. (e.g. 50%)
>   - Select S0ix Fail Level (e.g. S0i2-2). Note: Solar will verify that the system meets or exceeds the configured S0ix Residency Threshold percentage after completed each S0ix PM cycled.
>   - Specify which die to check for the S0ix residency. (e.g. SOC or PCH)
>   - Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
>
> ( Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>   - Wait for the S0ix cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
>
>   - NGA Testline for S0ix Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project :  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/d31e5241-c959-402d-aaa0-9769b08396f5 
>   - If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC0 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>   - THC PG + CG features Enabled via soft-straps and reflected in THC-IP registers. 
>   - Requirement: THC IP allows SOC/PCH to enter S0i2.0 state, when IP is in D0i2 + PG state.
>   - Requirement: For SOC/PCH to entry into S0i2.1 and S0i2.2 state, IP must be in D3 + PG state.
> New S0ix Mapping for THC
> Requirement
>  | 
> S0i2.0
>  | 
> S0i2.1
>  | 
> S0i2.2
>  | 
> THC Power Gated
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> THC in D3
>  | 
> Not Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
> Required
>  | 
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S0ix PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
>   - Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on both THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickSPI).
>   - Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch devices connected on THC0 still working normally after each S0ix cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S0ix cycling count with error code returned, 
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on any THC port, and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S0ix. (i.e. PIO and DMA operation)
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - System can reliably enter S0ix state and show residency counters increment over time.
>   - Verify driver stack and enumeration are correct for all critical peripherals (THC touch panel), with no yellow-bang errors through OS and kernel logs.
>   - Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S0ix cycle
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver
