### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC0 (HID-I2C) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458379 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through a single port THC0 that configured as HID-I2C interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458380 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through both THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently, both ports are configured as HID-I2C interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0/THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC0 (HID-I2C) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458381 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through THC0 port that configured as HID-I2C interface and THC1 port configured as HID-SPI interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C/SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c/spi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c/spi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C/SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C, QuickSPI).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C/SPI touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C/SPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>

### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC0 (HID-SPI) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458382 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through a single port THC0 that configured as HID-SPI interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC0 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch device connected on THC0 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458383 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through THC0 port that configured as HID-SPI interface and THC1 port configured as HID-I2C interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI/I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi/i2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi/i2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0/THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI/I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickSPI, QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI/I2C touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI/I2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

| Field | Value |

| **ID** | 13013458384 |

| **Owner** | willychi |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through both THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently, both ports are configured as HID-SPI interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.

> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location)

> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickSPI).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)

> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.

### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458385 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through a single port THC1 that configured as HID-I2C interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC1 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>

>

### ✅ [TC] S5 PM Cycling with THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458386 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically S5, while running test traffic through a single port THC1 that configured as HID-SPI interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of S5 PM Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS / SW Driver loading and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• This S5 sequence will also implicitly covered the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS, i.e. Sx Entry/Exit Flow. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
> 	• Go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Settings -> Wake System from S5 via RTC -> Enabled
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0     #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of S5 with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit on Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login in Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional).
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wake the system back to Ubuntu OS state. (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "S5" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "S5|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box on the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM regression/cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the S5 cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD) ]
> 	• NGA Testline for S5 Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project:  https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> 	(Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of S5 PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch device connected on THC1 still working normally after each S5 cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted S5 cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after S5. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the S5 cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of S5 cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Confirm that the platform resumes from every S5 cycle with the THC touch panel correctly re-enumerated, with proper kernel driver loaded and NO yellow bang errors.
> 	• All BIOS, OS and HW pre-requisites are validated before initiating deep power transitions, preventing false negatives.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-S4 cycle.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC0  Function Disable Flow (BWG + HAS) and check PG/CGs

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458261 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify and ensure function disable flow initiated by BIOS
>      on THC0 port is in compliance with the THC HAS
>      specifications/requirements.
>
>   - This test can be carried out
>      on the RVP either on UEFI shell or WinOS environment. 
>
>   - THC-IP Requirements for
>      "Function Disable" according to HAS:   
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be disabled in BIOS
>      settings.   
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> THC Port Configuration
>      ->  <None>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to
>      UEFI shell with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS
>      configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>   - In PythonSV, use show or search CLI functions/commands
>      to find and read the current value for relevant register namednodes, as
>      shown in below. (e.g. using MTL-P682 RVP as an example)
>   -  According to THC HAS, BIOS will initiate a function disable on THC0 port by configuring the registers in the following sequence:
> ﻿i. Verify all clock gating controls enabled on THC registers
>
> In :  soc.south.showsearch('thc_sb_.*_c.._cfg')
> thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pr_cdc_cfg = 0xccc0
> thc0.pvt.thc_sb_sd_cdc_cfg = 0xccc0
> thc0.pvt.thc_sb_ssc_clk_cfg = 0x1
> thc0.pvt.thc_sb_rosc_clk_cfg = 0x1
> thc1.pvt.thc_sb_pr_cdc_cfg = 0xccc0
> thc1.pvt.thc_sb_sd_cdc_cfg = 0xccc0
> thc1.pvt.thc_sb_ssc_clk_cfg = 0x1
> thc1.pvt.thc_sb_rosc_clk_cfg = 0x1
>
> In :  soc.south.showsearch('thc_sb_dcge_cfg')
> thc0.pvt.thc_sb_dcge_cfg = 0x3f
> thc1.pvt.thc_sb_dcge_cfg = 0x3f
>
> ii. Verify that the device reset signal asserted low. Device reset signal level can be checked via "GPIOTxState" register bit on the THC_SPI/I2C_RST GPIO pin. [Note: Ensure PMODE is configured to 0 (non-native GPIO mode) with GPIOTxDis set to 0]
> In [84]: # THC0: SPI_RST# (for MTL-P682 RVP)
> In [86]: soc.south.gpio.com1.sb.pad_cfg_dw0_xxgpp_e_6.show
> -------> soc.south.gpio.com1.sb.pad_cfg_dw0_xxgpp_e_6.show()
> 0x00000001 : padrstcfg (31:30) -- \n                    [p]This register controls which reset is used to reset the specified Pad Register fields.  It is the SoC responsibilities to decide the number of confi...
> 0x00000000 : rxpadstsel (29:29) -- \n                    [p]Determine from which node the RX pad state for native function should be source from.[/p]\n                    [p]This field only affects the pad s...
> 0x00000000 : rxraw1 (28:28) -- \n                    [p]This bit determines if the selected pad state is being overridden to '1'.  This field only makes sense when the buffer is configured as an input in eit...
> 0x00000000 : naf_vwe (27:27) -- \n                    [p]This bit enables Native Function IOSF-SB Virtual wire message generation.  Upon the enabling of this bit, the virtual wire message is sent to the appr...
> 0x00000002 : rxevcfg (26:25) -- \n                    [p]Determine if the internal RX pad state (RXPadStSel=1) should be passed on to the next logic stage as is, as a pulse, or level signal.  This field does...
> 0x00000000 : pregfrxsel (24:24) -- \n                    [p]Determine if the synchronized version of the raw RX pad state should be subjected to glitch filter or not.  [/p]\n                    [p]This field...
> 0x00000000 : rxinv (23:23) -- \n                    [p]This bit determines if the selected pad state should go through the polarity inversion stage.  This field only makes sense when the RX buffer is configu...
> 0x00000000 : rxtxencfg (22:21) -- \n                    [p]This controls the RX and TX buffer enables for the function selected by Pad Mode, but is not applicable when Pad Mode is 0 (i.e. GPIO mode).  [/p]\n...
> 0x00000000 : gpiroutioxapic (20:20) -- \n                    [p]Determine if the pad can be routed to cause peripheral IRQ when configured in GPIO input mode.  If the pad is not configured in GPIO input mode...
> 0x00000000 : gpiroutsci (19:19) -- \n                    [p]Determine if the pad can be routed to cause SCI when configured in GPIO input mode.  If the pad is not configured in GPIO input mode, this field ha...
> 0x00000000 : gpiroutsmi (18:18) -- \n                    [p]Determine if the pad can be routed to cause SMI when configured in GPIO input mode.  If the pad is not configured in GPIO input mode, this field ha...
> 0x00000000 : gpiroutnmi (17:17) -- \n                    [p]Determine if the pad can be routed to cause NMI when configured in GPIO input mode.  If the pad is not configured in GPIO input mode, this field ha...
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_0 (16:14) -- Reserved
> 0x00000000 : pmode (13:10) -- \n                    [p]This field determines whether the Pad is controlled by GPIO controller logic or one of the native functions muxed onto the Pad.[/p]\n                   ...
> 0x00000001 : gpiorxdis (09:09) -- \n                   [p] RX buffer enable control when PMode = 0 ONLY.  No effect when the pad in native mode.[/p]\n                    0 = Enable the input buffer (active l...
> 0x00000000 : gpiotxdis (08:08) -- \n                    [p] TX buffer enable control when PMode = 0 ONLY.  No effect when the pad in native mode.[/p]\n                    0 = Enable the output buffer (active...
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_1 (07:02) -- Reserved
> 0x00000000 : gpiorxstate (01:01) -- \n                    [p]This is the current internal RX pad state after Glitch Filter logic stage and is not affected by PMode and RXINV, hardware debouncer (if any) sett...
> 0x00000000 : gpiotxstate (00:00) -- \n                    [p] TX state in when PMode = 0 ONLY.  No effect when the pad in native mode.[/p]\n                    0 = Drive a level '0' to the TX output pad[br]\...
>
> Alternatively, observe that THC_RST# signal will be asserted low as expected from the HID-SPI/I2C LA trace.
>
> iii. Verify the HAE bit in THC_CFG_PCE Space is set to 1.
>
> soc.south.thc0.cfg.thc_cfg_pce.show()
> In :  soc.south.thc0.cfg.thc_cfg_pce.show
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_31_6 (31:06) -- Reserved.
> 0x00000001 : hae (05:05) -- If set, then the PGCB may request a PG whenever it is idle.[br]\nThis bit is loaded with the value from the Power...
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_4 (04:04) -- Reserved.
> 0x00000001 : se (03:03) -- if clear, then IP will never asset Sleep to the retention flops. If set, then IP may assert Sleep during PG'ing. N...
> 0x00000000 : d3he (02:02) -- if set, then IP will PG when idle and the PMCSR[1:0] register in the IP = 11.
> 0x00000000 : i3e (01:01) -- if set, then IP will PG when idle and the D0i3 register (D0i3C[2] = 1) is set.
> 0x00000000 : spe (00:00) -- Software PowerGate Enable: If this bit is set, the IP will PG when pmc_sw_pg_req_b =0. NOTE: PMCSR[1:0] must not ...
>
> iv. Verify the THC0 function is set into D3 power state.
> In :  soc.south.thc0.showsearch('pwrst', 'f')
> cfg.thc_cfg_pmd_pmcsrbse_pmcsr.pwrst = 0x3
>
> In :  soc.south.psf.showsearch('thc0')
> psf8.psf_8_agnt_t0_shdw_bar0_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x0
> psf8.psf_8_agnt_t0_shdw_bar1_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x0
> psf8.psf_8_agnt_t0_shdw_pcien_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x100
> psf8.psf_8_agnt_t0_shdw_pmcsr_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x3          # THC0 D3 Status reflected on PSF8
> psf8.psf_8_agnt_t0_shdw_cfg_dis_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x0
> psf8.psf_8_t0_agent_function_config_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x100
> psf8.psf_8_agent_tgt_rootspace_thc0_rs0_d16_f0_offset30 = 0x0
>
> v. Verify the THC0 function disable bit in THC_CFG_UR_STS_CTL.FD is set to 1. (Note: When FD=1, THC will respond with UR for all IOSF primary non-posted cycles to THC and drop all incoming IOSF primary posted cycles.)
> In :  soc.south.thc0.showsearch('^fd$', 'f')
> cfg.thc_cfg_ur_sts_ctl.fd = 0x1
>
> vi. The THC shall enter PG by this stage. (Note: The entry sequence is the same to D3 state entry)
> # Import PM Test Script to check all SOC's IP CLK-Gating(CG) and PWR-Gating(PG) Status 
>
> from meteorlake.vjt.pm import cgpg 
>
> # Check and print out the current IP PG Status on SOC die
>
> cgpg.print_soc_all_ip_pg_sts()
>
> # Check and print out the current IP CG/CLKREQ Status on SOC die
>
> cgpg.print_socpll_pcg_clkreq_status()
>
>   - [Additional
> Verification]: Use Intel Trace-Hub tool to check the VISA signal states as
> shown in below for THC0/THC1.
>
> 5. Host
> reset is the only way to exit from the function disabled state on THC, as the
> FD bit will be cleared after the reset exit. After system reset, make sure to
> check the register bit is cleared to 0. (Note:
> just right before BIOS attempting to set this bit again in the next
> reboot/reset cycle)
>
> In :  soc.south.thc0.showsearch('^fd$','f')
> cfg.thc_cfg_ur_sts_ctl.fd
> = 0x0
> 6. Repeat
> steps #2 to #4 again for two more attempts to ensure the consistency of the
> test outcomes.
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell or Windows OS.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - (Optional) Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the THC-SPI/I2C GPIO signal states for debug/analysis
>      purposes.
>
>   - (Optional) Intel Trace Hub
>      tool for VISA signal analysis
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - BIOS correctly configured all
>      the relevant registers involved in function disable flow as specified in
>      the test steps above. 
>
>   - [Additional/Stretch-Goal]:
>      VISA signals states reflected correctly as expected as shown in the figure
>      above. 
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Verify THC enters D3 and PG states after FD bit is set,
>      confirm the FD bit behavior as the following:
>
>
>   - FD=1 ->
>       THC responds with UR for non-posted IOSF cycles and drops posted cycles.
>
>   - FD clears
>       only after host reset.
>
>
>
>   - Validate
>      debug visibility via Trace-Hub VISA signals for THC state.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      BIOS re-applies FD bit after reset.
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC0 (HID-I2C) BDF and DID Assignments

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458266 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>
>   - The objective / intent of
>      this test is to verify and ensure the Vendor/Device ID values are valid
>      and correctly mapped to its' corresponding hardcoded BDF for both THC0 and
>      THC1 ports on RVP, which can be verified in UEFI Shell, Windows' Device
>      Manager, or THC register check via PythonSV.
>
>   - E.g. Using NVL-PCH-S (a.k.a
>      NVP-S) as example for this instance: According to Chap06_NVP-S_RegMaps, the following table listed
>      the PCI global device IDs and BDF for NVP-S :
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - In
>      BIOS setting, enable both THC0 and THC1 ports, go to Intel Advance Menu
>      -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->
>
>   - THC
>      Port Configuration -> <THC0> , and, THC Mode <HID over I2C or
>      HID over SPI>    ## To enable THC0 port
>
>   - THC
>      Port Configuration -> <THC1> , and, THC Mode <HID over I2C or
>      HID over SPI>    ## To enable THC1 port
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
> Ubuntu OS
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to UEFI shell with all the HW requirements met with
>      all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test
>      Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Boot the system to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Enter "lspci" command via PuTTY to print out a
>      complete list of devices enabled by BIOS.
>
>   - Look for "Digitizer Pen" device name from
>      the print-out PCI device list. Using NVL as example for this
>      instance:
>
>           THC0 port -> Bus:0, Device: 16 (0x10h), Function: 0, Device ID for
>      HID-SPI -> d349
>
>           THC1 port -> Bus:0, Device: 8 (0x08h), Function: 0, Device ID for
>      HID-SPI -> d34b
>
>   - Then, enter "lspci -kvvv -s 00:10.0"
>      command to check for device bus device function details. [Note:
>      Enter desire BDF in highlighted part]
>
>   - To check for BDF and Vendor/Device ID, it
>      can be checked via PuTTY with following command: "lspci -nn
>      -s 00:08.0" [Note:
>      Enter desire BDF in highlighted part]
>
>   - Alternatively, both
>      BDF, and Vendor/Device ID can be checked via register namednodes in
>      PythonSV (as shown below). 
>
> Windows OS (Non-POR)
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to UEFI shell with all the HW requirements met with
>      all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test
>      Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Enter "pci" command to print out a complete list of
>      devices enabled by BIOS.
>
>   - Look for "Input Devices - Digitizer (pen)" device name
>      from the print-out PCI device list. Using PTL as example for this
>      instance:
>
>           THC0 port -> Bus:0, Device: 16 (0x10h), Function: 0, Device ID for
>      HID-I2C -> E348 or E448, HID-SPI -> E349 or E449
>
>           THC1 port -> Bus:0, Device: 16 (0x10h), Function: 0, Device ID for
>      HID-I2C -> E34A or E44A, HID-SPI -> E34B or E44B
>
>           For reference, below screenshot is taken
>      from MTL-P RVP with a different Device ID.
>
>
>   - Finally, boot the
>      system to Windows OS. Go to Device Manager, and click on "Intel
>      QuickSPI/I2C Host Controller" properties to check for Vendor/Device
>      ID with its' respective PCI BDF mapped location. 
>
>           For reference, below screenshot is taken
>      from MTL-P RVP with a different Device ID.
>
>
>   - Alternatively, both BDF, and
>      Vendor/Device ID can be checked via register namednodes in PythonSV (as
>      shown below). 
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - Install
>      SW HID-SPI/-I2C Driver on Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C driver)
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to UEFI/WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for
>      both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria :
>
>
>   - Both UEFI Shell and Windows' Device Manager able to
>      reflect the valid Vendor/Device ID values and mapped to respective BDF for
>      both THC0 and THC1 ports, as required according to HAS.
>
>   - In PythonSV, register
>      namednodes verification able to reflect the correct values as well. 
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria :
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above, i.e.
>      incorrect/invalid BDF, Vendor or Device ID. 
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Validate the BDF, Vendor and Device ID match the
>      expected values from THC design specification.
>
>   - Ensure THC hardware is detected with correct identifiers
>      during system boot and after device initialization in OS.
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver
