### ✅ [TC] THC0 (HID-SPI) BDF and DID Assignments

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458267 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective / intent of this test is to verify and ensure the Vendor/Device ID values are valid and correctly mapped to its' corresponding hardcoded BDF for both THC0 and THC1 ports on RVP, which can be verified in UEFI Shell, Windows' Device Manager, or THC register check via PythonSV.
>   - E.g. Using NVL-PCH-S (a.k.a NVP-S) as example for this instance: According to Chap06_NVP-S_RegMaps, the following table listed the PCI global device IDs and BDF for NVP-S :
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - In BIOS setting, enable both THC0 and THC1 ports, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->
>   - THC Port Configuration -> <THC0> , and, THC Mode <HID over I2C or HID over SPI>    ## To enable THC0 port
>   - THC Port Configuration -> <THC1> , and, THC Mode <HID over I2C or HID over SPI>    ## To enable THC1 port
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> Ubuntu OS
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to UEFI shell with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>   - Boot the system to Ubuntu OS.
>   - Enter "lspci" command via PuTTY to print out a complete list of devices enabled by BIOS.
>   - Look for "Digitizer Pen" device name from the print-out PCI device list. Using NVL as example for this instance:
> THC0 port -> Bus:0, Device: 16 (0x10h), Function: 0, Device ID for HID-SPI -> d349
> THC1 port -> Bus:0, Device: 8 (0x08h), Function: 0, Device ID for HID-SPI -> d34b
>   - Then, enter "lspci -kvvv -s 00:10.0" command to check for device bus device function details. [Note: Enter desire BDF in highlighted part]
>   - To check for BDF and Vendor/Device ID, it can be checked via PuTTY with following command: "lspci -nn -s 00:08.0" [Note: Enter desire BDF in highlighted part]
>   - Alternatively, both BDF, and Vendor/Device ID can be checked via register namednodes in PythonSV (as shown below). 
>
> Windows OS (Non-POR)
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to UEFI shell with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>   - Enter "pci" command to print out a complete list of devices enabled by BIOS.
>   - Look for "Input Devices - Digitizer (pen)" device name from the print-out PCI device list. Using PTL as example for this instance:
> THC0 port -> Bus:0, Device: 16 (0x10h), Function: 0, Device ID for HID-I2C -> E348 or E448, HID-SPI -> E349 or E449
> THC1 port -> Bus:0, Device: 16 (0x10h), Function: 0, Device ID for HID-I2C -> E34A or E44A, HID-SPI -> E34B or E44B
> For reference, below screenshot is taken from MTL-P RVP with a different Device ID.
>
>   - Finally, boot the system to Windows OS. Go to Device Manager, and click on "Intel QuickSPI/I2C Host Controller" properties to check for Vendor/Device ID with its' respective PCI BDF mapped location. 
> For reference, below screenshot is taken from MTL-P RVP with a different Device ID.
>
>   - Alternatively, both BDF, and Vendor/Device ID can be checked via register namednodes in PythonSV (as shown below). 
>
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/-I2C Driver on Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI or QuickI2C driver)
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI/WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>   - Both UEFI Shell and Windows' Device Manager able to reflect the valid Vendor/Device ID values and mapped to respective BDF for both THC0 and THC1 ports, as required according to HAS.
>   - In PythonSV, register namednodes verification able to reflect the correct values as well. 
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above, i.e. incorrect/invalid BDF, Vendor or Device ID. 
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Validate the BDF, Vendor and Device ID match the expected values from THC design specification.
>   - Ensure THC hardware is detected with correct identifiers during system boot and after device initialization in OS.
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC0 D0i2 Entry/Exit Flow (D0-->D0i2-->D0) and check PG/CGs (Allow SUT/RVP to enter S0i2.0)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458257 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to assess and verify THC0 meets the platform power management
>      requirements and aligns with architecture specifications for low-power
>      states.
>
>   - D0i2 entry can help to verify
>      that the controller properly transitions when touch panel is idle to save
>      power.
>
>   - When touch event happens, the
>      D0i2 exit helps to ensure that system wakes up without latency or missed
>      gestures.
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled
>      in BIOS settings (Note: It can be with I2C interface or
>      SPI interface)
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel
>      Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration
>      ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below
>      to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Setup Menu, follow and configure all
>      the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>   - For
>      MTL-S/MTP-S VISA tracing on ITH tool , user will need to enable the
>      Platform Debug Consent to <Enabled - Trace Active> in BIOS
>      settings. (i.e. Intel Advance Menu --> Debug
>      Settings)
>
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS
>      Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take
>      effect.
>
>   - Verify touch panel is able to touch and leave the touch panel idle
>      (i.e. no touch activity) for a few seconds to enter D0i2 state.
>
>   - Modify the D0i2 entry timer register value in PythonSV to have
>      better visual and capture on D0i2 state transition between entry and exit (e.g. pch.thc1.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.d0i2_entry_timer = 0x9  
>        ## .getspec() for more information on the selection of timer
>      configuration available)
>
>
>   - To trigger D0i2 exit, simply touch the panel, observe
>      the ITH tool for state transition.
>
>
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>
>   - ITH tool to
>      check the D0i2 entry and exit state transition. Press the "Update All
>      Signal State" or  "Get Signal State", user can see
>      from "L" to "H" or vice-versa between D0i2 entry and
>      exit transition.
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to
>      enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0 touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location)
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according
>      to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - For D0i2 Entry, both PG_REQ_b and PG_ACK_b signals will be asserted
>      to LOW state; whereas, both FET_EN_b and FET_EN_ACK_b will be de-asserted
>      to HIGH state.
>
>   - For D0i2 Exit, both PG_REQ_b and PG_ACK_b signals will be
>      de-asserted to HIGH state; whereas, both FET_EN_b and FET_EN_ACK_b will be
>      asserted to LOW state.
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable
>      to meet any of the passing criteria above
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Validate that
>      the THC IP controller transitions correctly between D0i2 entry and exit
>      states
>
>   - Use ITH tool to
>      monitor the signal changes during idle-triggered entry and touch-triggered
>      exit
>
>   - Verify that the
>      observed transitions align with expected behaviour define in the
>      architecture specification
>
>
>   - According to https://docs.intel.com/documents/pch_uarch_doc/MTL/MAS/THC_D0_S0ix.html , the figure
>       below shows the complete flow for the D0i2 Entry requirement.
>
>
> THC D0i2/S0ix
> Entry Sequence
>
>
>
> THC D0i2/S0ix
> Exit Sequence
>
>
>
>
>   - PCR:
>        https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article/#/1409894130
>
>   - Summary:
>       From MTL+ onwards, allow S0ix with THC in D0i2, i.e. system S0i2.0
>      substate entry is allowed once THC entered D0i2.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC0 D3 Entry/Exit Flow (D0-->D3-->D0) and check PG/CGs  (Allow SUT/RVP S0i2.1/S0i2.2)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458259 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The
>      objective/intent of this test is to ensure both THC0 and THC1 can perform
>      D3 entry and exit.
>
>   - This will be carried out
>      using touch panel specifically on the platform, connected via the THC0
>      port.
>
>   - Expectation
>      is that the SW driver will have the capability to perform D3 entry/exit on
>      both THC ports when system entering/exiting S0ix.
>
>   - If THC
>      entered and maintained at D3 power state, SoCWatch should capture D3
>      residency increases over time.   
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Setup Menu, follow and
>      configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test
>      Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Use SoCWatch
>      tool to capture the D3 residency on THC enabled port(s).
>
>   - On RVP/SUT,
>      press the PWRBTN for system to enter CS state. (Note: expect the postcode transition from:0000 -> 00C5 ->
>      01C5, in "01C5" system fully entered CS/S0ix state)
>
>   - In PythonSV,
>      perform read to both THC's "pwrst" and PMC's
>      "thc0_d3_sts" register bits, should obtained "pwrst =
>      0x3" and "thc0_d3_sts = 0x1" which indicates THC0 has
>      entered D3 power state.
>
>   - Leave the
>      system to be idle for at least 3 minutes.
>
>   - Press PWRBTN
>      to wake the system from CS state. (Note: expect the
>      postcode transition from: 01C5 -> ABC5, in "ABC5" system wake
>      from CS/S0ix state)
>
>   - In PythonSV,
>      perform read to both THC's "pwrst" and PMC's
>      "thc0_d3_sts" register bits again, should obtained "pwrst =
>      0x0" and "thc0_d3_sts = 0x0" which indicates THC0 has
>      entered D0 power state. 
>
>   - Stop the
>      SoCWatch capture and analyze the log, expect to get high D3 residency on
>      THC0.
>
> SW
> Requirements :
>
>
>   - SoCWatch Tool
>
> HW
> Requirements :
>
>
>   - RVP with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface on
>      THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0 touch port/connector.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies :
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria :
>
>
>   - Able to
>      perform D3 entry and exit on THC0 port as expected.
>
>   - When perform
>      SoCWatch capture for at least 3 minutes (or more), a high D3 residency of
>      at least >70% on THC0 port is expected.
>
>
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria :
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Validate that
>      the THC IP controller transitions correctly between D3 entry and exit
>
>   - Use SoCWatch
>      tool to monitor the D3 residency on THC port
>
>   - Verify that the
>      observed transitions align with expected behaviour 
>
>
>   - For reference,
>
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - No specific configuration needed.
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - Execute bootscript in PythonSV to bring up RVP to post soc/PCD bootstall state/breakpoint, where both Fuses and THC0 register spaces should become accessible at this stage.
>   - The bootscript is expected to halt/hook to the specified boot breakpoint, i.e. means that the system will not continue booting further past this breakpoint.
>   - In PythonSV, by using show/searching CLI commands below to find the relevant PythonSV namednodes (both THC and Fuses).
>   - Then, check and compare the output results with HAS and Harness defined configurations.
> Example below is taken/captured from PTL PythonSV Targ-Sim Model:  
>
> # Load Fuse RAM before reading to fuse/softstraps registers
> In [9]: soc.south.fuses.load_fuse_ram()
> Loading fuse ram state from hardware...
> Decoding fuse ram string...
>
> In [10]: soc.fuses.showsearch('thc')
> thc0_fuse.thc_reserved_fuse = 0x0
> thc0_fuse.virtual.thc_reserved_fuse_df = 0x0
> thc0_fuse.soft_straps.thc_reserved_softstrap = 0x0
> thc1_fuse.thc_reserved_fuse = 0x0
> thc1_fuse.virtual.thc_reserved_fuse_df = 0x0
> thc1_fuse.soft_straps.thc_reserved_softstrap = 0x0
> pmc.thc0_fuse_dis = 0x0
> pmc.thc1_fuse_dis = 0x0
> pmc.virtual.thc0_fuse_dis_df = 0x0
> pmc.virtual.thc1_fuse_dis_df = 0x0
> isclk.virtual.cmu_three_thc_en_hysteresis_3_0 = 0xe             # ignore
> isclk.virtual.cmu_three_thc_ack_hysteresis_3_0 = 0x3            # ignore
> isclk.virtual.cmuug_misc_cfg_ck_thc_req = 0x0                   # ignore
> isclk.virtual.cmuug_misc_ck_thc_modectrl = 0x0                  # ignore
>
>
> In [43]: soc.fuses.thc0_fuse.soft_straps.show
> -------> soc.fuses.thc0_fuse.soft_straps.show()
> 0x0000000000000000 : clockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : pgcbclockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : powergateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : thc_reserved_softstrap
>
> In [44]: soc.fuses.thc1_fuse.soft_straps.show
> -------> soc.fuses.thc1_fuse.soft_straps.show()
> 0x0000000000000000 : clockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : pgcbclockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : powergateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : thc_reserved_softstrap
>
> In [16]: for port in ['thc0','thc1']:
>     ...:     print("\n" + port)
>     ...:     thc_fuse = eval(f"soc.{port}.search('fuse')")
>     ...:     for path_idx in thc_fuse:
>     ...:         print(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:         eval(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:
>     ...:     thc_strap = eval(f"soc.{port}.search('softstrap')")
>     ...:     for path_idx in thc_strap:
>     ...:         print(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:         eval(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:
>
> thc0
> soc.thc0.pvt.thc_fuses.show()
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_31_8 (31:08) (ro) -- Reserved Field
> 0x00000000 : thc_fuses (07:00) (ro/v) -- Reserved Fuses
>
> soc.thc0.pvt.thc_softstraps.show()
> 0x00000000 : thc_softstraps (31:03) (ro/v) -- Reserved Softstraps
> 0x00000000 : pgcbclockgateenable (02:02) (ro/v) -- Control default value for pgcb clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : clockgateenable (01:01) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : powergateenable (00:00) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal power gating related config registers.
>
> thc1
> soc.thc1.pvt.thc_fuses.show()
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_31_8 (31:08) (ro) -- Reserved Field
> 0x00000000 : thc_fuses (07:00) (ro/v) -- Reserved Fuses
>
> soc.thc1.pvt.thc_softstraps.show()
> 0x00000000 : thc_softstraps (31:03) (ro/v) -- Reserved Softstraps
> 0x00000000 : pgcbclockgateenable (02:02) (ro/v) -- Control default value for pgcb clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : clockgateenable (01:01) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : powergateenable (00:00) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal power gating related config registers.
>
>   - PythonSV print-out values for all the relevant THC0's Fuse/Softstraps must have the same configuration as stated in HAS and/or Harness.
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP in early-boot state whereby all Fuses' and THC0's register spaces are accessible [e.g. at Pre-BIOS state or even earlier via bootscript() method to boot to a certain breakpoint.]
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
> Dependencies:
>
>   - Bootscript utilize for early boot phase via PythonSV.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - All the Fuses/Softstraps configuration for THC0 perfectly matches the expectations outlined in the HAS and Harness requirements. 
>   - No missing Fuse/Softstrap register for THC0 in PythonSV.
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Cross-check each softstrap/fuse bit against the HAS configuration table.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ❌ [TC] THC0 Global Interrupt Enable/Disable sending Interrupt Upstream from THC to Host SW/Driver

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458277 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify and ensure that the enable/disable global interrupt
>      mechanism on THC0 port able to function normally as expected. 
>
>   - Expectation is that global
>      interrupt mechanism/feature will be fully integrated and utilized by
>      QuickSPI/QuickI2C SW Driver itself.
>
>   - Therefore, this test can be
>      implicitly covered using the SW driver in normal flow and use cases.
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS
>      settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to
>      Ubuntu OS/WinOS with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS
>      configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>   - On SUT/RVP, enable and setup the driver ETL tracing
>      (e.g. via TraceView tool) to capture the incoming input reports/packets on
>      THC0 port.
>
>   - On host, use ITH tool to perform ODLA or VISA2PIN
>      tracing to capture the two internal-IP VISA signals shown below (to
>      capture any incoming MSI interrupts when touch event occur):
>
> From:  (TIC Device interrupt
> synchronized on PGCB clock)
>
> /thc_top/i_thc_ugd_wrapper_top/thc_crpm_top/thc_cdc_pgcb_wrapper_inst/interrupt_clkreq  
>
> To: (MSI Upstream transmission request on
> IOSF Primary)
>
> /thc_top/i_thc_pgd_wrapper_top/i_tsch_parb/i_thc_ilb/i_thc_msi_init_blk/ilb_parb_cavail
>
>
>   - Perform test and verify the following scenarios in
>      below:
>
>           (Important Note: SW driver may take over control and override the
>      GBL_INT_EN bit when testing out the scenarios below) -> [William]: Need to confirm on this during PO?
>
>
>
> Scenario A: When
> GBL_INT_EN bit is 1 (Enabled by default)
>
> (a) In
> PythonSV, enable global interrupt feature on THC0 port by programming the
> following register bit, and read back this register to verify the configured
> value: 
>
>
>      soc.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_int_en.gbl_int_en
> = 1 
>
> (b) Verify and validate the following the expected outcome in
> below: 
>
> Expected outcome:
>
>
>   - When touch event occurred (i.e. THC received input
>      report from TiC), THC HW will send any MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW.
>
>
>   - Observed
>      internal signal toggling/assertion on "ilb_parb_cavail" (VISA full path:
>      /thc_top/i_thc_pgd_wrapper_top/i_tsch_parb/i_thc_ilb/i_thc_msi_init_blk/ilb_parb_cavail) 
>
>
>
> Scenario B: When
> GBL_INT_EN bit is 0 (Disabled)
>
> (a) In
> PythonSV, disable global interrupt feature on THC0 port by programming the
> following register bit, and read back this register to verify the configured
> value: 
>
>
>      soc.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_int_en.gbl_int_en
> = 0 
>
> (b) Verify and validate the following the expected outcome
> in below: 
>
> Expected outcome:
>
>
>   - When touch event occurred (i.e. THC received input
>      report from TiC), THC HW will not send any MSI/Legacy line interrupt to
>      SW. -> [William]:
>      Does this SW driver still work in this case ? 
>
>
>   - No
>      signal toggling/assertion observed on "ilb_parb_cavail" -> [William]: Need
>      to confirm on this during PO? 
>
>
>
> Scenario C: When
> GBL_INT_EN bit transition from 0->1
>
> (a) Attempt test scenario B first. And, then follow-up by test
> scenario A. 
>
> (b) Verify and validate the following the expected outcome
> in below: 
>
> Expected outcome:
>
>
>   - THC HW shall check all pending interrupt sources
>      (NDDI_STS, EOF_INT_STS, error interrupts, etc.) prior to GBL_INT_EN
>      bit enablement (?) ,
>
>   - And, after global interrupt enabled, THC
>      HW will generate a new MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW if there is
>      any pending interrupt source (from prior GBL_INT_EN enabled? ). 
>
>
>   - Repeat same steps #2 to #4 for the other test scenarios
>      above. And finally, verify whether the results meet the expected outcome
>      or not.
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - Install
>      SW HID-SPI/I2C Driver on Ubuntu OS/Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C
>      driver for THC0 port)
>
>   - For
>      driver logging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log
>      files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-SPI/I2C traffic/data payloads,
>      interrupt rates/frequencies, and error/failure logging.
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - Acute logic analyzer to
>      monitor the HID-SPI/I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - Optional: Custom rework
>      required to wire-out all the THC-SPI/I2C IO pins for Acute LA signals
>      tracing. (Note: Make sure to use single-core wires and keep the
>      length as short as possible to avoid/minimize any signal integrity
>      impact.)
>
>   - VISA-to-pin setup + Acute LA
>      to analyze the HID-SPI/-I2C traffic together with the THC-IP VISA signals.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.  
>
>   - ODLA via Intel TraceHub Tool, or VISA2PIN setup with
>      Acute LA. 
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Enable/disable
>      global interrupt feature on THC0 port able to work and function as
>      expected as stated in this TC's test steps above.
>
>   - QuickSPI/QuickI2C
>      SW Driver is used to implicit cover the enable/disable global
>      interrupt flow.
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above. 
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Validate that the ETL tracing is
>      correctly enabled on the SUT/RVP and able to capture input reports/packets
>      on THC0/THC1 ports. 
>
>   - Verify the MSI interrupts are captured
>      via ITH tool and the behaviour matched as expected.
>
>   - Verify and check back the register to
>      ensure configuration matches the intended test scenario (enabled,
>      disabled, or transition).
>
>   - Cross-check the ETL trace logs, VISA
>      signal captures, and register readbacks to ensure the observed HW/SW
>      behaviour matches the expected outcomes.
>
>   - Repeat the verification steps for both
>      THC0 and THC1 ports, and all relevant scenarios to ensure consistent and
>      robust validation. 
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDI2C (Fast-Mode/400kHz) Touch Device Enabling+Functional in OS [MSI Interrupt]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458228 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify
>      and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully
>      operational with HID-I2C SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS
>      environment.
>
>   - This will be carried out
>      using touch panel specifically connected via THC0 port on
>      current project's platform.
>
>   - This test is designed to
>      implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-I2C SW driver
>      loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device
>      initialization/enumeration sequences.
>
>   - It will also assess and
>      exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC0 port
>      to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC
>      device.
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be
>      enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS menu, go to
>      Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration
>      -> <Refer to the BKM settings below
>      to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure : 
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to
>      Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>      the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting
>      BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to
>      take effect.
>
>   - Check and verify the current version of touch panel's
>      firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team,
>      or by analyzing the HID-I2C Device Descriptor's payload
>      captured/traced from Acute LA.
>
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch
>      panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool
>      provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This
>      step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx,
>      PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's
>      finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the
>      device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>
>   - (Optional) Test with a touch pen (Note: It
>      should come together as a package with the touch device , if that
>      particular device supported touch pen feature.)
>
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on the QuickI2C device/driver (THC0 port) via PuTTY by the
>      following commands:
>
>
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail
>       kernel info on which device/slot)
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - Install Ubuntu-OS with built-in QuickI2C kernel driver
>      (i.e. QuickI2C driver for THC0 port)
>
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView
>      tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of
>      HID-I2C data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C
>      interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis
>      purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP
> schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are
>      fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations
>      perform as anticipated.
>
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings
>      occurred on the THC0 port's QuickI2C device/driver end (as verified in
>      PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process. 
>
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC0 port - QuickI2C
>      device/driver.
>
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not
>      working at all. 
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and
>      DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the
>      touch panel.
>
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which
>      caused by the touch events.
>
>
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm the
>      HID-I2C traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA
>      (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>
>   - Validate interrupt
>      frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C profile.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      QuickI2C driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>
>   - Validate logs
>      (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and
>      absence of error flags.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below
> is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC
> Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia -
> Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>
>   - BIOS version: 
>
>
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>
>
>   - OS kernel:
>
>
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later
> version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended
> solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>
>   - Driver versions: 
>
>
>   - Windows OS
>
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDI2C (Fast-Plus-Mode/1MHz) Touch Device Enabling+Functional in OS [MSI Interrupt]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458229 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully operational with HID-I2C SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically connected via THC0 port on current project's platform.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-I2C SW driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - Check and verify the current version of touch panel's firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team, or by analyzing the HID-I2C Device Descriptor's payload captured/traced from Acute LA.
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx, PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>   - (Optional) Test with a touch pen (Note: It should come together as a package with the touch device, if that particular device supported touch pen feature.)
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank occurred on the QuickI2C device/driver (THC0 port) via PuTTY by the following commands:
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail kernel info on which device/slot)
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - Install Ubuntu-OS with built-in QuickI2C kernel driver (i.e. QuickI2C driver for THC0 port)
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-I2C data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings occurred on the THC0 port's QuickI2C device/driver end (as verified in PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.    
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC0 port - QuickI2C device/driver.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working at all. 
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which caused by the touch events.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-I2C traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickI2C driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended
> solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

---

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully operational with HID-I2C SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically connected via THC0 port on current project's platform.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-I2C SW driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> Pre-Test Configuration:

>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx, PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)

>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-I2C data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:

> Test Pass Criteria:

>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings occurred on the THC0 port's QuickI2C device/driver end (as verified in PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.    
> Test Fail Criteria:
>

>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working at all. 
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.

>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C profile.

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDI2C GPIO Configuration (BWG + GPIO HAS Requirements)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458235 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description :
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to assess and verify the THC0-I2C interface GPIO register
>      configurations are done correctly as expected via BIOS initialization,
>      with I2C interface is enabled on THC0 port.
>
>   - THC HW will uses
>      I2C interfaces to communicate with touch device (TIC). The
>      I2C bus protocols used for touch operation are same as the legacy
>      I2C integrated touch.
>
>   - Without the correct GPIO
>      configurations, the touch operations of the touch devices will failed to
>      function normally as expected and deem un-usable.
>
>   - This test is also designed to
>      have coverage for the BIOS RCR below:  
>
>
>
>
> HSD Link
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> BIOS RCR Title
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Comments
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15012702523
>
>    | 
>
>
> Update IOSState
>   and IOSTerm for THC SPI GPIOs to improve D3/S0ix residency
>
>    | 
>
>
> This TC will check
>   and verify the BIOS configuration on both IOSState and IOSTerm register bits
>   in PAD_CFG_DW1.
>
>    | 
>
>
> The
> table below shown a complete list of GPIO with the expected register
> configurations once THC-I2C interface is enabled in BIOS settings.
> All the
> information tabulated in below are compiled based on the PTL_GPIO_Automation
> and PTL_RVP_GPIO_Platform_Mapping excel sheets.
>
> Note:
>
>
>   - With the exception on RST#
>      pin, due to native GPIO mode (pmode=0) is being used for this pin instead
>      of native THC mode. GPIO pin direction will need to configure to output
>      mode (gpiorxdis=1, gpiotxdis=0) as this GPIO pin will be utilized for
>      resetting the touch device.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Function Signal Name
>
>
> Touch/Human Interface Device (HID; incl.
>   THC-I2C )
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> GPIO Name
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> SIPFAM
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> PCD-P
>
>
>
>
>
> pkg pin
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> PCD-H
>
>
>
>

>
>
>

>    | 
>

>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Native Function
>
>
> (DW0.PMODE)
>
>
> 0 = Native GPIO mode
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> RxEvCfg
>
>
>
>
>
> (DW0.RXEVCFG)
>
>
> N/A = Default Value
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Pin Direction DW0.GPIO{Rx/Tx}Dis
>
>
> IN
>   - GPIORxDis=0, GPIOTxDis=1;
>
>
> OUT
>   - GPIORxDis=1, GPIOTxDis=0;
>
>
>
>
>
> HI_Z
>   - GPIORxDis=1, GPIOTxDis=1
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> IO Standby
>
>
>
>
>
> State
>
>
>
>
>
> (DW1.IOSSTATE)
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> IO Standby
>
>
>
>
>
> Termination
>
>
>
>
>
> (DW1.IOSTERM)
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>

>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled
>      in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel
>      Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration
>      ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below
>      to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to post-BIOS initialized state (i.e. BIOS Setup Menu,
>      UEFI Shell) with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS
>      configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>   - In PythonSV, check and
>      verify all the respective GPIO register bits are configured accordingly by
>      BIOS for each and every THC0-I2C's GPIO pins. Compare the register
>      output result with the table above.
>
> E.g. Using
> 'xxgpp_e_11' as an example, read/show the following register values: 
>
>
>   - Pad Ownership
>      (PAD_OWN):  
>
> [In]: soc.gpio.showsearch('xxgpp_e_11')
>
> [Out]: com1.sb.pad_own_xxgpp_e_11
> = 0x0
>
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW0:
>      Native Function (PMODE), RxEvCfg (RXEVCFG), GPIO Pin Direction
>      (GPIORXDIS/GPIOTXDIS): 
>
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW1: IO
>      Standby State (IOSSTATE), IO
>      Standby Termination (IOSTERM):
>
>
>   - Repeat the
>      same for the other THC0-I2C's GPIO pins/pads as listed in the table
>      above. 
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to
>      enable I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0 touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis
>      purposes.   
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - All the THC0-I2C's GPIO register bits
>      are correctly configured by BIOS as expected. (i.e. Same as table's
>      result) 
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing
>      criteria above.  
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Validate that
>      the THC register bits match the expected configuration based on the BIOS
>      knob settings
>
>   - Ensure the
>      register bits remain correct after system reboot (BIOS applied settings
>      permanently)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDI2C POR Touch Device Enabling+Functional (Pre-OS) using BIOS on-screen VKB support + Legacy Interrupt (Non-MSI) Mode

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458226 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities are operational in
>      the Pre-Ubuntu OS environment (including UEFI shell and BIOS Menu) using
>      the on-screen displayed virtual-keyboard (VKB) feature is enabled in BIOS.
>
>   - This will be carried out
>      using touch panel specifically on the platform, connected via the THC0
>      port.
>
>   - This test is designed to
>      implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, THC .EFI driver loading
>      which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration
>      sequences.
>
>   - It will also assess and
>      exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port
>      to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC
>      device.
>
>   - Soft keyboard
>      (or, a.k.a Virtual Keyboard - VKB) support is built on top of BIOS touch
>      support. VKB will become visible on touch panel display, once it is
>      enabled in the BIOS setting.
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>   - To enable VKB feature, go to BIOS
>      Menu:  Boot maintenance manager -> Boot configuration menu ->
>      Virtual Keyboard -> Enable
>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Setup
>      Menu, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described
>      in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting
>      BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to
>      take effect.
>
>   - After the SUT rebooted to BIOS Menu or UEFI shell, VKB
>      should appear and display on-screen of the touch panel.
>
>   - Perform touch gesture on-screen within the virtual
>      keyboard's white box boundary.
>
>   - Try to touch each and every key of the VKB displayed
>      on-screen to ensure all these keys are functioning correctly as expected.
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable
>      I2C interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0 touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
>
>   - To support Pre-OS touch
>      operations, BIOS must incorporate a UEFI driver that mimics the THC Host
>      Driver component similar to the QuickI2C driver being used in Windows OS
>      environment. (Note: This UEFI driver to support basic touch operation
>      may be just be a simplify/lite version of Ubuntu OS driver)
>
>   - Require BIOS to have VKB
>      feature added and supported.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - The touch functionality of touch panel, when connected
>      to THC0, is fully operational with the on-screen displayed virtual
>      keyboard (VKB).
>
>   - All the keys on the VKB respond correctly as expected to
>      user touch gestures.  
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Virtual keyboard failed to display on-screen of the
>      touch panel.
>
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not
>      working with the VKB available on-screen.   
>
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and DMA
>      cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch
>      panel.  
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm the
>      HID-I2C traffic is active during touch gestures using LA (PIO/DMA cycles
>      occur as expected).
>
>   - Validate
>      interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C
>      profile.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      QuickI2C driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>
>   - Validate logs
>      (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and
>      absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDSPI GPIO Configuration (BWG + GPIO HAS Requirements)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458255 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description :
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to assess and verify the THC0-SPI interface GPIO register
>      configurations are done correctly as expected via BIOS initialization,
>      with SPI interface is enabled on THC0 port.
>
>   - THC HW will uses
>      SPI interfaces to communicate with touch device (TIC). The
>      SPI bus protocols used for touch operation are same as the legacy
>      SPI integrated touch.
>
>   - Without the correct GPIO
>      configurations, the touch operations of the touch devices will failed to
>      function normally as expected and deem un-usable.
>
>   - This test is also designed to
>      have coverage for the BIOS RCR below:  
>
>
>
>
> HSD Link
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> BIOS RCR Title
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> Comments
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 14018584594 
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
> Add support for
>   SingleIo SPI mode for THC
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> This BIOS changes
>   can be implicitly covered during the THC-SPI GPIO Register Configuration
>   checkout.
>
>
>
>
>    | 
>
>
>
>
>
> 15012702523
>
>    | 
>
>
> Update IOSState
>   and IOSTerm for THC SPI GPIOs to improve D3/S0ix residency
>
>    | 
>
>
> This TC will check
>   and verify the BIOS configuration on both IOSState and IOSTerm register bits
>   in PAD_CFG_DW1.
>
>    | 
>
>
> The table below shown a complete list of GPIO pins with the valid
> register configurations once THC-SPI interface is enabled in BIOS
> settings.
> All the information tabulated in below are compiled based on the
> PTL_GPIO_Automation and PTL_RVP_GPIO_Platform_Mapping excel sheets.
> Note:
>
>
>   - With the new BIOS RCR added
>      in PTL, when Single/Dual SPI mode is enabled on THC port, BIOS will only
>      configured two SPI_IO pins(i.e. SPI_IO_0 and SPI_IO_1).
>
>           While the other two pins are freed-up/not configured and can be used
>      for other purposes. BIOS will only configured all four SPI_IO pins when
>      Quad-SPI mode enabled.
>
>
>   - With the exception on RST#
>      pin, due to native GPIO mode (pmode=0) is being used for this pin instead
>      of native THC mode. GPIO pin direction will need to configure to output
>      mode (gpiorxdis=1, gpiotxdis=0) as this GPIO pin will be utilized for
>      resetting the touch device.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Function Signal Name
>
>
> Touch/Human Interface
>   Device (HID; incl. THC-SPI)
>

>

>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration :
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled
>      in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel
>      Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration
>      ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below
>      to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to post-BIOS initialized state (i.e. BIOS Setup Menu,
>      UEFI Shell) with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS
>      configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>   - In PythonSV, check and
>      verify all the respective GPIO register bits are configured accordingly by
>      BIOS for each and every THC0-SPI's GPIO pins. Compare the register
>      output result with the table above.
>
> E.g. Using
> 'xxgpp_e_11' as an example, read/show the following register values: 
>
>
>   - Pad Ownership
>      (PAD_OWN):  
>
> [In]: soc.gpio.showsearch('xxgpp_e_11')
> [Out]: com1.sb.pad_own_xxgpp_e_11
> = 0x0
>
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW0:
>      Native Function (PMODE), RxEvCfg (RXEVCFG), GPIO Pin Direction
>      (GPIORXDIS/GPIOTXDIS): 
>
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW1: IO
>      Standby State (IOSSTATE), IO
>      Standby Termination (IOSTERM):
>
>
>   - Repeat the
>      same for the other THC0-SPI's GPIO pins/pads as listed in the table
>      above. 
>
>
> SW
> Requirements :
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> HW
> Requirements :
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to
>      enable SPI interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0 touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis
>      purposes.   
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>
>   - All the THC0-SPI's GPIO register bits
>      are correctly configured by BIOS as expected. (i.e. Same as table's
>      result) 
>
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing
>      criteria above.  
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Validate that
>      the THC register bits match the expected configuration based on the BIOS
>      knob settings
>
>   - Ensure the
>      register bits remain correct after system reboot (BIOS applied settings
>      permanently)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDSPI POR Touch Device Enabling+Functional (Pre-OS) using BIOS on-screen VKB support + Legacy Interrupt (Non-MSI) Mode

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458238 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities are operational in
>      the Pre-Ubuntu OS environment (including UEFI shell and BIOS Menu) using
>      the on-screen displayed virtual-keyboard (VKB) feature is enabled in BIOS.
>
>   - This will be carried out
>      using touch panel specifically on the platform, connected via the THC0
>      port.
>
>   - This test is designed to
>      implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, THC .EFI driver loading
>      which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration
>      sequences.
>
>   - It will also assess and
>      exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port
>      to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC
>      device.
>
>   - Soft keyboard
>      (or, a.k.a Virtual Keyboard - VKB) support is built on top of BIOS touch
>      support. VKB will become visible on touch panel display, once it is
>      enabled in the BIOS setting.
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>   - To enable VKB feature, go to BIOS
>      Menu:  Boot maintenance manager -> Boot configuration menu ->
>      Virtual Keyboard -> Enable
>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Setup
>      Menu, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described
>      in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting
>      BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to
>      take effect.
>
>   - After the SUT rebooted to BIOS Menu or UEFI shell, VKB
>      should appear and display on-screen of the touch panel.
>
>   - Perform touch gesture on-screen within the virtual
>      keyboard's white box boundary.
>
>   - Try to touch each and every key of the VKB displayed
>      on-screen to ensure all these keys are functioning correctly as expected.
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable
>      SPI interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the
>      THC0 touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm
> the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
>
>   - To support Pre-OS touch
>      operations, BIOS must incorporate a UEFI driver that mimics the THC Host
>      Driver component similar to the QuickSPI driver being used in Ubuntu OS
>      environment. (Note: This UEFI driver to support basic touch operation
>      may be just be a simplify/lite version of Ubuntu OS driver)
>
>   - Require BIOS to have VKB
>      feature added and supported.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - The touch functionality of touch panel, when connected
>      to THC0, is fully operational with the on-screen displayed virtual
>      keyboard (VKB).
>
>   - All the keys on the VKB respond correctly as expected to
>      user touch gestures.  
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
>   - Virtual keyboard failed to display on-screen of the
>      touch panel.
>
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not
>      working with the VKB available on-screen.   
>
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-SPI PIO and DMA
>      cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch
>      panel.  
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm the
>      HID-SPI traffic is active during touch gestures using LA (PIO/DMA cycles
>      occur as expected).
>
>   - Validate
>      interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-SPI
>      profile.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      QuickSPI driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>
>   - Validate logs
>      (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and
>      absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDSPI Touch Panel Enabling+Functional in OS (using Dual-SPI-IO Mode at various Frequencies-w/wo SPI Half-CLK Divider & CSA-to-CLK Delay Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458242 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify
>      and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully
>      operational with HID-SPI SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS
>      environment.
>
>   - This will be carried out
>      using touch panel specifically connected via THC0 port on
>      current project's platform.
>
>   - This test is designed to
>      implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-SPI SW driver
>      loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device
>      initialization/enumeration sequences.
>
>   - It will also assess and
>      exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC0 port
>      to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC
>      device.
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be
>      enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS menu, go to
>      Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration
>      -> <Refer to the BKM settings below
>      to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure : 
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to
>      Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>      the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting
>      BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to
>      take effect.
>
>   - Check and verify the current version of touch panel's
>      firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team,
>      or by analyzing the HID-SPI Device Descriptor's payload
>      captured/traced from Acute LA.
>
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch
>      panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool
>      provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This
>      step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx,
>      PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's
>      finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the
>      device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>
>   - (Optional) Test with a touch pen (Note: It
>      should come together as a package with the touch device , if that
>      particular device supported touch pen feature.)
>
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on the QuickSPI device/driver (THC0 port) via PuTTY by the
>      following commands:
>
>
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail
>       kernel info on which device/slot)
>
>
> SW
> Requirements :
>
>
>   - Install Ubuntu-OS with built-in QuickSPI kernel driver
>      (i.e. QuickSPI driver for THC0 port)
>
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView
>      tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of
>      HID-SPI data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
>
> HW
> Requirements :
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC0 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis
>      purposes.
>
> (Note: Please check RVP
> schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies :
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria :
>
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are
>      fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations
>      perform as anticipated.
>
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings
>      occurred on the THC0 port's QuickSPI device/driver end (as verified in
>      PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process. 
>
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria :
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC0 port - QuickSPI device/driver.
>
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not
>      working at all. 
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-SPI PIO and
>      DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the
>      touch panel.
>
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which
>      caused by the touch events.
>
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm the
>      HID-SPI traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA
>      (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>
>   - Validate interrupt
>      frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-SPI profile.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      QuickSPI driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>
>   - Validate logs
>      (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and
>      absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC0 HIDSPI Touch Panel Enabling+Functional in OS (using Quad-SPI-IO Mode at various Frequencies-w/wo SPI Half-CLK Divider & CSA-to-CLK Delay Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458243 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully operational with HID-SPI SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically connected via THC0 port on current project's platform.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-SPI SW driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - Check and verify the current version of touch panel's firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team, or by analyzing the HID-SPI Device Descriptor's payload captured/traced from Acute LA.
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx, PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>   - (Optional) Test with a touch pen (Note: It should come together as a package with the touch device , if that particular device supported touch pen feature.)
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank occurred on the QuickSPI device/driver (THC0 port) via PuTTY by the following commands:
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail kernel info on which device/slot)
>
> SW Requirements :
>
>   - Install Ubuntu-OS with built-in QuickSPI kernel driver (i.e. QuickSPI driver for THC0 port)
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-SPI data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
> HW Requirements :
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC0 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies :
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings occurred on the THC0 port's QuickSPI device/driver end (as verified in PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.    
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC0 port - QuickSPI device/driver.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working at all. 
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which caused by the touch events.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-SPI traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-SPI profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickSPI driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

---
