> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - No specific configuration needed.
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - Execute bootscript in PythonSV to bring up RVP to post soc/PCD bootstall state/breakpoint, where both Fuses and THC1 register spaces should become accessible at this stage.
>   - The bootscript is expected to halt/hook to the specified boot breakpoint, i.e. means that the system will not continue booting further past this breakpoint.
>   - In PythonSV, by using show/searching CLI commands below to find the relevant PythonSV namednodes (both THC and Fuses).
>   - Then, check and compare the output results with HAS and Harness defined configurations.
> Example below is taken/captured from PTL PythonSV Targ-Sim Model:  
>
> # Load Fuse RAM before reading to fuse/softstraps registers
> In [9]: soc.south.fuses.load_fuse_ram()
> Loading fuse ram state from hardware...
> Decoding fuse ram string...
>
> In [10]: soc.fuses.showsearch('thc')
> thc0_fuse.thc_reserved_fuse = 0x0
> thc0_fuse.virtual.thc_reserved_fuse_df = 0x0
> thc0_fuse.soft_straps.thc_reserved_softstrap = 0x0
> thc1_fuse.thc_reserved_fuse = 0x0
> thc1_fuse.virtual.thc_reserved_fuse_df = 0x0
> thc1_fuse.soft_straps.thc_reserved_softstrap = 0x0
> pmc.thc0_fuse_dis = 0x0
> pmc.thc1_fuse_dis = 0x0
> pmc.virtual.thc0_fuse_dis_df = 0x0
> pmc.virtual.thc1_fuse_dis_df = 0x0
> isclk.virtual.cmu_three_thc_en_hysteresis_3_0 = 0xe             # ignore
> isclk.virtual.cmu_three_thc_ack_hysteresis_3_0 = 0x3            # ignore
> isclk.virtual.cmuug_misc_cfg_ck_thc_req = 0x0                   # ignore
> isclk.virtual.cmuug_misc_ck_thc_modectrl = 0x0                  # ignore
>
>
> In [43]: soc.fuses.thc0_fuse.soft_straps.show
> -------> soc.fuses.thc0_fuse.soft_straps.show()
> 0x0000000000000000 : clockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : pgcbclockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : powergateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : thc_reserved_softstrap
>
> In [44]: soc.fuses.thc1_fuse.soft_straps.show
> -------> soc.fuses.thc1_fuse.soft_straps.show()
> 0x0000000000000000 : clockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : pgcbclockgateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : powergateenable
> 0x0000000000000000 : thc_reserved_softstrap
>
> In [16]: for port in ['thc0','thc1']:
>     ...:     print("\n" + port)
>     ...:     thc_fuse = eval(f"soc.{port}.search('fuse')")
>     ...:     for path_idx in thc_fuse:
>     ...:         print(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:         eval(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:
>     ...:     thc_strap = eval(f"soc.{port}.search('softstrap')")
>     ...:     for path_idx in thc_strap:
>     ...:         print(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:         eval(f"soc.{port}.{path_idx}.show()")
>     ...:
>
> thc0
> soc.thc0.pvt.thc_fuses.show()
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_31_8 (31:08) (ro) -- Reserved Field
> 0x00000000 : thc_fuses (07:00) (ro/v) -- Reserved Fuses
>
> soc.thc0.pvt.thc_softstraps.show()
> 0x00000000 : thc_softstraps (31:03) (ro/v) -- Reserved Softstraps
> 0x00000000 : pgcbclockgateenable (02:02) (ro/v) -- Control default value for pgcb clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : clockgateenable (01:01) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : powergateenable (00:00) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal power gating related config registers.
>
> thc1
> soc.thc1.pvt.thc_fuses.show()
> 0x00000000 : rsvd_31_8 (31:08) (ro) -- Reserved Field
> 0x00000000 : thc_fuses (07:00) (ro/v) -- Reserved Fuses
>
> soc.thc1.pvt.thc_softstraps.show()
> 0x00000000 : thc_softstraps (31:03) (ro/v) -- Reserved Softstraps
> 0x00000000 : pgcbclockgateenable (02:02) (ro/v) -- Control default value for pgcb clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : clockgateenable (01:01) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal clock gating related config registers.
> 0x00000000 : powergateenable (00:00) (ro/v) -- Control default value for internal power gating related config registers.
>
>   - PythonSV print-out values for all the relevant THC1's Fuse/Softstraps must have the same configuration as stated in HAS and/or Harness.
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP in early-boot state whereby all Fuses' and THC1's register spaces are accessible [e.g. at Pre-BIOS state or even earlier via bootscript() method to boot to a certain breakpoint.]
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
> Dependencies:
>
>   - Bootscript utilize for early boot phase via PythonSV.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - All the Fuses/Softstraps configuration for THC1 perfectly matches the expectations outlined in the HAS and Harness requirements. 
>   - No missing Fuse/Softstrap register for THC1 in PythonSV.
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Cross-check each softstrap/fuse bit against the HAS configuration table.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ❌ [TC] THC1 Global Interrupt  Enable/Disable sending Interrupt Upstream from THC to Host SW/Driver

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458278 |
| **Status** | rejected |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that the enable/disable global interrupt mechanism on THC1 port able to function normally as expected. 
>   - Expectation is that global interrupt mechanism/feature will be fully integrated and utilized by QuickSPI/QuickI2C SW Driver itself.
>   - Therefore, this test can be implicitly covered using the SW driver in normal flow and use cases.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to Ubuntu OS/WinOS with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>   - On SUT/RVP, enable and setup the driver ETL tracing (e.g. via TraceView tool) to capture the incoming input reports/packets on THC1 port.
>   - On host, use ITH tool to perform ODLA or VISA2PIN tracing to capture the two internal-IP VISA signals shown below (to capture any incoming MSI interrupts when touch event occur):
> From:  (TIC Device interrupt synchronized on PGCB clock)
> /thc_top/i_thc_ugd_wrapper_top/thc_crpm_top/thc_cdc_pgcb_wrapper_inst/interrupt_clkreq  
> To: (MSI Upstream transmission request on IOSF Primary)
> /thc_top/i_thc_pgd_wrapper_top/i_tsch_parb/i_thc_ilb/i_thc_msi_init_blk/ilb_parb_cavail
>
>   - Perform test and verify the following scenarios in below:
> (Important Note: SW driver may take over control and override the GBL_INT_EN bit when testing out the scenarios below) -> [William]: Need to confirm on this during PO?
>
> Scenario A: When GBL_INT_EN bit is 1 (Enabled by default)
> (a) In PythonSV, enable global interrupt feature on THC1 port by programming the following register bit, and read back this register to verify the configured value: 
>        soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_int_en.gbl_int_en = 1 
> (b) Verify and validate the following the expected outcome in below: 
> Expected outcome:
>
>   - When touch event occurred (i.e. THC received input report from TiC), THC HW will send any MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW.
>
>   - Observed internal signal toggling/assertion on "ilb_parb_cavail" (VISA full path: /thc_top/i_thc_pgd_wrapper_top/i_tsch_parb/i_thc_ilb/i_thc_msi_init_blk/ilb_parb_cavail) 
>
> Scenario B: When GBL_INT_EN bit is 0 (Disabled)
> (a) In PythonSV, disable global interrupt feature on THC1 port by programming the following register bit, and read back this register to verify the configured value: 
>        soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_int_en.gbl_int_en = 0 
> (b) Verify and validate the following the expected outcome in below: 
> Expected outcome:
>
>   - When touch event occurred (i.e. THC received input report from TiC), THC HW will not send any MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW. -> [William]: Does this SW driver still work in this case ? 
>
>   - No signal toggling/assertion observed on "ilb_parb_cavail" -> [William]: Need to confirm on this during PO? 
>
> Scenario C: When GBL_INT_EN bit transition from 0->1
> (a) Attempt test scenario B first. And, then follow-up by test scenario A. 
> (b) Verify and validate the following the expected outcome in below: 
> Expected outcome:
>
>   - THC HW shall check all pending interrupt sources (NDDI_STS, EOF_INT_STS, error interrupts, etc.) prior to GBL_INT_EN bit enablement (?) ,
>   - And, after global interrupt enabled, THC HW will generate a new MSI/Legacy line interrupt to SW if there is any pending interrupt source (from prior GBL_INT_EN enabled? ). 
>
>   - Repeat same steps #2 to #4 for the other test scenarios above. And finally, verify whether the results meet the expected outcome or not.
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/I2C Driver on Ubuntu OS/Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C driver for THC0 port)
>   - For driver logging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-SPI/I2C traffic/data payloads, interrupt rates/frequencies, and error/failure logging.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0 port.   
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>   - Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI/I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>   - Optional: Custom rework required to wire-out all the THC-SPI/I2C IO pins for Acute LA signals tracing. (Note: Make sure to use single-core wires and keep the length as short as possible to avoid/minimize any signal integrity impact.)
>   - VISA-to-pin setup + Acute LA to analyze the HID-SPI/-I2C traffic together with the THC-IP VISA signals.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.  
>   - ODLA via Intel TraceHub Tool, or VISA2PIN setup with Acute LA. 
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Enable/disable global interrupt feature on THC1 port able to work and function as expected as stated in this TC's test steps above.
>   - QuickSPI/QuickI2C SW Driver is used to implicit cover the enable/disable global interrupt flow.
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.   
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Validate that the ETL tracing is correctly enabled on the SUT/RVP and able to capture input reports/packets on THC0/THC1 ports.
>   - Verify the MSI interrupts are captured via ITH tool and the behaviour matched as expected.
>   - Verify and check back the register to ensure configuration matches the intended test scenario (enabled, disabled, or transition).
>   - Cross-check the ETL trace logs, VISA signal captures, and register readbacks to ensure the observed HW/SW behaviour matches the expected outcomes.
>   - Repeat the verification steps for both THC0 and THC1 ports, and all relevant scenarios to ensure consistent and robust validation.
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDI2C (Fast-Mode/400kHz) Touch Device Enabling+Functional in OS [MSI Interrupt]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458231 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>
>   - The objective/intent of this
>      test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch
>      device are fully operational with HID-I2C SW driver
>      integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.  
>
>   - This will be carried out using
>      touch panel specifically connected via THC1 port on current
>      project's platform.
>
>   - This test is designed to
>      implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-I2C SW driver
>      loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device
>      initialization/enumeration sequences.
>
>   - It will also assess and
>      exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC1 port
>      to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC
>      device.  
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC1 port will be required to be enabled in BIOS
>      settings
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the
>      BKM settings below to enable THC1 port>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test
> Steps / Procedure: 
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to
>      Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>      the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>      section above.
>
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting
>      BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to
>      take effect.
>
>   - Check and verify the current version of the
>      BOM52 touch panel's firmware either by using the dedicated vendor
>      tool provided from 3PE team, or by analyzing the HID-I2C Device
>      Descriptor's payload captured/traced from Acute LA.
>
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch
>      pad firmware into the TIC of the touch pad by using vendor tool
>      provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This
>      step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S,
>      NVL-Hx, PTL-U, PTL-H, etc.)
>
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger
>      input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the
>      device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank
>      occurred on the QuickI2C device/driver (THC1 port) via PuTTY by the following
>      commands:
>
>
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail
>       kernel info on which device/slot)
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - Install Ubuntu OS with built-in QuickI2C kernel driver
>      (i.e. QuickI2C driver for THC1 port)
>
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView
>      tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of
>      HID-I2C data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C
>      interface on THC1 port.
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - (Optional): Acute logic
>      analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis
>      purposes.  
>
> (Note: Please check NVL RVP
> schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are
>      fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations
>      perform as anticipated.
>
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings
>      occurred on the THC1 port's QuickI2C device/driver end (as verified in
>      PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC1 port - QuickI2C
>      device/driver.
>
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not
>      working at all. 
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and
>      DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and touch pad device.
>
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which
>      caused by the touch events.  
>
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm the
>      HID-I2C traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA
>      (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>
>   - Validate interrupt
>      frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C profile.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      QuickI2C driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>
>   - Validate logs
>      (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and
>      absence of error flags.
>
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below
> is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC
> Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia -
> Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>
>   - BIOS version: 
>
>
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>
>
>   - OS kernel:
>
>
>   - Window OS
>
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later
> version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended
> solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>
>   - Driver versions: 
>
>
>   - Windows OS
>
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDI2C (Fast-Plus-Mode/1MHz) Touch Device Enabling+Functional in OS [MSI Interrupt]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458232 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully operational with HID-I2C SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.  
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically connected via THC1 port on current project's platform.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-I2C SW driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.  
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will be required to be enabled in BIOS settings
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - Check and verify the current version of the BOM52 touch panel's firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team, or by analyzing the HID-I2C Device Descriptor's payload captured/traced from Acute LA.
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch pad firmware into the TIC of the touch pad by using vendor tool provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx, PTL-U, PTL-H, etc.)
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank occurred on the QuickI2C device/driver (THC1 port) via PuTTY by the following commands:
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail kernel info on which device/slot)
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - Install Ubuntu OS with built-in QuickI2C kernel driver (i.e. QuickI2C driver for THC1 port)
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-I2C data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.  
> (Note: Please check NVL RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings occurred on the THC1 port's QuickI2C device/driver end (as verified in PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC1 port - QuickI2C device/driver.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working at all. 
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and touch pad device.
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which caused by the touch events.  
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-I2C traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickI2C driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended
> solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDI2C GPIO Configuration (BWG + GPIO HAS Requirements)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458236 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to assess and verify the THC1-I2C interface GPIO register configurations are done correctly as expected via BIOS initialization, with I2C interface is enabled on THC1 port.
>   - THC HW will uses I2C interfaces to communicate with touch device (TIC). The I2C bus protocols used for touch operation are same as the legacy I2C integrated touch.
>   - Without the correct GPIO configurations, the touch operations of the touch devices will failed to function normally as expected and deem un-usable.
>   - This test is also designed to have coverage for the BIOS RCR below:  
> HSD Link
>
>  | 
>
> BIOS RCR Title
>
>  | 
>
> Comments
>
>  | 
> 15012702523
>  | 
> Update IOSState and IOSTerm for THC SPI GPIOs to improve D3/S0ix residency
>  | 
> This TC will check and verify the BIOS configuration on both IOSState and IOSTerm register bits in PAD_CFG_DW1.
>  | 
>
> The table below shown a complete list of GPIO with the expected register configurations once THC-I2C interface is enabled in BIOS settings.
> All the information tabulated in below are compiled based on the PTL_GPIO_Automation and PTL_RVP_GPIO_Platform_Mapping excel sheets.
> Note:
>
>   - With the exception on RST# pin, due to native GPIO mode (pmode=0) is being used for this pin instead of native THC mode. GPIO pin direction will need to configure to output mode (gpiorxdis=1, gpiotxdis=0) as this GPIO pin will be utilized for resetting the touch device.
>
>
> Function Signal Name
> Touch/Human Interface Device (HID; incl. THC-I2C )
>
>  | 
>
> GPIO Name
>
>  | 
>
> SIPFAM
>
>  | 
>
> PCD-P
>
> pkg pin
>
>  | 
>
> PCD-H
>
> pkg pin
>
>  | 
>
> Pad Ownership
>
> (PAD_OWN)
>
>  | 
>
> Native Function
> (DW0.PMODE)
> 0 = Native GPIO mode
>
>  | 
>
> RxEvCfg
>
> (DW0.RXEVCFG)
> N/A = Default Value
>
>  | 
>
> Pin Direction DW0.GPIO{Rx/Tx}Dis
> IN - GPIORxDis=0, GPIOTxDis=1;
> OUT - GPIORxDis=1, GPIOTxDis=0;
>
> HI_Z - GPIORxDis=1, GPIOTxDis=1
>
>  | 
>
> IO Standby
>
> State
>
> (DW1.IOSSTATE)
>
>  | 
>
> IO Standby
>
> Termination
>
> (DW1.IOSTERM)
>
>  | 
>
> Package Ball Name
>
>  | 
>
> thc_i2c0_scl
>
>  | 
>
> xxgpp_e_12
>
>  | 
>
> THC0_GSPI0
>
>  | 
>
> Pin
>
>  | 
>
> Pin
>
>  | 
>
> HOST_GPIO(0)
>
>  | 
>
> 0x1
>
>  | 
>
> N/A
>
>  | 
>
> HI_Z
>
>  | 
>
> Last_Value(0)
>
>  | 
>
> SAME(0)
>
>  | 
>
> xxgpp_e_12_thc_i2c0_scl_thc0_spi1_io_0_gspi0_mosi_i2c4_scl
>
>  | 
>
> thc_i2c0_sda
>
>  | 
>
> xxgpp_e_13
>
>  | 
>

>
>  |

>  | 
>

>

>
>  | 
>
> thc0_spi1_rst_b (shared with THC-I2C mode)
>

>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to post-BIOS initialized state (i.e. BIOS Setup Menu, UEFI Shell) with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>   - In PythonSV, check and verify all the respective GPIO register bits are configured accordingly by BIOS for each and every THC1-I2C's GPIO pins. Compare the register output result with the table above.
> E.g. Using 'xxgpp_e_11' as an example, read/show the following register values: 
>
>   - Pad Ownership (PAD_OWN):  
> [In]: soc.gpio.showsearch('xxgpp_e_11')
> [Out]: com1.sb.pad_own_xxgpp_e_11 = 0x0
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW0: Native Function (PMODE), RxEvCfg (RXEVCFG), GPIO Pin Direction (GPIORXDIS/GPIOTXDIS): 
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW1: IO Standby State (IOSSTATE), IO Standby Termination (IOSTERM):
>
>   - Repeat the same for the other THC1-I2C's GPIO pins/pads as listed in the table above. 
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - N/A
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.   
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - All the THC1-I2C's GPIO register bits are correctly configured by BIOS as expected. (i.e. Same as table's result) 
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.  
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Validate that the THC register bits match the expected configuration based on the BIOS knob settings
>   - Ensure the register bits remain correct after system reboot (BIOS applied settings permanently)
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDI2C POR Touch Device Enabling+Functional (Pre-OS) using BIOS on-screen VKB support + Legacy Interrupt (Non-MSI) Mode

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458227 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities are operational in the Pre-Ubuntu OS environment (including UEFI shell and BIOS Menu) using the on-screen displayed virtual-keyboard (VKB) feature is enabled in BIOS.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically on the platform, connected via the THC0 port.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, THC .EFI driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>   - Soft keyboard (or, a.k.a Virtual Keyboard - VKB) support is built on top of BIOS touch support. VKB will become visible on touch panel display, once it is enabled in the BIOS setting.
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>   - To enable VKB feature, go to BIOS Menu:  Boot maintenance manager -> Boot configuration menu -> Virtual Keyboard -> Enable
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Setup Menu, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - After the SUT rebooted to BIOS Menu or UEFI shell, VKB should appear and display on-screen of the touch panel.
>   - Perform touch gesture on-screen within the virtual keyboard's white box boundary.
>   - Try to touch each and every key of the VKB displayed on-screen to ensure all these keys are functioning correctly as expected.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - N/A
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
>   - To support Pre-OS touch operations, BIOS must incorporate a UEFI driver that mimics the THC Host Driver component similar to the QuickI2C driver being used in Windows OS environment. (Note: This UEFI driver to support basic touch operation may be just be a simplify/lite version of Ubuntu OS driver)
>   - Require BIOS to have VKB feature added and supported.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - The touch functionality of touch panel, when connected to THC1, is fully operational with the on-screen displayed virtual keyboard (VKB).
>   - All the keys on the VKB respond correctly as expected to user touch gestures.  
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>   - Virtual keyboard failed to display on-screen of the touch panel.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working with the VKB available on-screen.   
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-I2C PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.  
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-I2C traffic is active during touch gestures using LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-I2C profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickI2C driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended
> solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDSPI GPIO Configuration (BWG + GPIO HAS Requirements)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458256 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to assess and verify the THC1-SPI interface GPIO register configurations are done correctly as expected via BIOS initialization, with SPI interface is enabled on THC1 port.
>   - THC HW will uses SPI interfaces to communicate with touch device (TIC). The SPI bus protocols used for touch operation are same as the legacy SPI integrated touch.
>   - Without the correct GPIO configurations, the touch operations of the touch devices will failed to function normally as expected and deem un-usable.
>   - This test is also designed to have coverage for the BIOS RCR below:  
> HSD Link
>
>  | 
>
> BIOS RCR Title
>
>  | 
>
> Comments
>
>  | 
> 14018584594 
>
>  | 
> Add support for SingleIo SPI mode for THC
>
>  | 
>
> This BIOS changes can be implicitly covered during the THC-SPI GPIO Register Configuration checkout.
>
>  | 
> 15012702523
>  | 
> Update IOSState and IOSTerm for THC SPI GPIOs to improve D3/S0ix residency
>  | 
> This TC will check and verify the BIOS configuration on both IOSState and IOSTerm register bits in PAD_CFG_DW1.
>  | 
>
> The table below shown a complete list of GPIO pins with the valid register configurations once THC-SPI interface is enabled in BIOS settings.
> All the information tabulated in below are compiled based on the PTL_GPIO_Automation and PTL_RVP_GPIO_Platform_Mapping excel sheets.
> Note:
>
>   - With the new BIOS RCR added in PTL, when Single/Dual SPI mode is enabled on THC port, BIOS will only configured two SPI_IO pins(i.e. SPI_IO_0 and SPI_IO_1).
> While the other two pins are freed-up/not configured and can be used for other purposes. BIOS will only configured all four SPI_IO pins when Quad-SPI mode enabled.
>
>   - With the exception on RST# pin, due to native GPIO mode (pmode=0) is being used for this pin instead of native THC mode. GPIO pin direction will need to configure to output mode (gpiorxdis=1, gpiotxdis=0) as this GPIO pin will be utilized for resetting the touch device.
>

> Pre-Test Configuration :
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to post-BIOS initialized state (i.e. BIOS Setup Menu, UEFI Shell) with all the HW requirements met with all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>   - In PythonSV, check and verify all the respective GPIO register bits are configured accordingly by BIOS for each and every THC1-SPI's GPIO pins. Compare the register output result with the table above.
> E.g. Using 'xxgpp_e_11' as an example, read/show the following register values: 
>
>   - Pad Ownership (PAD_OWN):  
> [In]: soc.gpio.showsearch('xxgpp_e_11')
> [Out]: com1.sb.pad_own_xxgpp_e_11 = 0x0
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW0: Native Function (PMODE), RxEvCfg (RXEVCFG), GPIO Pin Direction (GPIORXDIS/GPIOTXDIS): 
>
>   - PAD_CFG_DW1: IO Standby State (IOSSTATE), IO Standby Termination (IOSTERM):
>
>   - Repeat the same for the other THC1-SPI's GPIO pins/pads as listed in the table above. 
>
> SW Requirements :
>
>   - N/A
> HW Requirements :
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.   
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>   - All the THC1-SPI's GPIO register bits are correctly configured by BIOS as expected. (i.e. Same as table's result) 
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.  
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Validate that the THC register bits match the expected configuration based on the BIOS knob settings
>   - Ensure the register bits remain correct after system reboot (BIOS applied settings permanently)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDSPI POR Touch Device Enabling+Functional (Pre-OS) using BIOS on-screen VKB support + Legacy Interrupt (Non-MSI) Mode

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458240 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities are operational in the Pre-Ubuntu OS environment (including UEFI shell and BIOS Menu) using the on-screen displayed virtual-keyboard (VKB) feature is enabled in BIOS.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically on the platform, connected via the THC1 port.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, THC .EFI driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>   - Soft keyboard (or, a.k.a Virtual Keyboard - VKB) support is built on top of BIOS touch support. VKB will become visible on touch panel display, once it is enabled in the BIOS setting.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>   - To enable VKB feature, go to BIOS Menu:  Boot maintenance manager -> Boot configuration menu -> Virtual Keyboard -> Enable
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Setup Menu, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - After the SUT rebooted to BIOS Menu or UEFI shell, VKB should appear and display on-screen of the touch panel.
>   - Perform touch gesture on-screen within the virtual keyboard's white box boundary.
>   - Try to touch each and every key of the VKB displayed on-screen to ensure all these keys are functioning correctly as expected.
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - N/A
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM36A, BOM37A, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
>   - To support Pre-OS touch operations, BIOS must incorporate a UEFI driver that mimics the THC Host Driver component similar to the QuickSPI driver being used in Ubuntu OS environment. (Note: This UEFI driver to support basic touch operation may be just be a simplify/lite version of Ubuntu OS driver)
>   - Require BIOS to have VKB feature added and supported.
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - The touch functionality of touch panel, when connected to THC1, is fully operational with the on-screen displayed virtual keyboard (VKB).
>   - All the keys on the VKB respond correctly as expected to user touch gestures.  
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>   - Virtual keyboard failed to display on-screen of the touch panel.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working with the VKB available on-screen.   
>
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.  
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-SPI traffic is active during touch gestures using LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-SPI profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickSPI driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDSPI Touch Panel Enabling+Functional in OS (using Dual-SPI-IO Mode at various Frequencies-w/wo SPI Half-CLK Divider & CSA-to-CLK Delay Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458247 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully operational with HID-SPI SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically connected via THC1 port on current project's platform.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-SPI SW driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - Check and verify the current version of touch panel's firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team, or by analyzing the HID-SPI Device Descriptor's payload captured/traced from Acute LA.
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx, PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>   - (Optional) Test with a touch pen (Note: It should come together as a package with the touch device , if that particular device supported touch pen feature.)
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank occurred on the QuickSPI device/driver (THC1 port) via PuTTY by the following commands:
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail kernel info on which device/slot)
>
> SW Requirements :
>
>   - Install Ubuntu-OS with built-in QuickSPI kernel driver (i.e. QuickSPI driver for THC1 port)
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-SPI data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
> HW Requirements :
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies :
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings occurred on the THC1 port's QuickSPI device/driver end (as verified in PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.    
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC1 port - QuickSPI device/driver.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working at all. 
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which caused by the touch events.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-SPI traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-SPI profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickSPI driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] THC1 HIDSPI Touch Panel Enabling+Functional in OS (using Single-SPI-IO Mode at various Frequencies-w/wo SPI Half-CLK Divider & CSA-to-CLK Delay Enabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458254 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to verify and ensure that touch functionalities of the touch device are fully operational with HID-SPI SW driver integrated in the Ubuntu OS environment.
>   - This will be carried out using touch panel specifically connected via THC1 port on current project's platform.
>   - This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of BIOS, HID-SPI SW driver loading which covered the THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
>   - It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration -> <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - Check and verify the current version of touch panel's firmware either by using the dedicated vendor tool provided from 3PE team, or by analyzing the HID-SPI Device Descriptor's payload captured/traced from Acute LA.
>   - (If require) On SUT, install/flash the latest touch panel firmware into the TIC of the touch panel by using vendor tool provided. Reboot the SUT once the flash process completed.  (Note: This step can be performed using a N-1 RVP or SUT, for example: NVL-S, NVL-Hx, PTL-U,  PTL-H, etc.)
>   - Perform different type of touch gestures by user's finger input on the touch panel to verify the touch functionality of the device. (e.g. 1-to-10 fingers touch, swipe, drag-hold, etc.)
>   - (Optional) Test with a touch pen (Note: It should come together as a package with the touch device , if that particular device supported touch pen feature.)
>   - Verify and make sure that there is NO yellow-bank occurred on the QuickSPI device/driver (THC1 port) via PuTTY by the following commands:
>   - sudo lspci (list pci device)
>   - lspci -kvvv -s xx:xx.x (list pci device with very detail kernel info on which device/slot)
>
> SW Requirements :
>
>   - Install Ubuntu-OS with built-in QuickSPI kernel driver (i.e. QuickSPI driver for THC1 port)
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files, enabling detailed analysis of HID-SPI data packets/payloads and interrupt rates/frequencies.
> HW Requirements :
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM36, BOM37, BOM52) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies :
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - Requirement: NO errors/issues and Yellow-Bank warnings occurred on the THC1 port's QuickSPI device/driver end (as verified in PuTTy) at any given point of time during the validation process.    
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.  
>   - Yellow-bang occurred on THC1 port - QuickSPI device/driver.
>   - Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working at all. 
>   - Encountered failure or errors during HID-SPI PIO and DMA cycle/traffic in the communication between THC and TIC device of the touch panel.
>
>   - SUT hung, became unresponsive, or hit into errors which caused by the touch events.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm the HID-SPI traffic is active during touch gestures using TraceView or LA (PIO/DMA cycles occur as expected).
>   - Validate interrupt frequency and data packet flow match the expected HID-SPI profile.
>   - Ensure the QuickSPI driver remains loaded and operational throughout the test.
>   - Validate logs (e.g. PuTTY output, .ETL traces) for correct device enumeration and absence of error flags.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
> https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC1 Interrupt Line Quiescing Mode Enabled/Disabled with Back-to-Back Device Interrupt

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458285 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - This test is to verify and ensure that the SW controlled touch device interrupt quiescing mechanism/feature in THC-IP able to function normally as described in below.   
>   - Once THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN bit enabled, THC HW will set THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS bit itself once RX sequencer is IDLE, after completing processing of any current microframe that started before the THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN.
> (Important note: SW cannot clear THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN until THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS bit is already set.)   
>   - Expectation: With THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN bit being set, THC HW (i.e. RxDMA sequencer) will temporarily ignore any new touch device interrupt (including touch and non-touch data interrupt), until THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN is cleared by SW later.
>   - When SW writes 1 to THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN bit, THC HW shall keep the bit read as 0 until it completely received any ongoing micro-frame interrupt and then THC set it to 1.
>   - After THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN is cleared by SW, THC shall start service device interrupt as usual.
>
> (Note: This quiescing mechanism does not affect/impact TXDMA and PIO operations. Both of these operations are supposed to work even when touch device interrupt is quiesced.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration :
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure : 
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to UEFI shell/Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Make sure to save the new changes before exiting BIOS Setup Menu, then allow the SUT to reboot itself for these changes to take effect.
>   - In PythonSV, make sure to check the current status of the quiesce registers with the following commands in below.
> By default, quiescing mode should be disabled in normal operations, i.e. both THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN and THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS should be cleared to 0. If not, configure the THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN to 0.
> soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_en = 0
> soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_hw_sts = 0
>
> Phase#1 - Enable Quiescing Mode:
> (i) Enable the touch device interrupt quiescing mode, and read to the register bit should immediately reflect 1 as well (if no ongoing/pending touch device micro-frame interrupt):
>       soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_en = 1
>       soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_en.read()   
>
> (ii) Then, THC HW will flagged the THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS to 1 indicating the quiescing mode is already enabled and in effect. 
>       Verify THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS bit is being set to 1:
>       soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_en.read()
>
> (iii) Perform PIO or TxDMA operations to make sure they are still functioning as usual, even after interrupt quiesce mode is enabled. (Note: Refer to PIO TCs for more details on how to execute PIO operations)
>
> (iv) Trigger new touch device interrupt by asserting the interrupt line on the INT# GPIO pin for THC1 port. (Note: Touch device interrupt can be either touch data or non-touch data interrupt.)
>       No RxDMA frames will be observed on the HID-SPI traffic, as THC HW ignored the touch device interrupt. 
>
> (v)  Once the THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN bit enabled, the touch functionality of the touch device will not work in this mode, as the human-interface touch data interrupt/frame will only work in RxDMA mode. 
>
>          Extra: Additional checking on THC Interrupt Status Register (THC_M_PRT_INT_STATUS)
> Bits
>  | 
> Access Type
>  | 
> Default
>  | 
> Description
>  | 
> 15
>  | 
> RW/1C/V
>  | 
> 1'b0
>  | 
> THC Device Raw Interrupt Status (DEV_RAW_INT_STS)
> Interrupt Status when the THC device (I2C or SPI) asserts the device GPIO interrupt. This bit is detected after level/edge, polarity detection and is independent of the interrupt quiescing (THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN). Even THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN=1, this bit is still set if the device asserts the interrupt (in all level/edge, pos/neg interrupt modes). SW clears the bit by writing 1 to the bit.
> If the DEV_RAW_INT_EN bit is also 1, then an SW interrupt is generated. 
> 1 - THC device (I2C or SPI) asserts the device GPIO interrupt.
> 0 - No device raw GPIO interrupt assertion is detected.
> Note: On LNL, due to an RTL issue,
>
>   - If Raw_INT_STS MSI is needed : Quiesce should be off
>   - If RAW_INT_STS MSI is not needed , just plan to read : hardware should come out of D0i2 and check interrupt and set RAW_INT_STS
>   - For line level-triggered interrupt, if TIC does not toggle the line interrupt between uframe/frames, this bit will not be set for the subsequent level interrupts even SW clears this bit.
>
>  | 
>
>
> Phase#2 - Disable Quiescing Mode:
> (i) Disable the quiesce mode by setting the following command: 
>       soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_en = 0
>       soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_en.read() 
>
> (ii)  Verify THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_HW_STS bit is cleared to 0 by THC HW:
>        soc.thc1.port.mem.thc_m_prt_control.thc_devint_quiesce_hw_sts.read()
>
> (iii) After both THC_DEVINT_QUIESCE_EN/_HW_STS bits are cleared, the RxDMA operation and touch functionality of the touch device will be working as usual. 
>
>   - Repeat for same test in step#3 for THC-I2C interface. Or, THC-SPI depending on which interface is being validated first. 
> SW Requirements :
>
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/-I2C Driver on Ubuntu/Windows OS. (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C driver for THC1 port)
>   - (Optional) For debugging, users can utilize the TraceView tool to capture .ETL log files for detailed analysis.
> HW Requirements :
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI shell/Ubuntu OS/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
>   - PythonSV connected to host system (via USB2DBC or XDP)
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0 port naming label and location) 
> Dependencies :
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
> Test Pass Criteria :
>
>   - Touch device interrupt quiescing mechanism function as expected on THC1 port, i.e. observed expected outcome in both Phase#1 and Phase#2 testing (as detailed above).
> Test Fail Criteria :
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above. 
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Verify that the register readbacks of the quiesce enabled/HW status matches the intended configuration.
>   - Validate that touch panel interrupts are suppressed when quiesce mode is enabled, and that normal interrupt handling resumes when quiesce mode is disabled.
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC1 RLTR (Memory) Programming to PMC via QoS_DMD [PM Telemetry#11, 12]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458264 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - THC
>      implements a controller-level Latency Tolerance Reporting (LTR) mechanism,
>      which allows the IP to send LTP message and its' values to PMC via IOSF-SB
>      whenever the LTR states changes (THC_LTR_EN bit = 1). If there are no
>      changes in the LTR states, THC shall not send the LTR message to PMC.
>
>   - The LTR
>      values for both active and low-power states are programmable by SW (e.g. BIOS, Driver) with {ACTIVE/LP}_LTR_VAL and {ACTIVE/LP}_LTR_SCALE registers.
>
>
>   - This test is
>      to verify that the Infinite, Active and Low power LTR messages and its'
>      values (from THC) are sent to PMC, whenever THC LTR states change (via
>      LAST_LTR_SENT_register).
>
>   - Once PMC
>      successfully received the LTR messages from THC, those LTR values will be
>      reflected in the PMC register (i.e. LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0/1)
>
>   - The test
>      will be run in PythonSV while SUT is booted to WinOS or UEFI Shell
>      environment with THC Port0/1 function enabled in BIOS . 
>
>
>
> Starting from MTL program onwards, THC will support
> Chassis 2.2 specification, which replaced the legacy LTR for Memory Access
> resources.
>
> THC will send memory resources LTR (a.k.a RLTR) to PMC
> via QOS_DMD mapped signals  on IOSF-SB channel.
>
>
>
>  Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to UEFI shell with all the HW requirements met with
>      all the BIOS configuration done accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test
>      Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Perform
>      Active/LP/Infinite LTR tests separately, by switching to one LTR_state at
>      a time.
>
> (a) Active LTR (LAST_LTR_SENT = 1)
>
>  THC sends Active LTR messages to PMC in any one of the following
> conditions in below:
>
>
>   - Any port's
>      RXDMA Start bit = 1
>
>   - THC_LTR_EN =
>      1
>
>   - Program both
>      ACT_LTR_VAL/SCALE accordingly
>
>   - When SW set
>      both THC ACT_LTR_EN/REQ bits
>
> [Note:
> Make sure to clear both LP_LTR_EN/REQ bits.]
>
>
>
> SW Flow:
>
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_1.start = 0
>
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_2.start = 1
>
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.thc_ltr_en = 1
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_en = 0 
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_req = 0 
>
>
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_val = (Any value - Max 10
> bits supported)
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_scale = (Any value between
> 0 ~ 5 supported)
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_en = 1
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_req = 1
>
>
>
> (b)  LP LTR (LAST_LTR_SENT = 2)
>
>  THC sends Low-Power LTR message to PMC in one of the following
> conditions:
>
>
>   - Any port's
>      RXDMA Start bit = 1 
>
>   - THC _LTR_EN
>      = 1 
>
>   - SW program
>      the LP_LTR_VAL/SCALE accordingly 
>
>   - SW set both
>      LP_LTR_EN/REQ bits
>
> [Note:
> Make sure to clear both ACT_LTR_EN/REQ bits.]
>
> [Note: If the LP_LTR_EN is set to 1
> while THC receives an device interrupt assertion, the LP LTR request will be
> ignored and THC won't send the LP_LTR message.]
>
>
>
> SW Flow:
>
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_1.start = 0
>
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_2.start = 1
>
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.thc_ltr_en = 1
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_en = 0 
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_req = 0 
>
>
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_val = (Any value - Max 10
> bits supported)
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_scale = (Any value between
> 0 ~ 5 supported)
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_en = 1
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_req = 1
>
>
>
> (c) Infinite LTR (LAST_LTR_SENT
> = 0)
>
>  THC sends the infinite LTR/No
> Requirement messages to PMC in any
> one of the following conditions:
>
>
>   - When all
>      port's RXDMA start bits are cleared, or
>
>   - SW can set
>      the LP_LTR_EN bit and clears the LP_LTR_REQ bit  [Note: Need to clear both
>      ACT_LTR_EN/REQ bits as well.], or
>
>
>   - Cold boot exit/VNN removal
>      exit, or
>
>   - Host warm/cold reset exit
>
>
>
> SW Flow:
>
> pch.thc0.pvt.thc_sb_pm_ctrl.thc_ltr_en = 1
>
>
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_en = 0 
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.act_ltr_req = 0 
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_req = 0 
>
> pch.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.lp_ltr_en =
> 1  
>
>
>
> OR
>
>
>
> ## When the all ports' RXDMA's start bits are cleared
>
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_1.start = 0
>
> pch.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_read_dma_cntrl_2.start = 0
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - N/A
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI Shell or Windows OS.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements /
>      config.
>
> Test
> Pass Criteria:
>
>
>   - Verified the LAST_LTR_SENT
>      register is sent by THC HW, whenever THC LTR state changes
>
>   - Check if the LTR values are
>      reflected in the PMC register as well (i.e. LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0/1)
>
> Test
> Fail Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Verify that each LTR state are able to send messages to
>      PMC with its respective conditions
>
>   - Validate that the LTR values are reflected in the PMC
>      register when message is sent from THC to PMC
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> Built in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] THC1 Register Checkout (PCI Config, MMIO, Private Spaces)  via PythonSV

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458276 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective/intent of this test is to validate both register accessibility of the THC1 port's register space.
>   - Ensure the access over both IOSF Primary and Sideband are supported and functional as expected.
>   - Ensure all default values defined for every register field or register field-bit (check via PythonSV) comply with the CRIF .xml file, as outlined for the program.
>
> This test is also designed to implicitly cover the PTL PCR below:
>
>   - This test is/can be carried out in UEFI shell or WinOS environment during PTL PO.
>   - The THC-IP is located in PTL-SOC/PCD support 16-bit port ID endpoint. 
>   - [PTL] CRIF/RDL .xml file Link: thc_top_ptl.xml 
> Implicit coverage for PCR below:  
> HSD Link
>  | 
> PCR Title
>  | 
> Action/Coverage for Post-Si FV
>  | 
> Comments
>  | 
> 15010721030
>  | 
> [PTL-SoC] IP to support 16-bit port ID endpoint
>  | 
> Able to cover
>  | 
> Existing register checkout test in PythonSV able to cover this PCR.
>  | 
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - No specific configuration needed.
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
>
>   - Execute bootscript in PythonSV to bring up RVP to post SOC/PCD bootstall state/breakpoint, THC-IP register spaces should become accessible at this stage.
>   - The bootscript is expected to halt/hook to specified boot breakpoint, i.e. means that system will not continue booting further past this breakpoint.
>   - Configure all 4 THC1 register spaces access method to 'TAP2SB' to validate register accessibility flow via IOSF-SB channel in PythonSV.
>   - Import 'thc_registers' script in PythonSV with the following command: "from vjt.thc import thc_registers".
>   - Read all the THC1 registers with PythonSV command below: [Note: THC1 register access method set to 'TAP2SB' by default.]
> thc_registers.thc_registers_dump(thc_num=0)                                     # Read Register-level (By Default)
> thc_registers.thc_registers_dump(thc_num=0, reg_bit_en=True)      # Read Register Bits-level
>
>   - Write to THC1 register bits with PythonSV command below: 
> thc_registers.thc_registers_dump(thc_num=0, write=True)               # Write Register Bits-level (By Default)
>
>   - Check and review if there are any failures/errors return by the script. (e.g. Mismatch register read default values, missing registers, failed register write, etc.)
>   - Switch to IOSF-Primary access method for all THC1 register spaces by using the PythonSV command shown in table below. Then, Repeat steps #5 to #7.
>
> Access Method 
> THC1 REG Spaces
>
>  | 
>
> IOSF-Sideband
>
> (i.e. TAP2SB)
>
>  | 
>
> IOSF-Primary
>
> (i.e. PCI-CFG, MEM/MMIO, P2SB)
>
>  | 
>
> CFG (PCI-CFG)
>
>  | 
>

>
> In: soc.thc1.cfg.get_access

> Out: 'tap2sb'

>

>
>  |

>

>

>

>
> In: soc.thc1.mem.get_access
>
> Out: 'mem'
>

>

>
>  | 
>

>

>

>

>

>
