### ✅ [TC] Warm Reset Cycles with THC0 (HID-SPI) and THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458396 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically Warm Resets (WR), while running test traffic through THC0 and THC1 ports concurrently with both ports configured as HID-SPI interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of WR Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of Fuses/Soft-straps loading, BIOS, SW Driver and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC0 and THC1 ports to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• Additionally, the THC controller will undergo a reset during each Warm-Reset cycle. This WR sequence will implicitly encompass the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC0 and THC1 ports will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 and THC1 ports.>
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0    #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of Warm-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional)
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wale the system back to Ubuntu OS state (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "Warm-Reset" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "Warm-Reset|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the WR cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD)]
> 	• NGA Testline for WR Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project: https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC0/THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC0/THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of Warm-Reset (WR) PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC0/THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickSPI).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch device connected on THC0/THC1 still working normally after each WR cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted WR cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after WR. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the WR cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of WR cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC controller/driver after any warm-reset cycle.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-reset (can operate via HID-SPI/I2C interface, PIO/DMA)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:

>

> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS

### ✅ [TC] Warm Reset Cycles with THC1 (HID-I2C) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458397 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically Warm Resets (WR), while running test traffic through a single port THC1 that configured as HID-I2C interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of WR Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of Fuses/Soft-straps loading, BIOS, SW Driver and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-I2C PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• Additionally, the THC controller will undergo a reset during each Warm-Reset cycle. This WR sequence will implicitly encompass the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quicki2c" & "Kernel modules: intel_quicki2c" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of Warm-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional)
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wale the system back to Ubuntu OS state (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "Warm-Reset" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "Warm-Reset|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the WR cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD)]
> 	• NGA Testline for WR Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project: https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable I2C interface on THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-I2C traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of Warm-Reset (WR) PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickI2C).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-I2C touch device connected on THC1 still working normally after each WR cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted WR cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickI2C device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after WR. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the WR cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of WR cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC controller/driver after any warm-reset cycle.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-reset (can operate via HID-SPI/I2C interface, PIO/DMA)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] Warm Reset Cycles with THC1 (HID-SPI) Enabled only

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458398 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
> 	• The objective/intent of this test is to conduct a thorough system-level regression that includes 'N' iterations of PM Cycling, specifically Warm Resets (WR), while running test traffic through a single port THC1 that configured as HID-SPI interface.
>          (Note: 'N' iterations of WR Cycling will be specified/defined at TR level, the number will vary across different project milestones.)
> 	• This test is designed to implicitly encompass the verification of Fuses/Soft-straps loading, BIOS, SW Driver and THC Controller + TIC device initialization/enumeration sequences.
> 	• It will also assess and exercise the HID-SPI PIO and DMA traffic cycling on the THC1 port to ensure robust touch operation between the host controller and TIC device.
> 	• Solar SW tool will be utilized to initiate the PM regression/cycling test in Ubuntu OS environment.
> 	• Additionally, the THC controller will undergo a reset during each Warm-Reset cycle. This WR sequence will implicitly encompass the various elements outlined in the IP-HAS. (Note: Refer to TCD --> 'Overview' Section for more information.)
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
> 	• THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
> 	• RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
> 	• Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
> Test Steps / Procedure: 
> 	1. RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above; Ensure latest Solar tool is installed.
> 	2. Check if there is yellow bang on THC touch panel with the following commands
> 	sudo lspci                     #list pci devices
> 	lspci -kvv -s 00:10.0   #list pci device with very detail kernel info and which bus device function
> 	The expected results is where "Kernel driver in use: intel_quickspi" & "Kernel modules: intel_quickspi" can be observed, no yellow bang occurs
> 	3. In Ubuntu OS, go into the Solar folder, right click to "open in terminal".
> 	4. Enter the following command to open the Solar GUI
> 	sudo ./SolarPanel.sh
> 	5. In Solar Tool, validator/user may use the following settings below as example, which will enable 100 cycles of Warm-Reset with Yellow-Bank Check, Exit On Failure and Bypass Windows Login enabled.
> 		a. Tick to enable Yellow Bang Check in Advanced Verification PRE/POST Check tab.
> 		b. Tick to enable Exit On Failure in Knobs tab.
> 		c. Tick to enable Bypass Windows Login Knobs tab.
> 		d. Specify 'N' total number of cycles per loop/test in Test Time tab. (e.g. 'N' = 100)
> 		e. Specify Start Delay Time (Optional)
> 		f. Specify total sleep time allowed, i.e. total allowed time to reboot/reset/wale the system back to Ubuntu OS state (e.g. 180 seconds)
> 		g. Specify total Active Time in Ubuntu OS before starting the next PM cycle (e.g. 15 seconds)
> 		h. Select the "Warm-Reset" button. It will be shown as green box once clicked, and the "Warm-Reset|sec:180|sec:15|Timer" will be shown in the big blue box in the right -> which implied it is confirmed being set.
> 		i. Finally, click on the green play button to start the PM Regression/Cycling test.
> 	(Note: Alternatively, instead of setup a new settings every time for each PM regression test, validator/user may load the existing config solar .xml file with pre-existing settings saved previously.)
>
> 	(Note: The photo below serves as a reference for the Solar Tool interface, as the setup/settings shown are used for Cold-Reset test.)
> 	6. Wait for the WR cycling test to complete. Solar tool will return a Pass/Fail result in the "Test Status" section, the result will be shown in the log file as well.
>
> [Alternate Method: Automated Touch traffic using NGA Framework + IVT Released NGA supported WIM image  -- (More info TBD)]
> 	• NGA Testline for WR Regression existing example used in MTL-S/MTP-S project: https://nga.laas.intel.com/#/mtl_validation/planning/testlines/13898a8c-5b6d-4a13-873e-9eba1ef39b17
> 	• If using the existing testline above, validator/user will need to port it over to PTL compliant framework/environment,  using IVT released WIM image for PTL with NGA support and all its' dependencies included.
>
> SW Requirements:
> 	• RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
> 	• Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
> 	• RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI interface on THC1 port.
> 	• 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36A/37A) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC1 touch port/connector.
> 	• (Optional): Acute logic analyzer to monitor the HID-SPI traffic for debug/analysis purposes.
> (Note: Please check RVP schematic/layout to confirm the THC1 port naming label and location) 
>
> Dependencies:
> 	• BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
> 	• Using Solar tool, verify and ensure targeted 'N' number of Warm-Reset (WR) PM cycling count can be achieved successfully with NO error(s) returned/encountered. 
> 	• Make sure NO yellow-bank occurred on THC1 controller/Device Driver (i.e. QuickSPI).
> 	• Extra: Ensure that the touch functionality of the HID-SPI touch device connected on THC1 still working normally after each WR cycled. Alternatively, this functionality can be verified by conducting both PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests.
> (Note: If NGA/Automation is not enabled especially during the early PO phase, manual touch gesture method by human input can be used to verify if touch panel still functional.)
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
> 	• Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
> 	• Solar tool unable to pass/complete the targeted WR cycling count with error code returned, 
> 	• Yellow-bang occurred on THC - QuickSPI device and/or caused by any other drivers/devices, 
> 	• Touch functionality of the touch device broken and not working after WR. (i.e. PIO and DMA operations/traffic tests failed.)
> 	• Other IP/domains, WinOS, IFWI, SW driver or any other system/platform-level components utilized in this test that may caused the WR cycle failed to complete the targeted "N" Iterations of WR cycle.
>
> Intent Verify:
> 	• Verify no yellow-bang or driver errors occur on THC controller/driver after any warm-reset cycle.
> 	• Ensure the touch device maintains functionality post-reset (can operate via HID-SPI/I2C interface, PIO/DMA)
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [ERT] THC0 Fine-Grain Power Reporting Counter on PMC (via FG_PR_CNTR_THC0 register) [PM Telemetry#3]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458292 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The goal of this test is
>      verify that the PMC has control over increasing the FG_PR_CNTR_PGD
>      register counters for THC0/THC1, when THC0/THC1 is active state (not idle,
>      or D0i2) due to ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - The register counters for
>      THC0/THC1 will also stop incrementing and/or reset back to 0 when
>      THC0/THC1 enters idle states (D0i2).
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel
>      Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle
>      Capability
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements
>      met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described
>      in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Ensure the system enable and
>      support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and checked-out by FV-PM
>      Team at this point of time.)
>
>   - Read and record down the
>      initial values of THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counters on PMC registers,
>      and also check the PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers with the
>      following commands: 
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=0,
> keyword_list=[''])  OR  pcd.pmc.showsearch("fg_pr.*thc0")
>
>
>   - Configure
>      the D0i2_entry_timer register to a higher value 0x8 or 0x9 for longer
>      timeout, so that THC controller will only enter D0i2 after the timer
>      expired/timeout (e.g. D0i2_entry_timer = 0x8 >> 1second, 0x9
>      >> 10seconds.)
>
>   - Dump the following script.
>
> #
> Base and offsets
>
> BASE_ADDR
> = 0xFE010000
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_OFFSET
> = 0x50
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_OFFSET
> = 0x3A0
>
>
>
> #
> Absolute addresses
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR
> = BASE_ADDR + FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_OFFSET
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_ADDR
> = BASE_ADDR + FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_OFFSET
>
>
>
> print("Absolute
> Addresses:")
>
> print(f"FG_PR_CNTR_THC1
> (offset 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_OFFSET:X}) = 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR:X} ")
>
> print(f"FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1
> (offset 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_OFFSET:X}) =
> 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_ADDR:X}\n")
>
>
>
> fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old
> = int(pcd.pmc.arc.mem(FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR))
>
> fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old
> = int(pcd.pmc.tm.fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_0)
>
>
>
> print(f"Starting
> values: FG_PR_CNTR_THC1 = 0x{fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old:X} , FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1 =
> 0x{fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old:X}\n")
>
> print("Polling
> (Ctrl+C to stop)...")
>
>
>
> try:
>
>      while True:
>
>           fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new =
> int(pcd.pmc.arc.mem(FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR))
>
>           fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new =
> int(pcd.pmc.tm.fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_0)
>
>           if fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new !=
> fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old or fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new != fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old:
>
>                print(f"FG_PR_CNTR_THC1 =
> 0x{fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new:X} , FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1 =
> 0x{fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new:X}")
>
>                fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old =
> fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new
>
>                fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old =
> fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new
>
> except
> KeyboardInterrupt:
>
>      print("Stopped.")
>
>
>   - Force
>      THC0/THC1 into active state by touching the eDP display screen of the
>      touch panel as it will generate touch input interrupt + traffic to THC.
>
>   - As THC enter and remain at
>      D0-active state until the D0i2_entry_timer expired or timeout
>
>
>   - Read and
>       record THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counter values.
>
>   - Expectation: THC0/THC1
>       FG_PR_CNTR blocker counter values will keep incrementing over time, as
>       long as THC remained at D0-active state (non-D0i2). The script will print
>       out the new value if the register value changes (increases).
>
>
>
>   - Allow THC0/THC1 to
>      enter idle state by leaving the system idling with no touch events which
>      will allow THC0/THC1 to enter D0i2 as a result. (Note:
>      Idle time should be always larger than D0i2_entry_timer configured value
>      to allow timer expired/timeout to occur and THC will then entered D0i2
>      state)
>
>
>   - Read the
>       THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counter multiple times to verify it always
>       remained at the same value. (command:
>       pcd.pmc.showsearch("fg_pr.*thc1"))
>
>   - Expectation: THC0/THC1
>       FG_PR_CNTR blocker counter will stop incrementing and/or reset back to 0.
>
>
>
>   - Repeat steps #3 to #8 for
>      sanity check with back-to-back cycles repetitively to ensure these PMC
>      counters behaviour are consistent and behaved as expected.
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>      booted to Ubuntu OS
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>      HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0/THC1 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - PMC
>      increments FG_PR_CNTR register counters for THC0/THC1 as expected, when
>      THC0/THC1 is in active state (not idle or non-D0i2) due to ongoing touch
>      input interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - FG_PR_CNTR
>      register counters for THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing once THC0/THC1
>      entered idle state (D0i2) with NO ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to
>      THC controller.
>
>
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Ensure
>      counter increments are directly linked to touch panel activity and
>      interrupts.
>
>   - Confirm that
>      FG_PR_CNTR counters increment only when THC is in D0-active state (with
>      touch activity), and stop/reset when THC enters D0i2 idle state.
>
>   - Repeated
>      cycles should yield consistent and expected counter and register behavior.
>
>   - The system
>      should reliably transition between active and idle states, with no
>      unexpected errors or anomalies in counter values.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] [ERT] THC1 Fine-Grain Power Reporting Counter on PMC (via FG_PR_CNTR_THC1 register) [PM Telemetry#3]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458293 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The goal of this test is
>      verify that the PMC has control over increasing the FG_PR_CNTR_PGD
>      register counters for THC0/THC1, when THC0/THC1 is active state (not idle,
>      or D0i2) due to ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - The register counters for
>      THC0/THC1 will also stop incrementing and/or reset back to 0 when
>      THC0/THC1 enters idle states (D0i2).
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel
>      Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle
>      Capability
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      successfully booted to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements
>      met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described
>      in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - Ensure the system enable and
>      support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and checked-out by FV-PM
>      Team at this point of time.)
>
>   - Read and record down the
>      initial values of THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counters on PMC registers,
>      and also check the PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers with the
>      following commands: 
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=1,
> keyword_list=[''])  OR  pcd.pmc.showsearch("fg_pr.*thc1")
>
>
>   - Configure
>      the D0i2_entry_timer register to a higher value 0x8 or 0x9 for longer
>      timeout, so that THC controller will only enter D0i2 after the timer
>      expired/timeout (e.g. D0i2_entry_timer = 0x8 >> 1second, 0x9
>      >> 10seconds.)
>
>   - Dump the following script.
>
> #
> Base and offsets
>
> BASE_ADDR
> = 0xFE010000
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_OFFSET
> = 0x50
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_OFFSET
> = 0x3A0
>
>
>
> #
> Absolute addresses
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR
> = BASE_ADDR + FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_OFFSET
>
> FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_ADDR
> = BASE_ADDR + FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_OFFSET
>
>
>
> print("Absolute
> Addresses:")
>
> print(f"FG_PR_CNTR_THC1
> (offset 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_OFFSET:X}) = 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR:X} ")
>
> print(f"FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1
> (offset 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_OFFSET:X}) =
> 0x{FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1_ADDR:X}\n")
>
>
>
> fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old
> = int(pcd.pmc.arc.mem(FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR))
>
> fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old
> = int(pcd.pmc.tm.fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_0)
>
>
>
> print(f"Starting
> values: FG_PR_CNTR_THC1 = 0x{fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old:X} , FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1 =
> 0x{fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old:X}\n")
>
> print("Polling
> (Ctrl+C to stop)...")
>
>
>
> try:
>
>      while True:
>
>           fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new =
> int(pcd.pmc.arc.mem(FG_PR_CNTR_THC1_ADDR))
>
>           fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new =
> int(pcd.pmc.tm.fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_0)
>
>           if fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new !=
> fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old or fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new != fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old:
>
>                print(f"FG_PR_CNTR_THC1 =
> 0x{fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new:X} , FG_PR_CNTR_PGD_THC1 =
> 0x{fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new:X}")
>
>                fg_pr_cntr_thc1_old =
> fg_pr_cntr_thc1_new
>
>                fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_old =
> fg_pr_cntr_pgd_thc1_new
>
> except
> KeyboardInterrupt:
>
>      print("Stopped.")
>
>
>   - Force
>      THC0/THC1 into active state by touching the eDP display screen of the
>      touch panel as it will generate touch input interrupt + traffic to THC.
>
>   - As THC enter and remain at
>      D0-active state until the D0i2_entry_timer expired or timeout
>
>
>   - Read and
>       record THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counter values.
>
>   - Expectation: THC0/THC1
>       FG_PR_CNTR blocker counter values will keep incrementing over time, as
>       long as THC remained at D0-active state (non-D0i2). The script will print
>       out the new value if the register value changes (increases).
>
>
>
>   - Allow THC0/THC1 to
>      enter idle state by leaving the system idling with no touch events which
>      will allow THC0/THC1 to enter D0i2 as a result. (Note:
>      Idle time should be always larger than D0i2_entry_timer configured value
>      to allow timer expired/timeout to occur and THC will then entered D0i2
>      state)
>
>
>   - Read the
>       THC0/THC1 FG_PR_CNTR register counter multiple times to verify it always
>       remained at the same value. (command:
>       pcd.pmc.showsearch("fg_pr.*thc1"))
>
>   - Expectation: THC0/THC1
>       FG_PR_CNTR blocker counter will stop incrementing and/or reset back to 0.
>
>
>
>   - Repeat steps #3 to #8 for
>      sanity check with back-to-back cycles repetitively to ensure these PMC
>      counters behaviour are consistent and behaved as expected.
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>      booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>      HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0/THC1 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - PMC
>      increments FG_PR_CNTR register counters for THC0/THC1 as expected, when
>      THC0/THC1 is in active state (not idle or non-D0i2) due to ongoing touch
>      input interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - FG_PR_CNTR
>      register counters for THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing once THC0/THC1
>      entered idle state (D0i2) with NO ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to
>      THC controller.
>
>
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Ensure
>      counter increments are directly linked to touch panel activity and
>      interrupts.
>
>   - Confirm that
>      FG_PR_CNTR counters increment only when THC is in D0-active state (with
>      touch activity), and stop/reset when THC enters D0i2 idle state.
>
>   - Repeated
>      cycles should yield consistent and expected counter and register behavior.
>
>   - The system
>      should reliably transition between active and idle states, with no
>      unexpected errors or anomalies in counter values.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [NVL+] Enabling THC0 Port Only (with THC1 Disabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458270 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>
>   - The test
>      objective is to verify that THC0 and THC1 are independent PCI devices that can be disabled independently and are assigned as root functions (function 0 in BDF).
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS
>      settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>   - Ensure that the system has the latest firmware and drivers installed.
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure:
>
>
>   - Access the BIOS/UEFI settings and navigate to
>      the PCI device configuration section.
>
>   - Ensure the THC0
>      is assigned to Dev 17 func0 and THC1 is assigned to Dev 16 func0.
>
>   - Disable THC0;
>      Save the configuration and reboot the system; Verify that THC0 is not
>      visible in the operating system's device manager or PCI device list.
>
>   - Disable THC1;
>      Save the configuration and reboot the system; Verify that THC1 is not
>      visible in the operating system's device manager or PCI device list.
>
>   - Then, enable THC0
>      and THC1 independently; Save the configuration and reboot the system;
>      Verify that THC0 and THC1 are visible and functional in the operating
>      system respectively.
>
>   - Check the BDF
>      assignment in the OS using appropriate tool (e.g. "lspci"
>      command).
>
>   - Ensure that THC0
>      is assigned to Dev 17 func0 and THC1 is assigned to Dev 16 func0.
>
>   - Verify the THC
>      independence; Disable THC0 and verify THC1 remains enabled and functional;
>      Disabled THC1 and verify THC0 remains enabled and functional.
>
>   - Lastly, ensure
>      that both THC0 and THC1 are root functions (function 0 in BDF).
>
>
>
> Post-Test
> Conditions:
>
>
>   - Re-enable THC0 and THC1 in
>      BIOS/UEFI settings if they were disabled during testing. 
>
>   - Ensure that the system is
>      functioning correctly with both THC0 and THC1 enabled.
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - Operating System with support for PCI device management
>
>   - BIOS/UEFI configuration utility
>
>
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - System with
>      NVL-PCH chipset
>
>   - BIOS/UEFI
>      that supports independent disabling of THC0 and THC1
>
>   - PCI devices
>      with THC0 and THC1
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS implementation according
>      to THC BWG requirements / config
>
>   - Latest drivers for THC0 and
>      THC1 installed on the system
>
>   - Tools for verifying PCI
>      device visibility (e.g. Device Mabager, "lspci" command)
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Verify THC0
>      and THC1 ports are independent ports, one port can be enabled and
>      functional without enabling another.
>
>   - Validate the
>      BDF assignment is as expected and match the THC design architecture.
>
>
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to
>      meet the passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Record the
>      results of each test case, including any discrepancies or issues
>      encountered.
>
>   - Provide
>      screenshots or logs as evidence of the test results.
>
>   - Document any
>      additional observations or recommendations.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [NVL+] Enabling THC1 Port Only (with THC0 Disabled)

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458271 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description :
>
>   - The test objective is to verify that THC0 and THC1 are independent PCI devices that can be disabled independently and are assigned as root functions (function 0 in BDF).
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>   - Ensure that the system has the latest firmware and drivers installed.
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
>   - Access the BIOS/UEFI settings and navigate to the PCI device configuration section.
>   - Ensure the THC0 is assigned to Dev 17 func0 and THC1 is assigned to Dev 16 func0.
>   - Disable THC0; Save the configuration and reboot the system; Verify that THC0 is not visible in the operating system's device manager or PCI device list.
>   - Disable THC1; Save the configuration and reboot the system; Verify that THC1 is not visible in the operating system's device manager or PCI device list.
>   - Then, enable THC0 and THC1 independently; Save the configuration and reboot the system; Verify that THC0 and THC1 are visible and functional in the operating system respectively.
>   - Check the BDF assignment in the OS using appropriate tool (e.g. "lspci" command).
>   - Ensure that THC0 is assigned to Dev 17 func0 and THC1 is assigned to Dev 16 func0.
>   - Verify the THC independence; Disable THC0 and verify THC1 remains enabled and functional; Disabled THC1 and verify THC0 remains enabled and functional.
>   - Lastly, ensure that both THC0 and THC1 are root functions (function 0 in BDF).
>
> Post-Test Conditions:
>
>   - Re-enable THC0 and THC1 in BIOS/UEFI settings if they were disabled during testing.
>   - Ensure that the system is functioning correctly with both THC0 and THC1 enabled.
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - Operating System with support for PCI device management
>   - BIOS/UEFI configuration utility
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - System with NVL-PCH chipset
>   - BIOS/UEFI that supports independent disabling of THC0 and THC1
>   - PCI devices with THC0 and THC1
>
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS implementation according to THC BWG requirements / config
>   - Latest drivers for THC0 and THC1 installed on the system
>   - Tools for verifying PCI device visibility (e.g. Device Mabager, "lspci" command)
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - Verify THC0 and THC1 ports are independent ports, one port can be enabled and functional without enabling another.
>   - Validate the BDF assignment is as expected and match the THC design architecture.
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Record the results of each test case, including any discrepancies or issues encountered.
>   - Provide screenshots or logs as evidence of the test results.
>   - Document any additional observations or recommendations.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/

### ✅ [TC] [PTL+] THC0 IP Telemetry Data via PMC IP_TELEM_MAILBOX mechanism [PM Telemetry#6]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458290 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - To validate
>      the new PMC IP Telemetry Mailbox mechanism specifically the
>      PULL_IP_TELEMETRY command triggers PMC HW/FW to collect telemetry data
>      from THC0 IPs and place them into SSRAM.
>
>   - To
>      validate and provide test coverage for NVL PCR# 15014380322
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled as HIDSPI
>      interface at 17MHz
>      in BIOS settings.
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled as HIDI2C interface at 100kHz (Standard-Speed Mode) in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> Serial-IO Configuration -> Disabled both
>      I2C4 and I2C5
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> PCH Energy Reporting -> Enabled
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> Energy Reporting Debug Mode -> Enabled
>
>   - Enable Virtual Keyboard (Note: To make sure the touch
>      functionality is working)
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure:
>
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to UEFI shell/Ubuntu OS with all
>      the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings
>      accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>
>   - In
>      PythonSV, enter the following command:
>
>
>   - sys.path.append(r"\\pgcv04a-cifs.png.intel.com\mve_fvpg_001\IVE_CPV\PCH_Post_Silicon\Users\willychi\dev\thc_script")
>
>   - import thc_registers
>
>   - thc_registers.ip_tm_mailbox_test(thc_port=0)
>       (Note:
>       Enter the THC port number which is tested.)
>
>
>
>   - The command will logs the
>      start of the pre-trigger phase and the current status of the IP telemetry
>      mailbox for the specified THC port.
>
>   - Then the command instructs
>      the PMC firmware to fetch telemetry data from THC registers. Example as
>      below:
>
>
>   - The
>      command will return the final result (PASS or FAIL) for the test.
>
>   - Attempt multiple touches on
>      the touch panel and run the command again to verify the read values for
>      THC counter registers are increasing.
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>      booted to UEFI Shell or WinOS
>
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/I2C driver
>      in WinOS (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C driver for THC0 port). 
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>      HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - RVP booted
>      to UEFI or WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both
>      THC0 and/or THC1 ports with touch devices connected.
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - PythonSV
>      (via USB2DBC or XDP) connected on host system.
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - No
>      errors and Yellow-Bang observed on THC0 / THC1 port.
>
>   - The
>      touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and
>      validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>
>   - The
>      test should return "PASS" as the final result.
>
>   - The
>      THC counter registers must be increasing after every touch gestures.
>
>
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm that
>      the PULL_IP_TELEMETRY command successfully triggers the PMC firmware to
>      collect and return telemetry data from the specified IPs.
>
>   - After
>      performing touch actions on the panel, rerun the test and check that
>      telemetry counters (such as touch event counts) increase as expected,
>      demonstrating that the mailbox is capturing real-time activity.
>
>   - Ensure the
>      test script can read the mailbox status before and after the command,
>      trigger the telemetry fetch, and report PASS/FAIL based on the actual
>      mailbox and SRAM contents.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [PTL+] THC1 IP Telemetry Data via PMC IP_TELEM_MAILBOX mechanism [PM Telemetry#6]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458291 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - To validate the new PMC IP Telemetry Mailbox mechanism specifically the PULL_IP_TELEMETRY command triggers PMC HW/FW to collect telemetry data from THC0 IPs and place them into SSRAM.
>   - To validate and provide test coverage for NVL PCR# 15014380322
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled as HIDSPI interface at 17MHz in BIOS settings.
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled as HIDI2C interface at 100kHz (Standard-Speed Mode) in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> Serial-IO Configuration -> Disabled both I2C4 and I2C5
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> PCH Energy Reporting -> Enabled
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> Energy Reporting Debug Mode -> Enabled
>   - Enable Virtual Keyboard (Note: To make sure the touch functionality is working)
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
>   - RVP successfully booted to UEFI shell/Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>
>   - In PythonSV, enter the following command:
>   - sys.path.append(r"\\pgcv04a-cifs.png.intel.com\mve_fvpg_001\IVE_CPV\PCH_Post_Silicon\Users\willychi\dev\thc_script")
>   - import thc_registers
>   - thc_registers.ip_tm_mailbox_test(thc_port=1) (Note: Enter the THC port number which is tested.)
>
>   - The command will logs the start of the pre-trigger phase and the current status of the IP telemetry mailbox for the specified THC port.
>   - Then the command instructs the PMC firmware to fetch telemetry data from THC registers. Example as below:
>
>   - The command will return the final result (PASS or FAIL) for the test.
>   - Attempt multiple touches on the touch panel and run the command again to verify the read values for THC counter registers are increasing.
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to UEFI Shell or WinOS
>   - Install SW HID-SPI/I2C driver in WinOS (i.e. QuickSPI/QuickI2C driver for THC0/THC1 port). 
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu/WinOS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0/THC1port.   
>   - 3PE POR touch panel  (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
> Dependencies:
>
>   - RVP booted to UEFI or WinOS with feature rework to enable I2C/SPI interface for both THC0 and/or THC1 ports with touch devices connected.
>   - BIOS enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>   - PythonSV (via USB2DBC or XDP) connected on host system.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - No errors and Yellow-Bang observed on THC0 / THC1 port.
>   - The touch functionalities of the Touch Panel are fully operational and validated, ensuring all touch gestures/operations perform as anticipated.
>   - The test should return "PASS" as the final result.
>   - The THC counter registers must be increasing after every touch gestures.
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm that the PULL_IP_TELEMETRY command successfully triggers the PMC firmware to collect and return telemetry data from the specified IPs.
>   - After performing touch actions on the panel, rerun the test and check that telemetry counters (such as touch event counts) increase as expected, demonstrating that the mailbox is capturing real-time activity.
>   - Ensure the test script can read the mailbox status before and after the command, trigger the telemetry fetch, and report PASS/FAIL based on the actual mailbox and SRAM contents.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
> Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OSBuilt in QuickSPI kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [S0ix_LPM] THC0 D3 Blocker/Breaker Counters on PMC [PM Telemetry#8, 14]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458294 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test
> Description:
>
>
>   - The goal of
>      this test is to verify that the PMC correctly increments the D3 blocker
>      counters for THC0/THC1 as expected, when SUT idling with display-ON S0ix.
>
>   - D3 blocker
>      counters for THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing, when SUT entered sleep
>      state with display-OFF S0ix.
>
>
>
> Pre-Test
> Configuration:
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>      PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>      the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel
>      Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle
>      Capability
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps
> / Procedure:
>
>
>   - RVP successfully boot to Ubuntu OS with all
>      the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS
>      settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section
>      above.
>
>   - Ensure the system enable and
>      support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and checked-out by FV-PM
>      Team at this point of time.)
>
>
>   - Read and
>      record down the initial values of THC0/THC1 D3 blocker counters (on PMC
>      registers), and also PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers.
>
>   - Trigger S0ix blocking
>      condition due to THC0/THC1 not in D3 power state, by leaving the system
>      idling after Ubuntu OS boot up (S0ix with display-ON).
>
>   - As THC only allowed SoC/PCD
>      to enter up to S0i2.0 low power substate after THC entered D0i2 state
>      (according to PMC LPM Dashboard).
>
>
>   - Enter the
>       following commands to read the THC0/THC1 D3 blocker counter values and
>       dump the blocker counter registers multiple times to verify it is always
>       incrementing.
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_breaker_counter_control.inc_s0ix_brk_cntrs.write(1);                                                                                 
> #workaround to write to breaker counter
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=0,
> keyword_list=["d3","pg","fg_pr"],
> duplicates_enable=True);  #print table to
> show the blocker & breaker counter values
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Trigger non-blocking
>      condition on S0ix by putting the system into Modern Standy or Sleep state
>      (S0ix with display-OFF).
>
>
>   - System will go
>       into deep sleep and enter the command again to read the blocker counter
>       registers, it will remain the same and stop incrementing.
>
>
>
>
> unlock();
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_breaker_counter_control.inc_s0ix_brk_cntrs.write(1);                                                                                 
> #workaround to write to breaker counter
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=0,
> keyword_list=["d3","pg","fg_pr"],
> duplicates_enable=True);  #print table to
> show the blocker & breaker counter values
>
>
>
>
>   - Dump the following registers
>      to verify that THC0/THC1 is in D3 status (system in S0i2.1 or S0i2.2
>      state).
>
>
>   - Wake the system from CS/S0ix using Power button, dump the register
>      again, observe the THC0/THC1 not in D3 anymore.
>
>
>   - Repeat steps #4 to #8
>      multiple times to ensure these PMC counters behaviour are as expected and
>      consistent.
>
>
>
> SW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>      booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>      HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
> HW
> Requirements:
>
>
>   - RVP
>      booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>      THC0/THC1 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel 
>      (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>      touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>      cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>   - BIOS
>      enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>   - SUT able to
>      support PKGC-10 and S0ix features.
>
>
>
> Test Pass
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - THC0 and
>      THC1 blocker counters increment only when THC0/THC1 is not in D3
>      state (SUT in S0ix with display-ON).
>
>   - Blocker
>      Counters stop incrementing when THC0/THC1 entered D3 state (SUT in
>      S0ix with display-OFF).
>
>
>
> Test Fail
> Criteria:
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>      passing criteria above.
>
>
>
> Intent
> Verify:
>
>
>   - Confirm that
>      the PMC D3 blocker counters increment only when S0ix entry is blocked (THC
>      not being in D3), and remain unchanged when S0ix entry is allowed (THC in
>      D3).
>
>   - Ensure
>      THC0/THC1 and system registers reflect correct power states during idle,
>      sleep, and wake cycles.
>
>   - Repeated
>      cycles of blocking and non-blocking conditions should yield consistent and
>      expected counter and register behaviour.
>
>   - The system
>      should reliably enter and exit S0ix/Modern Standby, with no unexpected
>      errors or register anomalies.
>
>   - Telemetry
>      data and counters must accurately reflect the system’s power state and
>      blocker conditions throughout the test.
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
> SW Requirements:
>
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [S0ix_LPM] THC0 LTR Blocker/Breaker Counters on PMC  [PM Telemetry#10]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458297 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> - 
>
>
> Test Description:
>
>
>
>
>   - The objective of this test
>       is to verify that the PMC correctly increments the LTR blocker counters
>       for THC0/THC1, when LTR values configured on THC0/THC1 are below the
>       programmable PMC threshold limit.
>   - The LTR blocker counters for
>       THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing, when LTR values configured on THC0/THC1
>       are above the programmable PMC threshold limit.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled as HIDSPI
>       interface at 17MHz
>       in BIOS settings.
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled as HIDI2C interface at 100kHz (Standard-Speed Mode) in BIOS
>       settings.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>       PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>       the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel
>       Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle
>       Capability
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
> 1. RVP successfully boot to Ubuntu
>       OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>       the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>       section above.
>
> 2. Ensure the system enable and
>       support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and checked-out by FV-PM
>       Team at this point of time.)
>
> 3. Read the PMC
>       threshold register, Expected default should be 0xFA (~250μs).
>
> 4. Record this value and it
>       will be used as reference point for below/above threshold testing.
>
>
>
>
> (Add. Info: LPM_LTR_THRESHOLD will only affected the
>  S0ix residency counters, but will not affect the LTR blocker counters)
>
>
>
> 5. Configure the
>       blocker counter mode by setting the inc_method to 1 and the
>       lpm_blk_cntr_pol to 1.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.inc_method
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd..pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.lpm_blk_cntr_pol
>  = 1
>
>
>
> 6. Check and verify
>       that the system is able to have S0ix residency; read and record the
>       initial S0ix residency values.
>
>
>
>
>
> 7. Read
>       and record the initial values of THC0/THC1 blocker counters + LTR config [LTR_THC0_BLC_CNTR=0X0,
>       LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0=0x8BFF (~1048µs)]
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=0,
>  keyword_list=["LTR"], duplicates_enable=True)
>
>
>
>
>
> 8. Read
>       the LTR control with the following command and disable the LP_LTR_EN,
>       LP_LTR_REQ, ACTIVE_LTR_EN, and ACTIVE_LTR_REQ bits.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.show()                 #read the LTR control
>  registers
>
>
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl[3:0] = 0b0000    #disable bit 0 to bit 3
>
>
>
>
>
> 9. Configure/trigger
>       THCO/THC1 to send below threshold LTR value to PMC, LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0/_THC1
>       < LTR_COUNTER_THRESHOLD (Note: refer to "THC RLTR
>       Programming to PMC via QoS_DMD test case for more details.) Alternatively, set the ACT_LTR_VAL to '1' and ACT_LTR_SCALE to
>  '0', with the intent of stretching it to the maximum.
>
>
>
> 10. Enable
>       the ACTIVE_LTR_EN and ACTIVE_LTR_REQ.
>
>
>
>
>
> 11. Dump
>       the LTR related registers again to observe the block counter increment
>       and register LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0 value change (due to 'ACTIVE_LTR_EN' &
>       'ACTIVE_LTR_REQ').
>
>
>
>
>
> 12. With the block counter increasing, observe that the
>       residency values do not increment as S0ix is blocked; Read
>       the values of THC0/THC1 blocker counters, counter must be rapidly
>       incrementing across reads.
>
>
>
>
>
> 13. Configure/trigger
>       THC0/THC1 to send above threshold LTR value to PMC, LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0/_THC1
>       > LTR_COUNTER_THRESHOLD (Note: refer to "THC RLTR
>       Programming to PMC via QoS_DMD test case for more details.) Alternatively, disable LTR control register bits and enable
>       LP_LTR_EN and LP_LTR_REQ, as shown in the commands below:
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl[3:0] = 0b0000    #disable bit 0 to bit 3
>
>
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl[3:0] = 0b1100    #enable LP_LTR_EN and LP_LTR_REQ
>
>
>
> 14. Dump
>       the registers again, the block counter should stop incrementing and
>       LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0 value returns back to 0x8BFF.
>
>
>
>
>
> 15. Observe
>       the residency values, it continue to increase with no S0ix blocking.
>       THC0/THC1 blocker counter must be frozen (same value across all reads)
>
>
>
>
>
> 16. Repeat
>       steps #8 to #15 multiple times back-to-back to confirm consistent
>       behaviour. Verify counter resumes incrementing when below-threshold LTR
>       is re-sent and counter stops again when above-threshold LTR is restored.
>
> 17. Change the threshold to a
>       different value for threshold programmability check. Verify the counter
>       stops (LTR> new threshold) and restore threshold to original value.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>       booted to Ubuntu OS.
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>       HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>
>
>   - RVP
>       booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>       THC0/THC1 port.   
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e.
>       BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>       touch port/connector.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>       cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>
>
>   - BIOS
>       enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 blocker
>       counters increment only when their LTR is below the threshold.
>   - Blocker counters stop
>       incrementing when LTR is above the threshold.
>   - Blocker counters respond to
>       threshold changes.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>       passing criteria above.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>
>
>   - Verify that the S0ix
>       residency stalls when any IP transmits LTR below threshold and LTR
>       blocker counters properly increment.
>   - Confirm that S0ix residency
>       variables increment and LTR blocker counters stop incrementing when all
>       LTR are above threshold.
>   - Ensure each register/control
>       bit operation reflects in observable system (such as counter behaviour).
>   - Counter increments, S0ix
>       block/allow, telemetry logs should be consistent across all platforms,
>       program versions, IP variants, and test cycles.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Ecosystem
>  SW Requirements:
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

---

### ✅ [TC] [S0ix_LPM] THC0 PG Blocker/Breaker Counters on PMC [PM Telemetry#8, 14]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458299 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> - 
>
>
>
>
> Test Description:
>
>
>
>   - The objective
>       of this test is to verify that the PMC correctly increments the PG
>       blocker/breaker counters for THC0 and/or THC1 as expected, when THC0/THC1
>       is active state (not idle, or D0i2) due to ongoing touch input
>       interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - Also to ensure
>       the PG blocker/breaker counters for THC0 and/or THC1 will stop
>       incrementing once THC0/THC1 entered idle state (or D0i2) with
>       NO ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to THC
>       controller.  
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>       PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>       the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>
>   - THC0 enabled as HIDSPI interface at 17MHz while THC1
>       enabled as HIDI2C interface at 100kHz (Standard Speed Mode) 
>
>   - Go to Intel Advanced Menu -> PCH-IO
>       Configuration-> Serial-IO Configuration -> Disabled both I2C4 and
>       I2C5 
>
>   - Intel Advanced Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration-> PCH
>       Energy Reporting -> Enabled
>
>   - Intel Advanced Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration->
>       Energy Reporting Debug Mode -> Enabled
>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel
>       Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle
>       Capability
>
>   - Enable Virtual KB (Note: To make sure the touch
>       functionality is working)
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
>
>
>   - RVP successfully boot to Ubuntu
>       OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>       the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>       section above.
>
>   - Ensure
>       the system enable and support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and
>       checkout by FV-PM Team at this point of time.)
>
>   - Read and
>       record down the initial values of THC0/THC1 PG blocker/breaker counters
>       on PMC registers, and also PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers.
>
>
>
> To dump all PWRST status register
>  for THC0 and THC1 instances:
>
>
>
>
> To dump all D3 and PG related
>  counter registers for both THC0 and THC1 instances on PMC side:
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Configure the
>       blocker counter mode by setting the inc_method to 1 and the
>       lpm_blk_cntr_pol to 1.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.inc_method
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.lpm_blk_cntr_pol
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.lpm_blk_cntr_en
>  = 1
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Verify energy reporting is enabled.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.pm_cfg.er_en
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.etr3.er_debug_en
>  = 1
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Read all the relevant counters when THC0 is in D0.
>       Observe the block counter is incrementing when touch activity is
>       triggered.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> thc.registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=0,
>  keyword_list=["fg_pr","pg"], duplicates_enable=True)
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Adjust the D0i2 entry timer to observe counter
>       sensitivity. The blocker counter growth rate depends on the D0i2 entry
>       timing.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_d0i2_et.d0i2_entry_timer
>  = 0b1000
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Trigger non-blocking condition by stop touching the
>       device and wait for THC0 to enter D0i2. 
>
>   - Read again
>       the relevant counters when THC0 is in D0i2. Observe the blocker counter
>       should stop/slow down (THC0 no longer blocking PG), the breaker counter
>       should start incrementing (THC0 breaking PG blockage by entering D0i2).
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_breaker_counter_control.inc_s0ix_brk_cntrs.write(1);
>
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=0,
>  keyword_list=["fg_pr", "pg"], duplicates_enable=True);
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Clear the counters and repeat steps #6 to #9
>       back-to-back multiple times to ensure these PMC counters behaviour are
>       consistent and behaved as expected.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>       booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>       HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>
>   - RVP
>       booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>       THC0/THC1 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e.
>       BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>       touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>       cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>
>   - BIOS
>       enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>
>   - Verify that the PMC
>       increments the PG blocker/breaker counters for THC0/THC1 when THC0/THC1
>       is in an active state (not idle or D0i2) as a result of ongoing touch
>       input interrupt/frames to the THC controller.
>
>   - Confirm that the PG
>       blocker/breaker counters for THC0/THC1 stop incrementing once THC0/THC1
>       enters the idle state (or D0i2) with no ongoing touch input
>       interrupt/frames to the THC controller.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>       passing criteria above.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>
>   - Verify the PG blocker
>       counter increment steadily when THC is forced into D0 (active via touch
>       gestures), showing S0ix block due to touch activity.
>
>   - Ensure the counters stop
>       incrementing when THC enters D0i2 (idle, no touch) and the power state
>       transitions reflect expected idle behaviour.
>
>   - Each state change
>       (active/idle) should be mirrored in both the PWRST and blocker/breaker
>       counter values, confirming register logic.
>
>   - The counter behaviour must
>       remain consistent across repeated cycles and system instances.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
