### ✅ [TC] [S0ix_LPM] THC1 D3 Blocker/Breaker Counters on PMC [PM Telemetry#8, 14]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458296 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The goal of this test is to verify that the PMC correctly increments the D3 blocker counters for THC0/THC1 as expected, when SUT idling with display-ON S0ix.
>   - D3 blocker counters for THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing, when SUT entered sleep state with display-OFF S0ix.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle Capability
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
>   - RVP successfully boot to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
>   - Ensure the system enable and support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and checked-out by FV-PM Team at this point of time.)
>
>   - Read and record down the initial values of THC0/THC1 D3 blocker counters (on PMC registers), and also PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers.
>   - Trigger S0ix blocking condition due to THC0/THC1 not in D3 power state, by leaving the system idling after Ubuntu OS boot up (S0ix with display-ON).
>   - As THC only allowed SoC/PCD to enter up to S0i2.0 low power substate after THC entered D0i2 state (according to PMC LPM Dashboard).
>   - Enter the following commands to read the THC0/THC1 D3 blocker counter values and dump the blocker counter registers multiple times to verify it is always incrementing.
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_breaker_counter_control.inc_s0ix_brk_cntrs.write(1);                                                                                  #workaround to write to breaker counter
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=1, keyword_list=["d3","pg","fg_pr"], duplicates_enable=True);  #print table to show the blocker & breaker counter values
>
>
>
>
>   - Trigger non-blocking condition on S0ix by putting the system into Modern Standy or Sleep state (S0ix with display-OFF).
>   - System will go into deep sleep and enter the command again to read the blocker counter registers, it will remain the same and stop incrementing.
>
>
> unlock();
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_breaker_counter_control.inc_s0ix_brk_cntrs.write(1);                                                                                  #workaround to write to breaker counter
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=1, keyword_list=["d3","pg","fg_pr"], duplicates_enable=True);  #print table to show the blocker & breaker counter values
>
>
>   - Dump the following registers to verify that THC0/THC1 is in D3 status (system in S0i2.1 or S0i2.2 state).
>
>   - Wake the system from CS/S0ix using Power button, dump the register again, observe the THC0/THC1 not in D3 anymore.
>
>   - Repeat steps #4 to #8 multiple times to ensure these PMC counters behaviour are as expected and consistent.
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0/THC1 port.   
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>   - SUT able to support PKGC-10 and S0ix features.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 blocker counters increment only when THC0/THC1 is not in D3 state (SUT in S0ix with display-ON).
>   - Blocker Counters stop incrementing when THC0/THC1 entered D3 state (SUT in S0ix with display-OFF).
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Confirm that the PMC D3 blocker counters increment only when S0ix entry is blocked (THC not being in D3), and remain unchanged when S0ix entry is allowed (THC in D3).
>   - Ensure THC0/THC1 and system registers reflect correct power states during idle, sleep, and wake cycles.
>   - Repeated cycles of blocking and non-blocking conditions should yield consistent and expected counter and register behaviour.
>   - The system should reliably enter and exit S0ix/Modern Standby, with no unexpected errors or register anomalies.
>   - Telemetry data and counters must accurately reflect the system’s power state and blocker conditions throughout the test.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [S0ix_LPM] THC1 LTR Blocker/Breaker Counters on PMC  [PM Telemetry#10]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458298 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>   - The objective of this test is to verify that the PMC correctly increments the LTR blocker counters for THC0/THC1, when LTR values configured on THC0/THC1 are below the programmable PMC threshold limit.
>   - The LTR blocker counters for THC0/THC1 will stop incrementing, when LTR values configured on THC0/THC1 are above the programmable PMC threshold limit.
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>   - THC0 port will required to be enabled as HIDSPI interface at 17MHz in BIOS settings.
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled as HIDI2C interface at 100kHz (Standard-Speed Mode) in BIOS settings.
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to the BKM settings below to enable THC0 port.>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle Capability
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
> 1. RVP successfully boot to Ubuntu OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration' section above.
> 2.  Ensure the system enable and support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and checked-out by FV-PM Team at this point of time.)
> 3. Read the PMC threshold register, Expected default should be 0xFA (~250μs).
> 4. Record this value and it will be used as reference point for below/above threshold testing.
> (Add. Info: LPM_LTR_THRESHOLD will only affected the S0ix residency counters, but will not affect the LTR blocker counters)
>
> 5. Configure the blocker counter mode by setting the inc_method to 1 and the lpm_blk_cntr_pol to 1.
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.inc_method = 1
> pcd..pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.lpm_blk_cntr_pol = 1
>
>
> 6. Check and verify that the system is able to have S0ix residency; read and record the initial S0ix residency values.
>
>       7. Read and record the initial values of THC0/THC1 blocker counters + LTR config [LTR_THC1_BLC_CNTR=0X0, LTR_C2P2_IP_THC1=0x8BFF (~1048µs)]
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=1, keyword_list=["LTR"], duplicates_enable=True)
>
> 8. Read the LTR control with the following command and disable the LP_LTR_EN, LP_LTR_REQ, ACTIVE_LTR_EN, and ACTIVE_LTR_REQ bits.
>
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl.show()                 #read the LTR control registers
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl[3:0] = 0b0000    #disable bit 0 to bit 3
>
>   9. Configure/trigger THCO/THC1 to send below threshold LTR value to PMC, LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0/_THC1 < LTR_COUNTER_THRESHOLD (Note: refer to "THC RLTR Programming to PMC via QoS_DMD test case for more details.)
> Alternatively, set the ACT_LTR_VAL to '1' and ACT_LTR_SCALE to '0', with the intent of stretching it to the maximum.
>
> 10. Enable the ACTIVE_LTR_EN and ACTIVE_LTR_REQ.
>
> 11. Dump the LTR related registers again to observe the block counter increment and register LTR_C2P2_IP_THC1 value change (due to 'ACTIVE_LTR_EN' & 'ACTIVE_LTR_REQ').
>
> 12. With the block counter increasing, observe that the residency values do not increment as S0ix is blocked; Read the values of THC0/THC1 blocker counters, counter must be rapidly incrementing across reads.
>
> 13. Configure/trigger THC0/THC1 to send above threshold LTR value to PMC, LTR_C2P2_IP_THC0/_THC1 > LTR_COUNTER_THRESHOLD (Note: refer to "THC RLTR Programming to PMC via QoS_DMD test case for more details.)
>
> Alternatively, disable LTR control register bits and enable LP_LTR_EN and LP_LTR_REQ, as shown in the commands below:
>
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl[3:0] = 0b0000    #disable bit 0 to bit 3
> pcd.thc0.mem.thc_m_cmn_ltr_ctrl[3:0] = 0b1100    #enable LP_LTR_EN and LP_LTR_REQ
>
> 14. Dump the registers again, the block counter should stop incrementing and LTR_C2P2_IP_THC1 value returns back to 0x8BFF.
>
> 15. Observe the residency values, it continue to increase with no S0ix blocking. THC0/THC1 blocker counter must be frozen (same value across all reads)
>
> 16. Repeat steps #8 to #15 multiple times back-to-back to confirm consistent behaviour. Verify counter resumes incrementing when below-threshold LTR is re-sent and counter stops again when above-threshold LTR is restored.
>
> 17. Change the threshold to a different value for threshold programmability check. Verify the counter stops (LTR> new threshold) and restore threshold to original value.
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP/SUT booted to Ubuntu OS.
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>   - RVP booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on THC0/THC1 port.   
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e. BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1 touch port/connector.
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
> Dependencies:
>
>   - BIOS enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>   - THC0 and THC1 blocker counters increment only when their LTR is below the threshold.
>   - Blocker counters stop incrementing when LTR is above the threshold.
>   - Blocker counters respond to threshold changes.
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the passing criteria above.
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>   - Verify that the S0ix residency stalls when any IP transmits LTR below threshold and LTR blocker counters properly increment.
>   - Confirm that S0ix residency variables increment and LTR blocker counters stop incrementing when all LTR are above threshold.
>   - Ensure each register/control bit operation reflects in observable system (such as counter behaviour).
>   - Counter increments, S0ix block/allow, telemetry logs should be consistent across all platforms, program versions, IP variants, and test cycles.
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver

### ✅ [TC] [S0ix_LPM] THC1 PG Blocker/Breaker Counters on PMC [PM Telemetry#8, 14]

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **ID** | 13013458302 |
| **Status** | complete |
| **Subject** | test_case |
| **Owner** | willychi |
| **Level** | L3 (TC) |

**Description:**

> Test Description:
>
>
>
>   - The objective
>       of this test is to verify that the PMC correctly increments the PG
>       blocker/breaker counters for THC0 and/or THC1 as expected, when THC0/THC1
>       is active state (not idle, or D0i2) due to ongoing touch input
>       interrupt/frames to THC controller.
>
>   - Also to ensure
>       the PG blocker/breaker counters for THC0 and/or THC1 will stop
>       incrementing once THC0/THC1 entered idle state (or D0i2) with
>       NO ongoing touch input interrupt/frames to THC
>       controller.  
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Pre-Test Configuration:
>
>
>
>   - THC1 port will required to be enabled in BIOS settings.
>
>   - RVP booted to BIOS Menu, go to Intel Advance Menu ->
>       PCH-IO Configuration -> THC Configuration ->   <Refer to
>       the BKM settings below to enable THC1 port.>
>
>   - THC1 enabled as HIDSPI interface at 17MHz while THC1
>       enabled as HIDI2C interface at 100kHz (Standard Speed Mode) 
>
>   - Go to Intel Advanced Menu -> PCH-IO
>       Configuration-> Serial-IO Configuration -> Disabled both I2C4 and
>       I2C5 
>
>   - Intel Advanced Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration-> PCH
>       Energy Reporting -> Enabled
>
>   - Intel Advanced Menu -> PCH-IO Configuration->
>       Energy Reporting Debug Mode -> Enabled
>
>   - To ensure the system goes into S0ix state, go to Intel
>       Advance Menu -> ACPI Configuration -> Enabled Low Power Idle
>       Capability
>
>   - Enable Virtual KB (Note: To make sure the touch
>       functionality is working)
>
>   - Note: The BKM below are captured from NVL RVP
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Steps / Procedure:
>
>
>
>   - RVP successfully boot to Ubuntu
>       OS with all the HW/SW requirements met, follow and configure all
>       the BIOS settings accordingly as described in 'Pre-Test Configuration'
>       section above.
>
>   - Ensure
>       the system enable and support S0ix + PKGC-10 with stable PM recipe/BKC. (PMC Telemetry feature should be enabled and
>       checkout by FV-PM Team at this point of time.)
>
>   - Read and
>       record down the initial values of THC0/THC1 PG blocker/breaker counters
>       on PMC registers, and also PWRST power state on THC0/THC1 registers.
>
>
>
> To dump all PWRST status register
>  for THC0 and THC1 instances:
>
>
>
>
> To dump all D3 and PG related
>  counter registers for both THC0 and THC1 instances on PMC side:
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Configure the
>       blocker counter mode by setting the inc_method to 1 and the
>       lpm_blk_cntr_pol to 1.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.inc_method
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.lpm_blk_cntr_pol
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_blocker_counter_control.lpm_blk_cntr_en
>  = 1
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Verify energy reporting is enabled.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.pm_cfg.er_en
>  = 1
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.etr3.er_debug_en
>  = 1
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Read all the relevant counters when THC1 is in D0.
>       Observe the block counter is incrementing when touch activity is
>       triggered.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> thc.registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=1,
>  keyword_list=["fg_pr","pg"], duplicates_enable=True)
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Adjust the D0i2 entry timer to observe counter
>       sensitivity. The blocker counter growth rate depends on the D0i2 entry
>       timing.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.thc0.port.mem.thc_m_prt_d0i2_et.d0i2_entry_timer
>  = 0b1000
>
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Trigger non-blocking condition by stop touching the
>       device and wait for THC1 to enter D0i2. 
>
>   - Read again
>       the relevant counters when THC1 is in D0i2. Observe the blocker counter
>       should stop/slow down (THC0 no longer blocking PG), the breaker counter
>       should start incrementing (THC1 breaking PG blockage by entering D0i2).
>
>
>
>
>
>
> pcd.pmc.mmr.lpm_breaker_counter_control.inc_s0ix_brk_cntrs.write(1);
>
>
> thc_registers.ThcStatus().read_thc_telemetry(thc_port=1,
>  keyword_list=["fg_pr", "pg"], duplicates_enable=True);
>
>
>
>
>
>   - Clear the counters and repeat steps #6 to #9
>       back-to-back multiple times to ensure these PMC counters behaviour are
>       consistent and behaved as expected.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> SW Requirements:
>
>
>
>   - RVP/SUT
>       booted to Ubuntu OS.
>
>   - Ubuntu OS is with built-in
>       HID-SPI/I2C kernel driver.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> HW Requirements:
>
>
>
>   - RVP
>       booted to Ubuntu OS with feature rework to enable SPI/I2C interface on
>       THC0/THC1 port.   
>
>   - 3PE POR touch panel (i.e.
>       BOM52, BOM36/37) with touch cable connected on RVP via the THC0/THC1
>       touch port/connector.
>
>   - PythonSV with USB2DBC debug
>       cable connected from SUT/RVP to host system.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Dependencies:
>
>
>
>   - BIOS
>       enabling and settings for HID-SPI/I2C on THC0 and/or THC1 ports.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Pass Criteria:
>
>
>
>   - Verify that the PMC
>       increments the PG blocker/breaker counters for THC0/THC1 when THC0/THC1
>       is in an active state (not idle or D0i2) as a result of ongoing touch
>       input interrupt/frames to the THC controller.
>
>   - Confirm that the PG
>       blocker/breaker counters for THC0/THC1 stop incrementing once THC0/THC1
>       enters the idle state (or D0i2) with no ongoing touch input
>       interrupt/frames to the THC controller.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Test Fail Criteria:
>
>
>
>   - Unable to meet any of the
>       passing criteria above.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Intent Verify:
>
>
>
>   - Verify the PG blocker
>       counter increment steadily when THC is forced into D0 (active via touch
>       gestures), showing S0ix block due to touch activity.
>
>   - Ensure the counters stop
>       incrementing when THC enters D0i2 (idle, no touch) and the power state
>       transitions reflect expected idle behaviour.
>
>   - Each state change
>       (active/idle) should be mirrored in both the PWRST and blocker/breaker
>       counter values, confirming register logic.
>
>   - The counter behaviour must
>       remain consistent across repeated cycles and system instances.
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Ecosystem SW Requirements:
>
>  Below is the example from NVL-Hx, please refer to Wiki for different project: THC Device Enablement on NVL-HX RVP1/RVP4 - Validation and Debug Encyclopedia - Intel Enterprise Wiki
>
>   - BIOS version:
>   - ifwi_nvl_hx_a1a0_pp_release_2025ww50.1.01/ or later version
>
>   - OS kernel:
>   - Window OS
>
> NVL-HX-CONS-25.48.2.287 or later version (NOTE: NON-POR, does not supported by driver team, intended solely for debugging/validation purposes)
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
> \\amr.corp.intel.com\ec\proj\sv\Debug\PowerOn\NVL_Hx\NVL_Hx_A1\OS_Image\Ubuntu\ubuntu-24.04.3-desktop-amd64.iso
>
>   - Driver versions:
>   - Windows OS
>
>  https://ubit-artifactory-or.intel.com/artifactory/one-windows-local/Ingredients/hidi2c_touch/ OR https://ubit-artifactory-ba.intel.com/artifactory/owr-repos/Submissions/quickspi/
>
>   - Ubuntu OS
>
>                              Built in QuickSPI/QuickI2C kernel driver
